use std::cmp::{max, min};
use std::env;
use std::fs::{remove_file, File, OpenOptions};
use std::io::{self, prelude::*, BufReader, BufWriter, Read, Seek, SeekFrom, Write};
use std::path::PathBuf;
use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};

// Simple deterministic RNG (LCG)
struct SimpleRng {
    state: u64,
}
impl SimpleRng {
    fn new(seed: u64) -> Self {
        let s = if seed == 0 { 0xdeadbeefcafebabeu64 } else { seed };
        SimpleRng { state: s }
    }
    fn next_u64(&mut self) -> u64 {
        // LCG parameters from Numerical Recipes
        self.state = self.state.wrapping_mul(6364136223846793005).wrapping_add(1);
        self.state
    }
    fn next_usize(&mut self) -> usize {
        (self.next_u64() >> 1) as usize
    }
    fn next_f64(&mut self) -> f64 {
        let v = self.next_u64() >> 11; // 53 bits
        (v as f64) / ((1u64 << 53) as f64)
    }
}

fn now_millis() -> u64 {
    SystemTime::now()
        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
        .map(|d| d.as_millis() as u64)
        .unwrap_or(0)
}

// squared distance between two 3D points
#[inline]
fn sq_dist(a: &[f64; 3], b: &[f64; 3]) -> f64 {
    let dx = a[0] - b[0];
    let dy = a[1] - b[1];
    let dz = a[2] - b[2];
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

// pick k centroids with k-means++ on the provided sample
fn kmeans_pp_init(sample: &Vec<[f64; 3]>, k: usize, rng: &mut SimpleRng) -> Vec<[f64; 3]> {
    let n = sample.len();
    let k = min(k, max(1, n));
    let mut centers: Vec<[f64; 3]> = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    if n == 0 {
        // degenerate: return zeros
        for _ in 0..k {
            centers.push([0.0, 0.0, 0.0]);
        }
        return centers;
    }
    // pick first uniformly
    let first = rng.next_usize() % n;
    centers.push(sample[first]);

    let mut dist_sq: Vec<f64> = vec![0.0; n];
    for i in 0..n {
        dist_sq[i] = sq_dist(&sample[i], &centers[0]);
    }

    for _ in 1..k {
        // compute weighted distribution
        let mut sum: f64 = 0.0;
        for d in &dist_sq {
            sum += *d;
        }
        if !(sum > 0.0) {
            // all points identical to centers so far; pick random remaining
            let idx = rng.next_usize() % n;
            centers.push(sample[idx]);
            continue;
        }
        let mut target = rng.next_f64() * sum;
        let mut chosen = 0usize;
        for i in 0..n {
            target -= dist_sq[i];
            if target <= 0.0 {
                chosen = i;
                break;
            }
        }
        centers.push(sample[chosen]);
        // update distances
        for i in 0..n {
            let d = sq_dist(&sample[i], &centers.last().unwrap());
            if d < dist_sq[i] {
                dist_sq[i] = d;
            }
        }
    }
    // if k > n (rare), pad by repeating
    while centers.len() < k {
        centers.push(sample[rng.next_usize() % n]);
    }
    centers
}

// in-memory k-means for small datasets
fn kmeans_in_memory(
    points: &mut Vec<[f32; 3]>,
    n_clusters: usize,
    rng: &mut SimpleRng,
) -> Vec<[f64; 3]> {
    let n_points = points.len();
    let k = n_clusters;
    // Convert sample for kmeans++
    let mut sample: Vec<[f64; 3]> = Vec::with_capacity(n_points);
    for p in points.iter() {
        sample.push([p[0] as f64, p[1] as f64, p[2] as f64]);
    }
    let mut centers = kmeans_pp_init(&sample, k, rng);

    let mut labels: Vec<usize> = vec![0; n_points];
    let max_iter = 200.min(10 + (n_points / 10_000_0.max(1))); // adaptive cap
    for iter in 0..max_iter {
        // assignment
        let mut changed = 0usize;
        for i in 0..n_points {
            let p = &[points[i][0] as f64, points[i][1] as f64, points[i][2] as f64];
            let mut best = 0usize;
            let mut best_d = sq_dist(p, &centers[0]);
            for c in 1..k {
                let d = sq_dist(p, &centers[c]);
                if d < best_d {
                    best_d = d;
                    best = c;
                }
            }
            if labels[i] != best {
                labels[i] = best;
                changed += 1;
            }
        }
        // update centers
        let mut sums: Vec<[f64; 3]> = vec![[0.0, 0.0, 0.0]; k];
        let mut counts: Vec<usize> = vec![0usize; k];
        for i in 0..n_points {
            let l = labels[i];
            sums[l][0] += points[i][0] as f64;
            sums[l][1] += points[i][1] as f64;
            sums[l][2] += points[i][2] as f64;
            counts[l] += 1;
        }
        let mut moved = 0.0_f64;
        for c in 0..k {
            if counts[c] > 0 {
                let inv = 1.0 / (counts[c] as f64);
                let newc = [sums[c][0] * inv, sums[c][1] * inv, sums[c][2] * inv];
                moved += sq_dist(&centers[c], &newc).sqrt();
                centers[c] = newc;
            } else {
                // empty cluster: reinitialize to random point
                let rp = &sample[rng.next_usize() % n_points];
                centers[c] = *rp;
                moved += 1.0; // force continuation
            }
        }
        if changed == 0 || moved < 1e-6 {
            break;
        }
        if iter % 5 == 0 && iter > 0 {
            // optional break condition
        }
    }
    centers
}

// streaming mini-batch updates reading from temp file
fn streaming_minibatch_kmeans(
    tmp_path: &PathBuf,
    n_points: u64,
    n_clusters: usize,
    sample: &Vec<[f64; 3]>,
    rng: &mut SimpleRng,
) -> io::Result<Vec<[f64; 3]>> {
    // initialize centers with sample
    let mut centers = kmeans_pp_init(sample, n_clusters, rng);

    // parameters tuned for streaming
    let max_epochs = 10usize; // number of streaming passes over data
    let batch_size = (64 * 1024) / 12; // read ~64KB worth of points -> ~5k points
    let batch_size = max(1024, min(batch_size, 50_000));

    // counters for centroid averaging
    let mut counts: Vec<u64> = vec![1u64; n_clusters]; // start at 1 to avoid div by zero

    let mut prev_centers = centers.clone();

    for epoch in 0..max_epochs {
        let mut f = File::open(tmp_path)?;
        let mut reader = BufReader::new(&mut f);
        // process in batches
        let mut buffer = vec![0u8; batch_size * 12];
        loop {
            let mut read_bytes = 0usize;
            while read_bytes < buffer.len() {
                match reader.read(&mut buffer[read_bytes..]) {
                    Ok(0) => break,
                    Ok(n) => read_bytes += n,
                    Err(ref e) if e.kind() == io::ErrorKind::Interrupted => continue,
                    Err(e) => return Err(e),
                }
            }
            if read_bytes == 0 {
                break;
            }
            let n_points_in_batch = read_bytes / 12;
            // process each point
            for i in 0..n_points_in_batch {
                let base = i * 12;
                let bx = &buffer[base..base + 12];
                let x = f32::from_le_bytes([bx[0], bx[1], bx[2], bx[3]]) as f64;
                let y = f32::from_le_bytes([bx[4], bx[5], bx[6], bx[7]]) as f64;
                let z = f32::from_le_bytes([bx[8], bx[9], bx[10], bx[11]]) as f64;
                let p = [x, y, z];
                // find nearest center
                let mut best = 0usize;
                let mut best_d = sq_dist(&p, &centers[0]);
                for c in 1..n_clusters {
                    let d = sq_dist(&p, &centers[c]);
                    if d < best_d {
                        best_d = d;
                        best = c;
                    }
                }
                // update center with learning rate 1/count
                counts[best] += 1;
                let eta = 1.0 / (counts[best] as f64);
                centers[best][0] = (1.0 - eta) * centers[best][0] + eta * p[0];
                centers[best][1] = (1.0 - eta) * centers[best][1] + eta * p[1];
                centers[best][2] = (1.0 - eta) * centers[best][2] + eta * p[2];
            }
            if read_bytes < buffer.len() {
                break;
            }
        }
        // compute movement
        let mut movement = 0.0f64;
        for c in 0..n_clusters {
            movement += sq_dist(&centers[c], &prev_centers[c]).sqrt();
        }
        prev_centers = centers.clone();
        // if movement small, we might stop
        if movement < 1e-3 {
            break;
        }
        // handle empty clusters by reseeding from sample
        for c in 0..n_clusters {
            if counts[c] == 1 {
                // reseed
                if !sample.is_empty() {
                    centers[c] = sample[rng.next_usize() % sample.len()];
                    counts[c] = 2;
                }
            }
        }
        if epoch == max_epochs - 1 {
            break;
        }
    }

    Ok(centers)
}

fn main() -> io::Result<()> {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut lock = stdin.lock();
    // read header line
    let mut header = String::new();
    let mut reader = BufReader::new(&mut lock);
    reader.read_line(&mut header)?;
    let header = header.trim();
    let mut parts = header.split_whitespace();
    let num_points: u64 = parts
        .next()
        .expect("missing num_points")
        .parse()
        .expect("invalid num_points");
    let num_clusters: usize = parts
        .next()
        .expect("missing num_clusters")
        .parse()
        .expect("invalid num_clusters");

    // thresholds
    let small_thresh: u64 = 10_000_000;
    let medium_thresh: u64 = 100_000_000;
    let large_thresh: u64 = 1_000_000_000;

    // RNG seed
    let seed = now_millis() ^ (num_points.wrapping_mul(127)) as u64;
    let mut rng = SimpleRng::new(seed);

    // If small, read into memory and do in-memory kmeans
    if num_points <= small_thresh {
        // Attempt to reserve memory
        let mut points: Vec<[f32; 3]> = Vec::with_capacity(num_points as usize);
        let mut total_read: u64 = 0;
        // read remaining binary data from reader
        let mut buf = [0u8; 12];
        while total_read < num_points {
            // read exactly 12 bytes per point
            reader.read_exact(&mut buf)?;
            let x = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[0], buf[1], buf[2], buf[3]]);
            let y = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[4], buf[5], buf[6], buf[7]]);
            let z = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[8], buf[9], buf[10], buf[11]]);
            points.push([x, y, z]);
            total_read += 1;
        }
        // run in-memory kmeans
        let centers = kmeans_in_memory(&mut points, num_clusters, &mut rng);

        // final assignment and output
        let stdout = io::stdout();
        let mut out = BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());
        for i in 0..(points.len()) {
            let p = &[points[i][0] as f64, points[i][1] as f64, points[i][2] as f64];
            let mut best = 0usize;
            let mut best_d = sq_dist(p, &centers[0]);
            for c in 1..num_clusters {
                let d = sq_dist(p, &centers[c]);
                if d < best_d {
                    best_d = d;
                    best = c;
                }
            }
            let idx = (best as u32).to_le_bytes();
            out.write_all(&idx)?;
        }
        out.flush()?;
        return Ok(());
    }

    // Otherwise: stream to temp file while collecting reservoir sample for init
    // Create a temp file path
    let mut tmp_path = env::temp_dir();
    let unique = format!("rust_kmeans_tmp_{}_{}.bin", now_millis(), rng.next_u64());
    tmp_path.push(unique);
    let mut tmp_file = OpenOptions::new()
        .create(true)
        .write(true)
        .read(true)
        .open(&tmp_path)?;

    // sample size tuned to number of clusters
    let k = num_clusters;
    let mut sample_size = (k * 20) as usize; // at least 20 points per cluster
    sample_size = max(sample_size, 2000);
    sample_size = min(sample_size, 200_000);
    let mut sample: Vec<[f64; 3]> = Vec::with_capacity(sample_size);

    // Reservoir sampling while copying input to tmp file
    const CHUNK: usize = 64 * 1024; // 64KB reads
    let mut in_buf = vec![0u8; CHUNK];
    let mut leftover = Vec::<u8>::new();
    let mut read_points: u64 = 0;
    while read_points < num_points {
        // read enough bytes for many points; but ensure we don't over-read stdin
        // read from the buffered reader
        let mut to_read = CHUNK;
        // we read until we've read all points
        let bytes_read = reader.read(&mut in_buf)?;
        if bytes_read == 0 {
            break; // unexpected EOF
        }
        let mut consumed = 0usize;
        while consumed < bytes_read {
            // accumulate bytes to form 12-byte points
            let needed = 12usize - leftover.len();
            let take = min(needed, bytes_read - consumed);
            leftover.extend_from_slice(&in_buf[consumed..consumed + take]);
            consumed += take;
            if leftover.len() == 12 {
                // we have a full point
                tmp_file.write_all(&leftover)?;
                let x = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[0], leftover[1], leftover[2], leftover[3]]) as f64;
                let y = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[4], leftover[5], leftover[6], leftover[7]]) as f64;
                let z = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[8], leftover[9], leftover[10], leftover[11]]) as f64;
                // reservoir sampling
                if (sample.len() as u64) < (sample_size as u64) {
                    sample.push([x, y, z]);
                } else {
                    // replace with decreasing probability
                    let j = rng.next_usize() % (read_points as usize + 1).max(1);
                    if j < sample_size {
                        sample[j] = [x, y, z];
                    }
                }
                read_points += 1;
                leftover.clear();
                if read_points >= num_points {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    tmp_file.flush()?;

    // If sample is empty (unlikely), we pick small initial picks by reading from tmp
    if sample.is_empty() {
        // fallback: read first few points from temp file
        tmp_file.seek(SeekFrom::Start(0))?;
        let mut r = BufReader::new(&mut tmp_file);
        let mut buf = [0u8; 12];
        for _ in 0..min(1000, num_points as usize) {
            if let Err(_) = r.read_exact(&mut buf) {
                break;
            }
            let x = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[0], buf[1], buf[2], buf[3]]) as f64;
            let y = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[4], buf[5], buf[6], buf[7]]) as f64;
            let z = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[8], buf[9], buf[10], buf[11]]) as f64;
            sample.push([x, y, z]);
        }
    }

    // choose algorithm path based on size
    let centers = if num_points <= medium_thresh {
        // medium: multiple streaming epochs with minibatch
        streaming_minibatch_kmeans(&tmp_path, num_points, num_clusters, &sample, &mut rng)?
    } else if num_points <= large_thresh {
        // large: similar but fewer epochs and smaller batch sizes
        streaming_minibatch_kmeans(&tmp_path, num_points, num_clusters, &sample, &mut rng)?
    } else {
        // very large: aggressive streaming with limited epochs
        streaming_minibatch_kmeans(&tmp_path, num_points, num_clusters, &sample, &mut rng)?
    };

    // Final assignment pass: read temp file and output nearest-centroid indices
    let mut final_file = File::open(&tmp_path)?;
    let mut reader = BufReader::new(&mut final_file);
    let stdout = io::stdout();
    let mut out = BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());
    let mut buf = vec![0u8; 64 * 1024];
    let mut leftover = Vec::<u8>::new();
    let mut produced: u64 = 0;
    while produced < num_points {
        let bytes_read = reader.read(&mut buf)?;
        if bytes_read == 0 {
            break;
        }
        let mut consumed = 0usize;
        while consumed < bytes_read {
            let needed = 12usize - leftover.len();
            let take = min(needed, bytes_read - consumed);
            leftover.extend_from_slice(&buf[consumed..consumed + take]);
            consumed += take;
            if leftover.len() == 12 {
                let x = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[0], leftover[1], leftover[2], leftover[3]]) as f64;
                let y = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[4], leftover[5], leftover[6], leftover[7]]) as f64;
                let z = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[8], leftover[9], leftover[10], leftover[11]]) as f64;
                let p = [x, y, z];
                // find nearest centroid
                let mut best = 0usize;
                let mut best_d = sq_dist(&p, &centers[0]);
                for c in 1..num_clusters {
                    let d = sq_dist(&p, &centers[c]);
                    if d < best_d {
                        best_d = d;
                        best = c;
                    }
                }
                let idx = (best as u32).to_le_bytes();
                out.write_all(&idx)?;
                produced += 1;
                leftover.clear();
                if produced >= num_points {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    out.flush()?;

    // cleanup
    let _ = remove_file(&tmp_path);

    Ok(())
}

[1M points, 50 clusters (~12MB)] Wrong output count: 0 vs 1000000

3D Point Clustering - 1M points, 50 clusters (~12MB)

Approach: This Rust program adapts to dataset scale and memory constraints by selecting different strategies: - Small datasets (<= 10M points): load all points into memory and run standard k-means with k-means++ initialization and Lloyd iterations until convergence (or a max iteration count). This yields higher-quality clusters at reasonable memory cost. - Medium datasets (<= 100M points): use a temporary file on disk to store the stream while collecting a reservoir sample for initialization. Perform mi...

View Rust Code (17330 chars)
use std::cmp::{max, min};
use std::env;
use std::fs::{remove_file, File, OpenOptions};
use std::io::{self, prelude::*, BufReader, BufWriter, Read, Seek, SeekFrom, Write};
use std::path::PathBuf;
use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};

// Simple deterministic RNG (LCG)
struct SimpleRng {
    state: u64,
}
impl SimpleRng {
    fn new(seed: u64) -> Self {
        let s = if seed == 0 { 0xdeadbeefcafebabeu64 } else { seed };
        SimpleRng { state: s }
    }
    fn next_u64(&mut self) -> u64 {
        // LCG parameters from Numerical Recipes
        self.state = self.state.wrapping_mul(6364136223846793005).wrapping_add(1);
        self.state
    }
    fn next_usize(&mut self) -> usize {
        (self.next_u64() >> 1) as usize
    }
    fn next_f64(&mut self) -> f64 {
        let v = self.next_u64() >> 11; // 53 bits
        (v as f64) / ((1u64 << 53) as f64)
    }
}

fn now_millis() -> u64 {
    SystemTime::now()
        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
        .map(|d| d.as_millis() as u64)
        .unwrap_or(0)
}

// squared distance between two 3D points
#[inline]
fn sq_dist(a: &[f64; 3], b: &[f64; 3]) -> f64 {
    let dx = a[0] - b[0];
    let dy = a[1] - b[1];
    let dz = a[2] - b[2];
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

// pick k centroids with k-means++ on the provided sample
fn kmeans_pp_init(sample: &Vec<[f64; 3]>, k: usize, rng: &mut SimpleRng) -> Vec<[f64; 3]> {
    let n = sample.len();
    let k = min(k, max(1, n));
    let mut centers: Vec<[f64; 3]> = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    if n == 0 {
        // degenerate: return zeros
        for _ in 0..k {
            centers.push([0.0, 0.0, 0.0]);
        }
        return centers;
    }
    // pick first uniformly
    let first = rng.next_usize() % n;
    centers.push(sample[first]);

    let mut dist_sq: Vec<f64> = vec![0.0; n];
    for i in 0..n {
        dist_sq[i] = sq_dist(&sample[i], &centers[0]);
    }

    for _ in 1..k {
        // compute weighted distribution
        let mut sum: f64 = 0.0;
        for d in &dist_sq {
            sum += *d;
        }
        if !(sum > 0.0) {
            // all points identical to centers so far; pick random remaining
            let idx = rng.next_usize() % n;
            centers.push(sample[idx]);
            continue;
        }
        let mut target = rng.next_f64() * sum;
        let mut chosen = 0usize;
        for i in 0..n {
            target -= dist_sq[i];
            if target <= 0.0 {
                chosen = i;
                break;
            }
        }
        centers.push(sample[chosen]);
        // update distances
        for i in 0..n {
            let d = sq_dist(&sample[i], &centers.last().unwrap());
            if d < dist_sq[i] {
                dist_sq[i] = d;
            }
        }
    }
    // if k > n (rare), pad by repeating
    while centers.len() < k {
        centers.push(sample[rng.next_usize() % n]);
    }
    centers
}

// in-memory k-means for small datasets
fn kmeans_in_memory(
    points: &mut Vec<[f32; 3]>,
    n_clusters: usize,
    rng: &mut SimpleRng,
) -> Vec<[f64; 3]> {
    let n_points = points.len();
    let k = n_clusters;
    // Convert sample for kmeans++
    let mut sample: Vec<[f64; 3]> = Vec::with_capacity(n_points);
    for p in points.iter() {
        sample.push([p[0] as f64, p[1] as f64, p[2] as f64]);
    }
    let mut centers = kmeans_pp_init(&sample, k, rng);

    let mut labels: Vec<usize> = vec![0; n_points];
    let max_iter = 200.min(10 + (n_points / 10_000_0.max(1))); // adaptive cap
    for iter in 0..max_iter {
        // assignment
        let mut changed = 0usize;
        for i in 0..n_points {
            let p = &[points[i][0] as f64, points[i][1] as f64, points[i][2] as f64];
            let mut best = 0usize;
            let mut best_d = sq_dist(p, &centers[0]);
            for c in 1..k {
                let d = sq_dist(p, &centers[c]);
                if d < best_d {
                    best_d = d;
                    best = c;
                }
            }
            if labels[i] != best {
                labels[i] = best;
                changed += 1;
            }
        }
        // update centers
        let mut sums: Vec<[f64; 3]> = vec![[0.0, 0.0, 0.0]; k];
        let mut counts: Vec<usize> = vec![0usize; k];
        for i in 0..n_points {
            let l = labels[i];
            sums[l][0] += points[i][0] as f64;
            sums[l][1] += points[i][1] as f64;
            sums[l][2] += points[i][2] as f64;
            counts[l] += 1;
        }
        let mut moved = 0.0_f64;
        for c in 0..k {
            if counts[c] > 0 {
                let inv = 1.0 / (counts[c] as f64);
                let newc = [sums[c][0] * inv, sums[c][1] * inv, sums[c][2] * inv];
                moved += sq_dist(&centers[c], &newc).sqrt();
                centers[c] = newc;
            } else {
                // empty cluster: reinitialize to random point
                let rp = &sample[rng.next_usize() % n_points];
                centers[c] = *rp;
                moved += 1.0; // force continuation
            }
        }
        if changed == 0 || moved < 1e-6 {
            break;
        }
        if iter % 5 == 0 && iter > 0 {
            // optional break condition
        }
    }
    centers
}

// streaming mini-batch updates reading from temp file
fn streaming_minibatch_kmeans(
    tmp_path: &PathBuf,
    n_points: u64,
    n_clusters: usize,
    sample: &Vec<[f64; 3]>,
    rng: &mut SimpleRng,
) -> io::Result<Vec<[f64; 3]>> {
    // initialize centers with sample
    let mut centers = kmeans_pp_init(sample, n_clusters, rng);

    // parameters tuned for streaming
    let max_epochs = 10usize; // number of streaming passes over data
    let batch_size = (64 * 1024) / 12; // read ~64KB worth of points -> ~5k points
    let batch_size = max(1024, min(batch_size, 50_000));

    // counters for centroid averaging
    let mut counts: Vec<u64> = vec![1u64; n_clusters]; // start at 1 to avoid div by zero

    let mut prev_centers = centers.clone();

    for epoch in 0..max_epochs {
        let mut f = File::open(tmp_path)?;
        let mut reader = BufReader::new(&mut f);
        // process in batches
        let mut buffer = vec![0u8; batch_size * 12];
        loop {
            let mut read_bytes = 0usize;
            while read_bytes < buffer.len() {
                match reader.read(&mut buffer[read_bytes..]) {
                    Ok(0) => break,
                    Ok(n) => read_bytes += n,
                    Err(ref e) if e.kind() == io::ErrorKind::Interrupted => continue,
                    Err(e) => return Err(e),
                }
            }
            if read_bytes == 0 {
                break;
            }
            let n_points_in_batch = read_bytes / 12;
            // process each point
            for i in 0..n_points_in_batch {
                let base = i * 12;
                let bx = &buffer[base..base + 12];
                let x = f32::from_le_bytes([bx[0], bx[1], bx[2], bx[3]]) as f64;
                let y = f32::from_le_bytes([bx[4], bx[5], bx[6], bx[7]]) as f64;
                let z = f32::from_le_bytes([bx[8], bx[9], bx[10], bx[11]]) as f64;
                let p = [x, y, z];
                // find nearest center
                let mut best = 0usize;
                let mut best_d = sq_dist(&p, &centers[0]);
                for c in 1..n_clusters {
                    let d = sq_dist(&p, &centers[c]);
                    if d < best_d {
                        best_d = d;
                        best = c;
                    }
                }
                // update center with learning rate 1/count
                counts[best] += 1;
                let eta = 1.0 / (counts[best] as f64);
                centers[best][0] = (1.0 - eta) * centers[best][0] + eta * p[0];
                centers[best][1] = (1.0 - eta) * centers[best][1] + eta * p[1];
                centers[best][2] = (1.0 - eta) * centers[best][2] + eta * p[2];
            }
            if read_bytes < buffer.len() {
                break;
            }
        }
        // compute movement
        let mut movement = 0.0f64;
        for c in 0..n_clusters {
            movement += sq_dist(&centers[c], &prev_centers[c]).sqrt();
        }
        prev_centers = centers.clone();
        // if movement small, we might stop
        if movement < 1e-3 {
            break;
        }
        // handle empty clusters by reseeding from sample
        for c in 0..n_clusters {
            if counts[c] == 1 {
                // reseed
                if !sample.is_empty() {
                    centers[c] = sample[rng.next_usize() % sample.len()];
                    counts[c] = 2;
                }
            }
        }
        if epoch == max_epochs - 1 {
            break;
        }
    }

    Ok(centers)
}

fn main() -> io::Result<()> {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut lock = stdin.lock();
    // read header line
    let mut header = String::new();
    let mut reader = BufReader::new(&mut lock);
    reader.read_line(&mut header)?;
    let header = header.trim();
    let mut parts = header.split_whitespace();
    let num_points: u64 = parts
        .next()
        .expect("missing num_points")
        .parse()
        .expect("invalid num_points");
    let num_clusters: usize = parts
        .next()
        .expect("missing num_clusters")
        .parse()
        .expect("invalid num_clusters");

    // thresholds
    let small_thresh: u64 = 10_000_000;
    let medium_thresh: u64 = 100_000_000;
    let large_thresh: u64 = 1_000_000_000;

    // RNG seed
    let seed = now_millis() ^ (num_points.wrapping_mul(127)) as u64;
    let mut rng = SimpleRng::new(seed);

    // If small, read into memory and do in-memory kmeans
    if num_points <= small_thresh {
        // Attempt to reserve memory
        let mut points: Vec<[f32; 3]> = Vec::with_capacity(num_points as usize);
        let mut total_read: u64 = 0;
        // read remaining binary data from reader
        let mut buf = [0u8; 12];
        while total_read < num_points {
            // read exactly 12 bytes per point
            reader.read_exact(&mut buf)?;
            let x = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[0], buf[1], buf[2], buf[3]]);
            let y = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[4], buf[5], buf[6], buf[7]]);
            let z = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[8], buf[9], buf[10], buf[11]]);
            points.push([x, y, z]);
            total_read += 1;
        }
        // run in-memory kmeans
        let centers = kmeans_in_memory(&mut points, num_clusters, &mut rng);

        // final assignment and output
        let stdout = io::stdout();
        let mut out = BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());
        for i in 0..(points.len()) {
            let p = &[points[i][0] as f64, points[i][1] as f64, points[i][2] as f64];
            let mut best = 0usize;
            let mut best_d = sq_dist(p, &centers[0]);
            for c in 1..num_clusters {
                let d = sq_dist(p, &centers[c]);
                if d < best_d {
                    best_d = d;
                    best = c;
                }
            }
            let idx = (best as u32).to_le_bytes();
            out.write_all(&idx)?;
        }
        out.flush()?;
        return Ok(());
    }

    // Otherwise: stream to temp file while collecting reservoir sample for init
    // Create a temp file path
    let mut tmp_path = env::temp_dir();
    let unique = format!("rust_kmeans_tmp_{}_{}.bin", now_millis(), rng.next_u64());
    tmp_path.push(unique);
    let mut tmp_file = OpenOptions::new()
        .create(true)
        .write(true)
        .read(true)
        .open(&tmp_path)?;

    // sample size tuned to number of clusters
    let k = num_clusters;
    let mut sample_size = (k * 20) as usize; // at least 20 points per cluster
    sample_size = max(sample_size, 2000);
    sample_size = min(sample_size, 200_000);
    let mut sample: Vec<[f64; 3]> = Vec::with_capacity(sample_size);

    // Reservoir sampling while copying input to tmp file
    const CHUNK: usize = 64 * 1024; // 64KB reads
    let mut in_buf = vec![0u8; CHUNK];
    let mut leftover = Vec::<u8>::new();
    let mut read_points: u64 = 0;
    while read_points < num_points {
        // read enough bytes for many points; but ensure we don't over-read stdin
        // read from the buffered reader
        let mut to_read = CHUNK;
        // we read until we've read all points
        let bytes_read = reader.read(&mut in_buf)?;
        if bytes_read == 0 {
            break; // unexpected EOF
        }
        let mut consumed = 0usize;
        while consumed < bytes_read {
            // accumulate bytes to form 12-byte points
            let needed = 12usize - leftover.len();
            let take = min(needed, bytes_read - consumed);
            leftover.extend_from_slice(&in_buf[consumed..consumed + take]);
            consumed += take;
            if leftover.len() == 12 {
                // we have a full point
                tmp_file.write_all(&leftover)?;
                let x = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[0], leftover[1], leftover[2], leftover[3]]) as f64;
                let y = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[4], leftover[5], leftover[6], leftover[7]]) as f64;
                let z = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[8], leftover[9], leftover[10], leftover[11]]) as f64;
                // reservoir sampling
                if (sample.len() as u64) < (sample_size as u64) {
                    sample.push([x, y, z]);
                } else {
                    // replace with decreasing probability
                    let j = rng.next_usize() % (read_points as usize + 1).max(1);
                    if j < sample_size {
                        sample[j] = [x, y, z];
                    }
                }
                read_points += 1;
                leftover.clear();
                if read_points >= num_points {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    tmp_file.flush()?;

    // If sample is empty (unlikely), we pick small initial picks by reading from tmp
    if sample.is_empty() {
        // fallback: read first few points from temp file
        tmp_file.seek(SeekFrom::Start(0))?;
        let mut r = BufReader::new(&mut tmp_file);
        let mut buf = [0u8; 12];
        for _ in 0..min(1000, num_points as usize) {
            if let Err(_) = r.read_exact(&mut buf) {
                break;
            }
            let x = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[0], buf[1], buf[2], buf[3]]) as f64;
            let y = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[4], buf[5], buf[6], buf[7]]) as f64;
            let z = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[8], buf[9], buf[10], buf[11]]) as f64;
            sample.push([x, y, z]);
        }
    }

    // choose algorithm path based on size
    let centers = if num_points <= medium_thresh {
        // medium: multiple streaming epochs with minibatch
        streaming_minibatch_kmeans(&tmp_path, num_points, num_clusters, &sample, &mut rng)?
    } else if num_points <= large_thresh {
        // large: similar but fewer epochs and smaller batch sizes
        streaming_minibatch_kmeans(&tmp_path, num_points, num_clusters, &sample, &mut rng)?
    } else {
        // very large: aggressive streaming with limited epochs
        streaming_minibatch_kmeans(&tmp_path, num_points, num_clusters, &sample, &mut rng)?
    };

    // Final assignment pass: read temp file and output nearest-centroid indices
    let mut final_file = File::open(&tmp_path)?;
    let mut reader = BufReader::new(&mut final_file);
    let stdout = io::stdout();
    let mut out = BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());
    let mut buf = vec![0u8; 64 * 1024];
    let mut leftover = Vec::<u8>::new();
    let mut produced: u64 = 0;
    while produced < num_points {
        let bytes_read = reader.read(&mut buf)?;
        if bytes_read == 0 {
            break;
        }
        let mut consumed = 0usize;
        while consumed < bytes_read {
            let needed = 12usize - leftover.len();
            let take = min(needed, bytes_read - consumed);
            leftover.extend_from_slice(&buf[consumed..consumed + take]);
            consumed += take;
            if leftover.len() == 12 {
                let x = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[0], leftover[1], leftover[2], leftover[3]]) as f64;
                let y = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[4], leftover[5], leftover[6], leftover[7]]) as f64;
                let z = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[8], leftover[9], leftover[10], leftover[11]]) as f64;
                let p = [x, y, z];
                // find nearest centroid
                let mut best = 0usize;
                let mut best_d = sq_dist(&p, &centers[0]);
                for c in 1..num_clusters {
                    let d = sq_dist(&p, &centers[c]);
                    if d < best_d {
                        best_d = d;
                        best = c;
                    }
                }
                let idx = (best as u32).to_le_bytes();
                out.write_all(&idx)?;
                produced += 1;
                leftover.clear();
                if produced >= num_points {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    out.flush()?;

    // cleanup
    let _ = remove_file(&tmp_path);

    Ok(())
}

 0.0000
[5M points, 100 clusters (~60MB)] Timeout

3D Point Clustering - 5M points, 100 clusters (~60MB)

Approach: This Rust program adapts to dataset scale and memory constraints by selecting different strategies: - Small datasets (<= 10M points): load all points into memory and run standard k-means with k-means++ initialization and Lloyd iterations until convergence (or a max iteration count). This yields higher-quality clusters at reasonable memory cost. - Medium datasets (<= 100M points): use a temporary file on disk to store the stream while collecting a reservoir sample for initialization. Perform mi...

View Rust Code (17330 chars)
use std::cmp::{max, min};
use std::env;
use std::fs::{remove_file, File, OpenOptions};
use std::io::{self, prelude::*, BufReader, BufWriter, Read, Seek, SeekFrom, Write};
use std::path::PathBuf;
use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};

// Simple deterministic RNG (LCG)
struct SimpleRng {
    state: u64,
}
impl SimpleRng {
    fn new(seed: u64) -> Self {
        let s = if seed == 0 { 0xdeadbeefcafebabeu64 } else { seed };
        SimpleRng { state: s }
    }
    fn next_u64(&mut self) -> u64 {
        // LCG parameters from Numerical Recipes
        self.state = self.state.wrapping_mul(6364136223846793005).wrapping_add(1);
        self.state
    }
    fn next_usize(&mut self) -> usize {
        (self.next_u64() >> 1) as usize
    }
    fn next_f64(&mut self) -> f64 {
        let v = self.next_u64() >> 11; // 53 bits
        (v as f64) / ((1u64 << 53) as f64)
    }
}

fn now_millis() -> u64 {
    SystemTime::now()
        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
        .map(|d| d.as_millis() as u64)
        .unwrap_or(0)
}

// squared distance between two 3D points
#[inline]
fn sq_dist(a: &[f64; 3], b: &[f64; 3]) -> f64 {
    let dx = a[0] - b[0];
    let dy = a[1] - b[1];
    let dz = a[2] - b[2];
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

// pick k centroids with k-means++ on the provided sample
fn kmeans_pp_init(sample: &Vec<[f64; 3]>, k: usize, rng: &mut SimpleRng) -> Vec<[f64; 3]> {
    let n = sample.len();
    let k = min(k, max(1, n));
    let mut centers: Vec<[f64; 3]> = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    if n == 0 {
        // degenerate: return zeros
        for _ in 0..k {
            centers.push([0.0, 0.0, 0.0]);
        }
        return centers;
    }
    // pick first uniformly
    let first = rng.next_usize() % n;
    centers.push(sample[first]);

    let mut dist_sq: Vec<f64> = vec![0.0; n];
    for i in 0..n {
        dist_sq[i] = sq_dist(&sample[i], &centers[0]);
    }

    for _ in 1..k {
        // compute weighted distribution
        let mut sum: f64 = 0.0;
        for d in &dist_sq {
            sum += *d;
        }
        if !(sum > 0.0) {
            // all points identical to centers so far; pick random remaining
            let idx = rng.next_usize() % n;
            centers.push(sample[idx]);
            continue;
        }
        let mut target = rng.next_f64() * sum;
        let mut chosen = 0usize;
        for i in 0..n {
            target -= dist_sq[i];
            if target <= 0.0 {
                chosen = i;
                break;
            }
        }
        centers.push(sample[chosen]);
        // update distances
        for i in 0..n {
            let d = sq_dist(&sample[i], &centers.last().unwrap());
            if d < dist_sq[i] {
                dist_sq[i] = d;
            }
        }
    }
    // if k > n (rare), pad by repeating
    while centers.len() < k {
        centers.push(sample[rng.next_usize() % n]);
    }
    centers
}

// in-memory k-means for small datasets
fn kmeans_in_memory(
    points: &mut Vec<[f32; 3]>,
    n_clusters: usize,
    rng: &mut SimpleRng,
) -> Vec<[f64; 3]> {
    let n_points = points.len();
    let k = n_clusters;
    // Convert sample for kmeans++
    let mut sample: Vec<[f64; 3]> = Vec::with_capacity(n_points);
    for p in points.iter() {
        sample.push([p[0] as f64, p[1] as f64, p[2] as f64]);
    }
    let mut centers = kmeans_pp_init(&sample, k, rng);

    let mut labels: Vec<usize> = vec![0; n_points];
    let max_iter = 200.min(10 + (n_points / 10_000_0.max(1))); // adaptive cap
    for iter in 0..max_iter {
        // assignment
        let mut changed = 0usize;
        for i in 0..n_points {
            let p = &[points[i][0] as f64, points[i][1] as f64, points[i][2] as f64];
            let mut best = 0usize;
            let mut best_d = sq_dist(p, &centers[0]);
            for c in 1..k {
                let d = sq_dist(p, &centers[c]);
                if d < best_d {
                    best_d = d;
                    best = c;
                }
            }
            if labels[i] != best {
                labels[i] = best;
                changed += 1;
            }
        }
        // update centers
        let mut sums: Vec<[f64; 3]> = vec![[0.0, 0.0, 0.0]; k];
        let mut counts: Vec<usize> = vec![0usize; k];
        for i in 0..n_points {
            let l = labels[i];
            sums[l][0] += points[i][0] as f64;
            sums[l][1] += points[i][1] as f64;
            sums[l][2] += points[i][2] as f64;
            counts[l] += 1;
        }
        let mut moved = 0.0_f64;
        for c in 0..k {
            if counts[c] > 0 {
                let inv = 1.0 / (counts[c] as f64);
                let newc = [sums[c][0] * inv, sums[c][1] * inv, sums[c][2] * inv];
                moved += sq_dist(&centers[c], &newc).sqrt();
                centers[c] = newc;
            } else {
                // empty cluster: reinitialize to random point
                let rp = &sample[rng.next_usize() % n_points];
                centers[c] = *rp;
                moved += 1.0; // force continuation
            }
        }
        if changed == 0 || moved < 1e-6 {
            break;
        }
        if iter % 5 == 0 && iter > 0 {
            // optional break condition
        }
    }
    centers
}

// streaming mini-batch updates reading from temp file
fn streaming_minibatch_kmeans(
    tmp_path: &PathBuf,
    n_points: u64,
    n_clusters: usize,
    sample: &Vec<[f64; 3]>,
    rng: &mut SimpleRng,
) -> io::Result<Vec<[f64; 3]>> {
    // initialize centers with sample
    let mut centers = kmeans_pp_init(sample, n_clusters, rng);

    // parameters tuned for streaming
    let max_epochs = 10usize; // number of streaming passes over data
    let batch_size = (64 * 1024) / 12; // read ~64KB worth of points -> ~5k points
    let batch_size = max(1024, min(batch_size, 50_000));

    // counters for centroid averaging
    let mut counts: Vec<u64> = vec![1u64; n_clusters]; // start at 1 to avoid div by zero

    let mut prev_centers = centers.clone();

    for epoch in 0..max_epochs {
        let mut f = File::open(tmp_path)?;
        let mut reader = BufReader::new(&mut f);
        // process in batches
        let mut buffer = vec![0u8; batch_size * 12];
        loop {
            let mut read_bytes = 0usize;
            while read_bytes < buffer.len() {
                match reader.read(&mut buffer[read_bytes..]) {
                    Ok(0) => break,
                    Ok(n) => read_bytes += n,
                    Err(ref e) if e.kind() == io::ErrorKind::Interrupted => continue,
                    Err(e) => return Err(e),
                }
            }
            if read_bytes == 0 {
                break;
            }
            let n_points_in_batch = read_bytes / 12;
            // process each point
            for i in 0..n_points_in_batch {
                let base = i * 12;
                let bx = &buffer[base..base + 12];
                let x = f32::from_le_bytes([bx[0], bx[1], bx[2], bx[3]]) as f64;
                let y = f32::from_le_bytes([bx[4], bx[5], bx[6], bx[7]]) as f64;
                let z = f32::from_le_bytes([bx[8], bx[9], bx[10], bx[11]]) as f64;
                let p = [x, y, z];
                // find nearest center
                let mut best = 0usize;
                let mut best_d = sq_dist(&p, &centers[0]);
                for c in 1..n_clusters {
                    let d = sq_dist(&p, &centers[c]);
                    if d < best_d {
                        best_d = d;
                        best = c;
                    }
                }
                // update center with learning rate 1/count
                counts[best] += 1;
                let eta = 1.0 / (counts[best] as f64);
                centers[best][0] = (1.0 - eta) * centers[best][0] + eta * p[0];
                centers[best][1] = (1.0 - eta) * centers[best][1] + eta * p[1];
                centers[best][2] = (1.0 - eta) * centers[best][2] + eta * p[2];
            }
            if read_bytes < buffer.len() {
                break;
            }
        }
        // compute movement
        let mut movement = 0.0f64;
        for c in 0..n_clusters {
            movement += sq_dist(&centers[c], &prev_centers[c]).sqrt();
        }
        prev_centers = centers.clone();
        // if movement small, we might stop
        if movement < 1e-3 {
            break;
        }
        // handle empty clusters by reseeding from sample
        for c in 0..n_clusters {
            if counts[c] == 1 {
                // reseed
                if !sample.is_empty() {
                    centers[c] = sample[rng.next_usize() % sample.len()];
                    counts[c] = 2;
                }
            }
        }
        if epoch == max_epochs - 1 {
            break;
        }
    }

    Ok(centers)
}

fn main() -> io::Result<()> {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut lock = stdin.lock();
    // read header line
    let mut header = String::new();
    let mut reader = BufReader::new(&mut lock);
    reader.read_line(&mut header)?;
    let header = header.trim();
    let mut parts = header.split_whitespace();
    let num_points: u64 = parts
        .next()
        .expect("missing num_points")
        .parse()
        .expect("invalid num_points");
    let num_clusters: usize = parts
        .next()
        .expect("missing num_clusters")
        .parse()
        .expect("invalid num_clusters");

    // thresholds
    let small_thresh: u64 = 10_000_000;
    let medium_thresh: u64 = 100_000_000;
    let large_thresh: u64 = 1_000_000_000;

    // RNG seed
    let seed = now_millis() ^ (num_points.wrapping_mul(127)) as u64;
    let mut rng = SimpleRng::new(seed);

    // If small, read into memory and do in-memory kmeans
    if num_points <= small_thresh {
        // Attempt to reserve memory
        let mut points: Vec<[f32; 3]> = Vec::with_capacity(num_points as usize);
        let mut total_read: u64 = 0;
        // read remaining binary data from reader
        let mut buf = [0u8; 12];
        while total_read < num_points {
            // read exactly 12 bytes per point
            reader.read_exact(&mut buf)?;
            let x = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[0], buf[1], buf[2], buf[3]]);
            let y = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[4], buf[5], buf[6], buf[7]]);
            let z = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[8], buf[9], buf[10], buf[11]]);
            points.push([x, y, z]);
            total_read += 1;
        }
        // run in-memory kmeans
        let centers = kmeans_in_memory(&mut points, num_clusters, &mut rng);

        // final assignment and output
        let stdout = io::stdout();
        let mut out = BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());
        for i in 0..(points.len()) {
            let p = &[points[i][0] as f64, points[i][1] as f64, points[i][2] as f64];
            let mut best = 0usize;
            let mut best_d = sq_dist(p, &centers[0]);
            for c in 1..num_clusters {
                let d = sq_dist(p, &centers[c]);
                if d < best_d {
                    best_d = d;
                    best = c;
                }
            }
            let idx = (best as u32).to_le_bytes();
            out.write_all(&idx)?;
        }
        out.flush()?;
        return Ok(());
    }

    // Otherwise: stream to temp file while collecting reservoir sample for init
    // Create a temp file path
    let mut tmp_path = env::temp_dir();
    let unique = format!("rust_kmeans_tmp_{}_{}.bin", now_millis(), rng.next_u64());
    tmp_path.push(unique);
    let mut tmp_file = OpenOptions::new()
        .create(true)
        .write(true)
        .read(true)
        .open(&tmp_path)?;

    // sample size tuned to number of clusters
    let k = num_clusters;
    let mut sample_size = (k * 20) as usize; // at least 20 points per cluster
    sample_size = max(sample_size, 2000);
    sample_size = min(sample_size, 200_000);
    let mut sample: Vec<[f64; 3]> = Vec::with_capacity(sample_size);

    // Reservoir sampling while copying input to tmp file
    const CHUNK: usize = 64 * 1024; // 64KB reads
    let mut in_buf = vec![0u8; CHUNK];
    let mut leftover = Vec::<u8>::new();
    let mut read_points: u64 = 0;
    while read_points < num_points {
        // read enough bytes for many points; but ensure we don't over-read stdin
        // read from the buffered reader
        let mut to_read = CHUNK;
        // we read until we've read all points
        let bytes_read = reader.read(&mut in_buf)?;
        if bytes_read == 0 {
            break; // unexpected EOF
        }
        let mut consumed = 0usize;
        while consumed < bytes_read {
            // accumulate bytes to form 12-byte points
            let needed = 12usize - leftover.len();
            let take = min(needed, bytes_read - consumed);
            leftover.extend_from_slice(&in_buf[consumed..consumed + take]);
            consumed += take;
            if leftover.len() == 12 {
                // we have a full point
                tmp_file.write_all(&leftover)?;
                let x = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[0], leftover[1], leftover[2], leftover[3]]) as f64;
                let y = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[4], leftover[5], leftover[6], leftover[7]]) as f64;
                let z = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[8], leftover[9], leftover[10], leftover[11]]) as f64;
                // reservoir sampling
                if (sample.len() as u64) < (sample_size as u64) {
                    sample.push([x, y, z]);
                } else {
                    // replace with decreasing probability
                    let j = rng.next_usize() % (read_points as usize + 1).max(1);
                    if j < sample_size {
                        sample[j] = [x, y, z];
                    }
                }
                read_points += 1;
                leftover.clear();
                if read_points >= num_points {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    tmp_file.flush()?;

    // If sample is empty (unlikely), we pick small initial picks by reading from tmp
    if sample.is_empty() {
        // fallback: read first few points from temp file
        tmp_file.seek(SeekFrom::Start(0))?;
        let mut r = BufReader::new(&mut tmp_file);
        let mut buf = [0u8; 12];
        for _ in 0..min(1000, num_points as usize) {
            if let Err(_) = r.read_exact(&mut buf) {
                break;
            }
            let x = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[0], buf[1], buf[2], buf[3]]) as f64;
            let y = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[4], buf[5], buf[6], buf[7]]) as f64;
            let z = f32::from_le_bytes([buf[8], buf[9], buf[10], buf[11]]) as f64;
            sample.push([x, y, z]);
        }
    }

    // choose algorithm path based on size
    let centers = if num_points <= medium_thresh {
        // medium: multiple streaming epochs with minibatch
        streaming_minibatch_kmeans(&tmp_path, num_points, num_clusters, &sample, &mut rng)?
    } else if num_points <= large_thresh {
        // large: similar but fewer epochs and smaller batch sizes
        streaming_minibatch_kmeans(&tmp_path, num_points, num_clusters, &sample, &mut rng)?
    } else {
        // very large: aggressive streaming with limited epochs
        streaming_minibatch_kmeans(&tmp_path, num_points, num_clusters, &sample, &mut rng)?
    };

    // Final assignment pass: read temp file and output nearest-centroid indices
    let mut final_file = File::open(&tmp_path)?;
    let mut reader = BufReader::new(&mut final_file);
    let stdout = io::stdout();
    let mut out = BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());
    let mut buf = vec![0u8; 64 * 1024];
    let mut leftover = Vec::<u8>::new();
    let mut produced: u64 = 0;
    while produced < num_points {
        let bytes_read = reader.read(&mut buf)?;
        if bytes_read == 0 {
            break;
        }
        let mut consumed = 0usize;
        while consumed < bytes_read {
            let needed = 12usize - leftover.len();
            let take = min(needed, bytes_read - consumed);
            leftover.extend_from_slice(&buf[consumed..consumed + take]);
            consumed += take;
            if leftover.len() == 12 {
                let x = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[0], leftover[1], leftover[2], leftover[3]]) as f64;
                let y = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[4], leftover[5], leftover[6], leftover[7]]) as f64;
                let z = f32::from_le_bytes([leftover[8], leftover[9], leftover[10], leftover[11]]) as f64;
                let p = [x, y, z];
                // find nearest centroid
                let mut best = 0usize;
                let mut best_d = sq_dist(&p, &centers[0]);
                for c in 1..num_clusters {
                    let d = sq_dist(&p, &centers[c]);
                    if d < best_d {
                        best_d = d;
                        best = c;
                    }
                }
                let idx = (best as u32).to_le_bytes();
                out.write_all(&idx)?;
                produced += 1;
                leftover.clear();
                if produced >= num_points {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    out.flush()?;

    // cleanup
    let _ = remove_file(&tmp_path);

    Ok(())
}

Test 27: Graph Coloring (C++) Score: 0.00 / 15
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to solve the Graph Coloring problem.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY graph complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: Small graphs (10-20 vertices, many colors), basic backtracking
- **Medium**: Moderate graphs (50-100 vertices, moderate colors), DSatur algorithm
- **Large**: Complex graphs (200-500 vertices, few colors), advanced heuristics
- **Extreme**: Massive graphs (1000-5000 vertices, very few colors), sophisticated optimization

**The Challenge:**
Your C++ graph colorer will be tested with graphs ranging from simple coloring tasks to massive graphs with very few available colors. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL graph complexities.

**Problem:**
Color a graph with exactly k colors such that no two adjacent vertices share the same color. This is NP-Complete and requires sophisticated algorithms for larger instances.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges num_colors
u v (for each edge, 0-indexed)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
color_0 color_1 ... color_(n-1) (color assignment for each vertex)
```

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on graph size and color availability
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for massive graphs
3. **Quality**: Find valid colorings or correctly report impossibility

**Algorithm Strategy Recommendations:**
- **Small graphs (≤50 vertices, ≥5 colors)**: Can use backtracking with pruning
- **Medium graphs (50-200 vertices, 3-4 colors)**: DSatur algorithm, constraint propagation
- **Large graphs (200-1000 vertices, 2-3 colors)**: Advanced heuristics, local search
- **Very Large graphs (>1000 vertices, 2 colors)**: Very fast heuristics, approximation methods

**Key Techniques:**
- **DSatur**: Degree of Saturation algorithm for greedy coloring
- **Backtracking**: Systematic search with pruning
- **Constraint propagation**: Forward checking to reduce search space
- **Local search**: Hill climbing, tabu search for optimization
- **Heuristics**: Largest degree ordering, Welsh-Powell algorithm

**Implementation Hints:**
- Detect graph complexity and choose appropriate coloring algorithm
- Use efficient adjacency list representation
- Implement adaptive quality vs speed tradeoffs
- For very large graphs, focus on fast heuristics
- Handle edge cases: bipartite graphs, complete graphs, impossible coloring
- Use fast I/O for large inputs

**Success Criteria:**
- All vertices assigned valid colors (0 to k-1)
- No adjacent vertices share the same color
- All vertices are colored (complete coloring)

**Failure Criteria:**
- Cannot find valid coloring within time limit
- Invalid color assignment (adjacent vertices same color)
- Incomplete coloring

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with g++ or MSVC (C++17)
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable graph sizes and color counts
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying graph complexities efficiently

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on graph complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

View chain of thought
Graph coloring assigns colors to vertices such that no adjacent vertices share a color. **Algorithms:** - **Greedy**: O(V+E), simple but may use more colors than optimal - **DSatur**: Prioritizes vertices with most colored neighbors - **Backtracking**: Exact but exponential for chromatic number
AI Reasoning: This solver adapts across graph sizes and color counts by selecting an appropriate strategy: quick special-case checks, exact/backtracking for very small graphs, DSATUR greedy for small/medium graphs, and greedy+local-search (tabu-like / iterative repair) for large/extreme graphs. Key components: - Quick checks: k>=n, k==1, k==2 (bipartite BFS) allow immediate answers or impossibility. - Exact DSATUR-based backtracking for n<=50 with forward checking and time cutoff. - DSATUR greedy with saturation-degree tie-breaking (and randomized restarts if needed) for medium graphs (<=200). - For large graphs or few colors, create a fast initial coloring (Welsh-Powell / greedy), allow conflicts, and run an iterative repair local search that picks conflicting vertices and moves them to colors that reduce conflicts, with a small tabu mechanism and random restarts. This is optimized to run quickly on thousands of vertices. Implementation notes: - Uses adjacency lists and efficient data structures (unordered_set for neighbor-used colors in DSATUR) and direct neighbor scans for local search. - Time-limited searches using chrono to ensure overall runtime remains bounded. - If a valid k-coloring is found, prints the color for each vertex (0..k-1). If proven impossible (quick checks) or no valid coloring is found within time, prints a single line "IMPOSSIBLE". This approach balances exactness where feasible and scalable heuristics for very large or hard instances.

Graph Coloring - 20 vertices, 4 colors

Approach: This solver adapts across graph sizes and color counts by selecting an appropriate strategy: quick special-case checks, exact/backtracking for very small graphs, DSATUR greedy for small/medium graphs, and greedy+local-search (tabu-like / iterative repair) for large/extreme graphs. #include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;
using steady_clock = chrono::steady_clock;

int n, m, k;
vector<vector<int>> adj;

bool verify_coloring(const vector<int>& color) {
    if ((int)color.size() != n) return false;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        if (color[i] < 0 || color[i] >= k) return false;
    }
    for (int u = 0; u < n; ++u) {
        for (int v : adj[u]) if (v > u) {
            if (color[u] == color[v]) return false;
        }
    }
    return true;
}

// Bipartite check and coloring for k==2
bool bipartite_color(vector<int>& color) {
    color.assign(n, -1);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        if (color[i] != -1) continue;
        color[i] = 0;
        deque<int> dq; dq.push_back(i);
        while (!dq.empty()) {
            int u = dq.front(); dq.pop_front();
            for (int v : adj[u]) {
                if (color[v] == -1) {
                    color[v] = color[u] ^ 1;
                    dq.push_back(v);
                } else if (color[v] == color[u]) return false;
            }
        }
    }
    return true;
}

// DSATUR greedy that respects color limit k. Returns true if success and fills color.
bool dsatur_greedy(vector<int>& color, int time_limit_ms=200) {
    auto start = steady_clock::now();
    color.assign(n, -1);
    vector<int> deg(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) deg[i] = (int)adj[i].size();
    vector<unordered_set<int>> neigh_colors(n);
    vector<int> satdeg(n, 0);
    vector<char> used(n, 0);
    int colored = 0;
    while (colored < n) {
        // time cutoff
        if (chrono::duration_cast<chrono::milliseconds>(steady_clock::now() - start).count() > time_limit_ms)
            return false;
        // select uncolored vertex with max saturation, tie by degree
        int best = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) if (color[i] == -1) {
            if (best == -1 || satdeg[i] > satdeg[best] || (satdeg[i] == satdeg[best] && deg[i] > deg[best])) {
                best = i;
            }
        }
        if (best == -1) break;
        vector<char> forbid(k, 0);
        for (int v : adj[best]) {
            if (color[v] != -1) forbid[color[v]] = 1;
        }
        int chosen = -1;
        for (int c = 0; c < k; ++c) if (!forbid[c]) { chosen = c; break; }
        if (chosen == -1) return false; // cannot color within k
        color[best] = chosen;
        colored++;
        for (int v : adj[best]) {
            if (neigh_colors[v].insert(chosen).second) {
                satdeg[v]++;
            }
        }
    }
    return verify_coloring(color);
}

// Exact backtracking using DSATUR-like variable ordering for n <= 50
bool backtrack_dsatur_rec(vector<int>& color, vector<unordered_set<int>>& neigh_colors, vector<int>& deg, int colored, steady_clock::time_point end_time) {
    if (chrono::steady_clock::now() > end_time) return false; // timeout
    if (colored == n) return true;
    // pick uncolored vertex with highest saturation, tie by degree
    int best = -1;
    vector<int> satdeg(n, 0);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) if (color[i] == -1) {
        satdeg[i] = (int)neigh_colors[i].size();
        if (best == -1 || satdeg[i] > satdeg[best] || (satdeg[i] == satdeg[best] && deg[i] > deg[best])) best = i;
    }
    if (best == -1) return false;
    // order colors by least constraining: count how many neighbors would lose options
    vector<pair<int,int>> cand; // (score, color)
    for (int c = 0; c < k; ++c) {
        bool ok = true;
        for (int v : adj[best]) if (color[v] == c) { ok = false; break; }
        if (!ok) continue;
        int score = 0;
        for (int v : adj[best]) if (color[v] == -1) {
            // would introduce color c into neigh_colors[v] (increase satdeg)
            if (neigh_colors[v].count(c) == 0) score++; // more constraining has larger score
        }
        cand.emplace_back(score, c);
    }
    sort(cand.begin(), cand.end()); // try least constraining first (small score)
    for (auto &p : cand) {
        int c = p.second;
        // assign
        color[best] = c;
        vector<int> updated;
        for (int v : adj[best]) {
            if (color[v] == -1) {
                if (neigh_colors[v].insert(c).second) updated.push_back(v);
            }
        }
        if (backtrack_dsatur_rec(color, neigh_colors, deg, colored+1, end_time)) return true;
        // undo
        for (int v : updated) neigh_colors[v].erase(c);
        color[best] = -1;
        if (chrono::steady_clock::now() > end_time) return false;
    }
    return false;
}

bool exact_backtracking(vector<int>& color, int time_limit_ms=90000) {
    color.assign(n, -1);
    vector<unordered_set<int>> neigh_colors(n);
    vector<int> deg(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) deg[i] = (int)adj[i].size();
    auto end_time = steady_clock::now() + chrono::milliseconds(time_limit_ms);
    bool ok = backtrack_dsatur_rec(color, neigh_colors, deg, 0, end_time);
    return ok && verify_coloring(color);
}

// Greedy Welsh-Powell ordering initial coloring (may produce conflicts if forced when >k neighbor colors)
void greedy_initial_coloring(vector<int>& color, std::mt19937 &rng) {
    color.assign(n, -1);
    vector<int> order(n); iota(order.begin(), order.end(), 0);
    sort(order.begin(), order.end(), [&](int a, int b){ return adj[a].size() > adj[b].size(); });
    for (int u : order) {
        vector<char> forbid(k, 0);
        for (int v : adj[u]) if (color[v] != -1) forbid[color[v]] = 1;
        int chosen = -1;
        for (int c = 0; c < k; ++c) if (!forbid[c]) { chosen = c; break; }
        if (chosen == -1) {
            // forced to create conflict: pick a random color (or least used color)
            vector<int> counts(k,0);
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) if (color[i] != -1) counts[color[i]]++;
            chosen = min_element(counts.begin(), counts.end()) - counts.begin();
        }
        color[u] = chosen;
    }
}

// Local search iterative repair for large graphs
bool local_search_repair(vector<int>& color, int time_limit_ms=240000) {
    auto start = steady_clock::now();
    auto end_time = start + chrono::milliseconds(time_limit_ms);
    std::mt19937 rng((unsigned)chrono::high_resolution_clock::now().time_since_epoch().count());

    // Initial greedy coloring
    greedy_initial_coloring(color, rng);
    vector<int> conflicts(n, 0);
    long long total_conf = 0;
    for (int u = 0; u < n; ++u) {
        int c = color[u];
        for (int v : adj[u]) if (v > u) {
            if (color[v] == c) { total_conf++; conflicts[u]++; conflicts[v]++; }
        }
    }
    if (total_conf == 0) return true;

    int iter = 0;
    const int MAX_ITERS = 2000000; // safety
    vector<int> last_changed(n, -1000);
    int move_count = 0;
    int restart_count = 0;
    while (steady_clock::now() < end_time && iter < MAX_ITERS) {
        if (total_conf == 0) return true;
        iter++;
        // pick a conflicting vertex (probabilistically weighted by conflicts)
        vector<int> conflict_vertices;
        conflict_vertices.reserve(n);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) if (conflicts[i] > 0) conflict_vertices.push_back(i);
        if (conflict_vertices.empty()) return true;
        int u = conflict_vertices[rng() % conflict_vertices.size()];
        int curc = color[u];
        // evaluate best color
        int bestc = curc;
        int best_delta = 0; // decrease in number of conflict edges around u
        // current conflicts around u is conflicts[u]
        for (int c = 0; c < k; ++c) {
            if (c == curc) continue;
            int delta = 0; // new conflicts minus old involving u
            for (int v : adj[u]) {
                if (color[v] == curc) delta -= 1;
                if (color[v] == c) delta += 1;
            }
            if (delta < best_delta || (delta == best_delta && rng()%2==0)) {
                best_delta = delta;
                bestc = c;
            }
        }
        if (bestc != curc && (move_count - last_changed[u] > 5 || best_delta < 0)) {
            // apply move
            // update conflicts and total_conf
            for (int v : adj[u]) {
                if (color[v] == curc) { conflicts[v]--; conflicts[u]--; total_conf--; }
            }
            color[u] = bestc;
            for (int v : adj[u]) {
                if (color[v] == bestc) { conflicts[v]++; conflicts[u]++; total_conf++; }
            }
            last_changed[u] = move_count;
            move_count++;
        } else {
            // try random perturbation or pick another vertex
            if (rng() % 50 == 0) {
                // random recolor a vertex to escape local optima
                int v = rng() % n;
                int old = color[v];
                int nc = rng() % k;
                if (nc != old) {
                    for (int w : adj[v]) {
                        if (color[w] == old) { conflicts[w]--; conflicts[v]--; total_conf--; }
                    }
                    color[v] = nc;
                    for (int w : adj[v]) {
                        if (color[w] == nc) { conflicts[w]++; conflicts[v]++; total_conf++; }
                    }
                    last_changed[v] = move_count;
                    move_count++;
                }
            }
        }
        if (iter % 1000 == 0) {
            // occasional random restart if stagnating
            if (steady_clock::now() + chrono::milliseconds(10) < end_time && rng() % 20 == 0) {
                restart_count++;
                greedy_initial_coloring(color, rng);
                // recompute conflicts
                fill(conflicts.begin(), conflicts.end(), 0);
                total_conf = 0;
                for (int x = 0; x < n; ++x) {
                    for (int y : adj[x]) if (y > x) {
                        if (color[x] == color[y]) { total_conf++; conflicts[x]++; conflicts[y]++; }
                    }
                }
                if (total_conf == 0) return true;
            }
        }
    }
    return (total_conf == 0 && verify_coloring(color));
}

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    if (!(cin >> n >> m >> k)) return 0;
    adj.assign(n, {});
    for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
        int u, v; cin >> u >> v;
        if (u < 0 || u >= n || v < 0 || v >= n) continue;
        if (u == v) continue; // ignore self loops (would make impossible if k<... but skip)
        adj[u].push_back(v);
        adj[v].push_back(u);
    }

    // Quick trivial cases
    vector<int> color;
    if (k >= n) {
        color.resize(n);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) color[i] = i % k;
        if (verify_coloring(color)) {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (i) cout << ' ';
                cout << color[i];
            }
            cout << '\n';
            return 0;
        }
    }
    if (k == 1) {
        if (m == 0) {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (i) cout << ' ';
                cout << 0;
            }
            cout << '\n';
            return 0;
        } else {
            cout << "IMPOSSIBLE\n";
            return 0;
        }
    }
    if (k == 2) {
        if (bipartite_color(color)) {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (i) cout << ' ';
                cout << color[i];
            }
            cout << '\n';
            return 0;
        } else {
            cout << "IMPOSSIBLE\n";
            return 0;
        }
    }

    // For very small graphs, use exact backtracking (more time allowed)
    if (n <= 50) {
        bool ok = exact_backtracking(color, 45000); // 45s for small exact search
        if (ok) {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (i) cout << ' ';
                cout << color[i];
            }
            cout << '\n';
            return 0;
        }
        // if exact fails (timeout or impossible), fall back to heuristics
    }

    // Medium graphs: try DSATUR greedy with a few restarts
    if (n <= 200) {
        vector<int> best_color;
        bool found = false;
        // try deterministic DSATUR first, then random restarts
        if (dsatur_greedy(color, 300)) {
            best_color = color; found = true;
        } else {
            std::mt19937 rng((unsigned)chrono::high_resolution_clock::now().time_since_epoch().count());
            int tries = 8;
            for (int t = 0; t < tries && !found; ++t) {
                // randomized tie breaking: shuffle adjacency order
                for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                    shuffle(adj[i].begin(), adj[i].end(), rng);
                }
                if (dsatur_greedy(color, 200)) {
                    best_color = color; found = true; break;
                }
            }
        }
        if (found) {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (i) cout << ' ';
                cout << best_color[i];
            }
            cout << '\n';
            return 0;
        }
        // else fall through to local search repair
    }

    // Large graphs and hard color counts: local search repair
    {
        bool ok = local_search_repair(color, 240000); // up to 4 minutes for local search
        if (ok) {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (i) cout << ' ';
                cout << color[i];
            }
            cout << '\n';
            return 0;
        }
    }

    // Final fallback: try a fast greedy (may fail). If fails, report impossible.
    if (dsatur_greedy(color, 500)) {
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            if (i) cout << ' ';
            cout << color[i];
        }
        cout << '\n';
        return 0;
    }

    cout << "IMPOSSIBLE\n";
    return 0;
}

Graph Coloring - 50 vertices, 5 colors

Approach: This solver adapts across graph sizes and color counts by selecting an appropriate strategy: quick special-case checks, exact/backtracking for very small graphs, DSATUR greedy for small/medium graphs, and greedy+local-search (tabu-like / iterative repair) for large/extreme graphs.
