View C++ Code (13699 chars)
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;
using steady_clock = chrono::steady_clock;

int n, m, k;
vector<vector<int>> adj;

bool verify_coloring(const vector<int>& color) {
    if ((int)color.size() != n) return false;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        if (color[i] < 0 || color[i] >= k) return false;
    }
    for (int u = 0; u < n; ++u) {
        for (int v : adj[u]) if (v > u) {
            if (color[u] == color[v]) return false;
        }
    }
    return true;
}

// Bipartite check and coloring for k==2
bool bipartite_color(vector<int>& color) {
    color.assign(n, -1);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        if (color[i] != -1) continue;
        color[i] = 0;
        deque<int> dq; dq.push_back(i);
        while (!dq.empty()) {
            int u = dq.front(); dq.pop_front();
            for (int v : adj[u]) {
                if (color[v] == -1) {
                    color[v] = color[u] ^ 1;
                    dq.push_back(v);
                } else if (color[v] == color[u]) return false;
            }
        }
    }
    return true;
}

// DSATUR greedy that respects color limit k. Returns true if success and fills color.
bool dsatur_greedy(vector<int>& color, int time_limit_ms=200) {
    auto start = steady_clock::now();
    color.assign(n, -1);
    vector<int> deg(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) deg[i] = (int)adj[i].size();
    vector<unordered_set<int>> neigh_colors(n);
    vector<int> satdeg(n, 0);
    vector<char> used(n, 0);
    int colored = 0;
    while (colored < n) {
        // time cutoff
        if (chrono::duration_cast<chrono::milliseconds>(steady_clock::now() - start).count() > time_limit_ms)
            return false;
        // select uncolored vertex with max saturation, tie by degree
        int best = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) if (color[i] == -1) {
            if (best == -1 || satdeg[i] > satdeg[best] || (satdeg[i] == satdeg[best] && deg[i] > deg[best])) {
                best = i;
            }
        }
        if (best == -1) break;
        vector<char> forbid(k, 0);
        for (int v : adj[best]) {
            if (color[v] != -1) forbid[color[v]] = 1;
        }
        int chosen = -1;
        for (int c = 0; c < k; ++c) if (!forbid[c]) { chosen = c; break; }
        if (chosen == -1) return false; // cannot color within k
        color[best] = chosen;
        colored++;
        for (int v : adj[best]) {
            if (neigh_colors[v].insert(chosen).second) {
                satdeg[v]++;
            }
        }
    }
    return verify_coloring(color);
}

// Exact backtracking using DSATUR-like variable ordering for n <= 50
bool backtrack_dsatur_rec(vector<int>& color, vector<unordered_set<int>>& neigh_colors, vector<int>& deg, int colored, steady_clock::time_point end_time) {
    if (chrono::steady_clock::now() > end_time) return false; // timeout
    if (colored == n) return true;
    // pick uncolored vertex with highest saturation, tie by degree
    int best = -1;
    vector<int> satdeg(n, 0);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) if (color[i] == -1) {
        satdeg[i] = (int)neigh_colors[i].size();
        if (best == -1 || satdeg[i] > satdeg[best] || (satdeg[i] == satdeg[best] && deg[i] > deg[best])) best = i;
    }
    if (best == -1) return false;
    // order colors by least constraining: count how many neighbors would lose options
    vector<pair<int,int>> cand; // (score, color)
    for (int c = 0; c < k; ++c) {
        bool ok = true;
        for (int v : adj[best]) if (color[v] == c) { ok = false; break; }
        if (!ok) continue;
        int score = 0;
        for (int v : adj[best]) if (color[v] == -1) {
            // would introduce color c into neigh_colors[v] (increase satdeg)
            if (neigh_colors[v].count(c) == 0) score++; // more constraining has larger score
        }
        cand.emplace_back(score, c);
    }
    sort(cand.begin(), cand.end()); // try least constraining first (small score)
    for (auto &p : cand) {
        int c = p.second;
        // assign
        color[best] = c;
        vector<int> updated;
        for (int v : adj[best]) {
            if (color[v] == -1) {
                if (neigh_colors[v].insert(c).second) updated.push_back(v);
            }
        }
        if (backtrack_dsatur_rec(color, neigh_colors, deg, colored+1, end_time)) return true;
        // undo
        for (int v : updated) neigh_colors[v].erase(c);
        color[best] = -1;
        if (chrono::steady_clock::now() > end_time) return false;
    }
    return false;
}

bool exact_backtracking(vector<int>& color, int time_limit_ms=90000) {
    color.assign(n, -1);
    vector<unordered_set<int>> neigh_colors(n);
    vector<int> deg(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) deg[i] = (int)adj[i].size();
    auto end_time = steady_clock::now() + chrono::milliseconds(time_limit_ms);
    bool ok = backtrack_dsatur_rec(color, neigh_colors, deg, 0, end_time);
    return ok && verify_coloring(color);
}

// Greedy Welsh-Powell ordering initial coloring (may produce conflicts if forced when >k neighbor colors)
void greedy_initial_coloring(vector<int>& color, std::mt19937 &rng) {
    color.assign(n, -1);
    vector<int> order(n); iota(order.begin(), order.end(), 0);
    sort(order.begin(), order.end(), [&](int a, int b){ return adj[a].size() > adj[b].size(); });
    for (int u : order) {
        vector<char> forbid(k, 0);
        for (int v : adj[u]) if (color[v] != -1) forbid[color[v]] = 1;
        int chosen = -1;
        for (int c = 0; c < k; ++c) if (!forbid[c]) { chosen = c; break; }
        if (chosen == -1) {
            // forced to create conflict: pick a random color (or least used color)
            vector<int> counts(k,0);
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) if (color[i] != -1) counts[color[i]]++;
            chosen = min_element(counts.begin(), counts.end()) - counts.begin();
        }
        color[u] = chosen;
    }
}

// Local search iterative repair for large graphs
bool local_search_repair(vector<int>& color, int time_limit_ms=240000) {
    auto start = steady_clock::now();
    auto end_time = start + chrono::milliseconds(time_limit_ms);
    std::mt19937 rng((unsigned)chrono::high_resolution_clock::now().time_since_epoch().count());

    // Initial greedy coloring
    greedy_initial_coloring(color, rng);
    vector<int> conflicts(n, 0);
    long long total_conf = 0;
    for (int u = 0; u < n; ++u) {
        int c = color[u];
        for (int v : adj[u]) if (v > u) {
            if (color[v] == c) { total_conf++; conflicts[u]++; conflicts[v]++; }
        }
    }
    if (total_conf == 0) return true;

    int iter = 0;
    const int MAX_ITERS = 2000000; // safety
    vector<int> last_changed(n, -1000);
    int move_count = 0;
    int restart_count = 0;
    while (steady_clock::now() < end_time && iter < MAX_ITERS) {
        if (total_conf == 0) return true;
        iter++;
        // pick a conflicting vertex (probabilistically weighted by conflicts)
        vector<int> conflict_vertices;
        conflict_vertices.reserve(n);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) if (conflicts[i] > 0) conflict_vertices.push_back(i);
        if (conflict_vertices.empty()) return true;
        int u = conflict_vertices[rng() % conflict_vertices.size()];
        int curc = color[u];
        // evaluate best color
        int bestc = curc;
        int best_delta = 0; // decrease in number of conflict edges around u
        // current conflicts around u is conflicts[u]
        for (int c = 0; c < k; ++c) {
            if (c == curc) continue;
            int delta = 0; // new conflicts minus old involving u
            for (int v : adj[u]) {
                if (color[v] == curc) delta -= 1;
                if (color[v] == c) delta += 1;
            }
            if (delta < best_delta || (delta == best_delta && rng()%2==0)) {
                best_delta = delta;
                bestc = c;
            }
        }
        if (bestc != curc && (move_count - last_changed[u] > 5 || best_delta < 0)) {
            // apply move
            // update conflicts and total_conf
            for (int v : adj[u]) {
                if (color[v] == curc) { conflicts[v]--; conflicts[u]--; total_conf--; }
            }
            color[u] = bestc;
            for (int v : adj[u]) {
                if (color[v] == bestc) { conflicts[v]++; conflicts[u]++; total_conf++; }
            }
            last_changed[u] = move_count;
            move_count++;
        } else {
            // try random perturbation or pick another vertex
            if (rng() % 50 == 0) {
                // random recolor a vertex to escape local optima
                int v = rng() % n;
                int old = color[v];
                int nc = rng() % k;
                if (nc != old) {
                    for (int w : adj[v]) {
                        if (color[w] == old) { conflicts[w]--; conflicts[v]--; total_conf--; }
                    }
                    color[v] = nc;
                    for (int w : adj[v]) {
                        if (color[w] == nc) { conflicts[w]++; conflicts[v]++; total_conf++; }
                    }
                    last_changed[v] = move_count;
                    move_count++;
                }
            }
        }
        if (iter % 1000 == 0) {
            // occasional random restart if stagnating
            if (steady_clock::now() + chrono::milliseconds(10) < end_time && rng() % 20 == 0) {
                restart_count++;
                greedy_initial_coloring(color, rng);
                // recompute conflicts
                fill(conflicts.begin(), conflicts.end(), 0);
                total_conf = 0;
                for (int x = 0; x < n; ++x) {
                    for (int y : adj[x]) if (y > x) {
                        if (color[x] == color[y]) { total_conf++; conflicts[x]++; conflicts[y]++; }
                    }
                }
                if (total_conf == 0) return true;
            }
        }
    }
    return (total_conf == 0 && verify_coloring(color));
}

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    if (!(cin >> n >> m >> k)) return 0;
    adj.assign(n, {});
    for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
        int u, v; cin >> u >> v;
        if (u < 0 || u >= n || v < 0 || v >= n) continue;
        if (u == v) continue; // ignore self loops (would make impossible if k<... but skip)
        adj[u].push_back(v);
        adj[v].push_back(u);
    }

    // Quick trivial cases
    vector<int> color;
    if (k >= n) {
        color.resize(n);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) color[i] = i % k;
        if (verify_coloring(color)) {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (i) cout << ' ';
                cout << color[i];
            }
            cout << '\n';
            return 0;
        }
    }
    if (k == 1) {
        if (m == 0) {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (i) cout << ' ';
                cout << 0;
            }
            cout << '\n';
            return 0;
        } else {
            cout << "IMPOSSIBLE\n";
            return 0;
        }
    }
    if (k == 2) {
        if (bipartite_color(color)) {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (i) cout << ' ';
                cout << color[i];
            }
            cout << '\n';
            return 0;
        } else {
            cout << "IMPOSSIBLE\n";
            return 0;
        }
    }

    // For very small graphs, use exact backtracking (more time allowed)
    if (n <= 50) {
        bool ok = exact_backtracking(color, 45000); // 45s for small exact search
        if (ok) {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (i) cout << ' ';
                cout << color[i];
            }
            cout << '\n';
            return 0;
        }
        // if exact fails (timeout or impossible), fall back to heuristics
    }

    // Medium graphs: try DSATUR greedy with a few restarts
    if (n <= 200) {
        vector<int> best_color;
        bool found = false;
        // try deterministic DSATUR first, then random restarts
        if (dsatur_greedy(color, 300)) {
            best_color = color; found = true;
        } else {
            std::mt19937 rng((unsigned)chrono::high_resolution_clock::now().time_since_epoch().count());
            int tries = 8;
            for (int t = 0; t < tries && !found; ++t) {
                // randomized tie breaking: shuffle adjacency order
                for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                    shuffle(adj[i].begin(), adj[i].end(), rng);
                }
                if (dsatur_greedy(color, 200)) {
                    best_color = color; found = true; break;
                }
            }
        }
        if (found) {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (i) cout << ' ';
                cout << best_color[i];
            }
            cout << '\n';
            return 0;
        }
        // else fall through to local search repair
    }

    // Large graphs and hard color counts: local search repair
    {
        bool ok = local_search_repair(color, 240000); // up to 4 minutes for local search
        if (ok) {
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (i) cout << ' ';
                cout << color[i];
            }
            cout << '\n';
            return 0;
        }
    }

    // Final fallback: try a fast greedy (may fail). If fails, report impossible.
    if (dsatur_greedy(color, 500)) {
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            if (i) cout << ' ';
            cout << color[i];
        }
        cout << '\n';
        return 0;
    }

    cout << "IMPOSSIBLE\n";
    return 0;
}

Test 28: Minimum Vertex Cover (Rust) Score: 4.12 / 15
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to solve the Minimum Vertex Cover problem.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY graph complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: Small graphs (10-20 vertices), exact algorithms, brute force
- **Medium**: Moderate graphs (50-100 vertices), branch-and-bound, kernelization
- **Large**: Complex graphs (200-500 vertices), advanced heuristics, approximation
- **Extreme**: Massive graphs (1000+ vertices), very fast heuristics, local search

**The Challenge:**
Your Rust vertex cover solver will be tested with graphs ranging from simple to massive instances. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL graph complexities.

**Problem:**
Find the minimum set of vertices such that every edge has at least one endpoint in the set. This is NP-Hard and requires sophisticated algorithms for larger instances.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges
u v (for each edge, 0-indexed)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
vertex_count
vertex_1 vertex_2 ... vertex_k (vertices in the cover, space-separated)
```

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on graph size and density
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for massive graphs
3. **Quality**: Find minimum or near-minimum vertex covers

**Algorithm Strategy Recommendations:**
- **Small graphs (≤50 vertices)**: Can use exact branch-and-bound algorithms
- **Medium graphs (50-200 vertices)**: Kernelization + branch-and-bound
- **Large graphs (200-1000 vertices)**: 2-approximation algorithms, local search
- **Very Large graphs (>1000 vertices)**: Very fast heuristics, greedy approaches

**Key Techniques:**
- **Branch-and-bound**: Systematic search with pruning
- **Kernelization**: Reduce problem size while preserving optimality
- **2-approximation**: Greedy algorithm with guaranteed factor 2
- **Local search**: Hill climbing, simulated annealing for refinement
- **LP relaxation**: Linear programming relaxation for bounds

**Implementation Hints:**
- Detect graph complexity and choose appropriate algorithm
- Use efficient adjacency list representation
- Implement adaptive quality vs speed tradeoffs
- For very large graphs, focus on fast approximation algorithms
- Handle edge cases: empty graphs, complete graphs, bipartite graphs
- Use fast I/O for large inputs

**Success Criteria:**
- All edges are covered by selected vertices
- Vertex set size is minimized (or near-minimized for large graphs)
- Output format is correct

**Failure Criteria:**
- Not all edges are covered
- Timeout without solution
- Invalid output format

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with rustc (edition 2021)
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable graph sizes and densities
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying graph complexities efficiently

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on graph complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

View chain of thought
Minimum Vertex Cover finds the smallest set of vertices covering all edges. **Algorithms:** - **2-Approximation**: Greedy edge-picking, O(V+E) - **Branch and Bound**: Exact but exponential - **Kernelization**: Reduce problem size for FPT algorithms
AI Reasoning: The solver adapts to graph size and structure. It first handles trivial cases (no edges). It detects bipartite graphs and uses Hopcroft–Karp plus Kőnig's theorem to produce an exact minimum vertex cover for bipartite graphs (works for any size). For small general graphs (n ≤ 50) it runs an exact branch-and-bound using bitmasks, degree-0/1 reductions, and a maximal-matching lower bound to prune. For larger graphs it runs iterative kernelization (degree-0/1) to reduce size; if the kernel becomes small it falls back to exact. Otherwise it computes a 2-approximation (greedy pick uncovered edge, add both endpoints) and then improves it with lightweight local search: repeated redundant-vertex removals and beneficial swaps that reduce cover size. For very large graphs (>1000) it uses the fast greedy + redundancy cleanup only. A global timer bounds heavy routines so it finishes quickly on massive inputs. The code uses adjacency lists for general graphs and u64 bitmasks for the small/exact branch-and-bound case for speed. The solver always verifies coverage before outputting the cover.

It detects bipartite graphs and uses Hopcroft–Karp plus Kőnig's theorem to produce an exact minimum vertex cover for bipartite graphs (works for any size). For small general graphs (n ≤ 50) it runs an exact branch-and-bound using bitmasks, degree-0/1 reductions, and a maximal-matching lower bound to prune. Fo...

use std::io::{self, Read};
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
use std::collections::VecDeque;

fn read_input() -> (usize, usize, Vec<(usize, usize)>) {
    let mut s = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut s).unwrap();
    let mut it = s.split_whitespace();
    let n: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let m: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let mut edges = Vec::with_capacity(m);
    for _ in 0..m {
        let u: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let v: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        if u < n && v < n && u != v {
            edges.push((u, v));
        }
    }
    (n, edges.len(), edges)
}

// Build adjacency list
fn build_adj(n: usize, edges: &[(usize, usize)]) -> Vec<Vec<usize>> {
    let mut adj = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for &(u, v) in edges {
        adj[u].push(v);
        adj[v].push(u);
    }
    for i in 0..n {
        adj[i].sort_unstable();
        adj[i].dedup();
    }
    adj
}

// Check bipartite and return color vec if bipartite (0/1). Otherwise None.
fn bipartite_color(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>) -> Option<Vec<i8>> {
    let mut color = vec![-1i8; n];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    for s in 0..n {
        if color[s] != -1 { continue; }
        color[s] = 0;
        q.push_back(s);
        while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                if color[v] == -1 {
                    color[v] = 1 - color[u];
                    q.push_back(v);
                } else if color[v] == color[u] {
                    return None;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    Some(color)
}

// Hopcroft-Karp for bipartite matching. Left side = vertices with color 0.
fn hopcroft_karp(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, color: &Vec<i8>) -> Vec<Option<usize>> {
    // Map left indices to list of neighbors in right
    let mut left_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut right_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut lcount = 0usize;
    let mut rcount = 0usize;
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_ids[i] = lcount as isize; lcount += 1; }
        else { right_ids[i] = rcount as isize; rcount += 1; }
    }
    let mut g: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); lcount];
    for u in 0..n {
        if color[u] != 0 { continue; }
        let lu = left_ids[u] as usize;
        for &v in &adj[u] {
            // edge u-v where v is right
            let rv = right_ids[v] as usize; // valid because bipartite
            g[lu].push(rv);
        }
    }
    // implement classic HK
    let mut pair_u = vec![None; lcount];
    let mut pair_v = vec![None; rcount];
    let mut dist = vec![0usize; lcount];
    let mut inf = usize::MAX / 4;
    fn bfs(g: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, pair_u: &Vec<Option<usize>>, pair_v: &Vec<Option<usize>>, dist: &mut Vec<usize>) -> bool {
        let mut q = VecDeque::new();
        let mut found = false;
        for u in 0..g.len() {
            if pair_u[u].is_none() {
                dist[u] = 0;
                q.push_back(u);
            } else {
                dist[u] = usize::MAX/4;
            }
        }
        while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
            for &v in &g[u] {
                if let Some(pu) = pair_v[v] {
                    if dist[pu] == usize::MAX/4 {
                        dist[pu] = dist[u] + 1;
                        q.push_back(pu);
                    }
                } else {
                    found = true;
                }
            }
        }
        found
    }
    fn dfs(u: usize, g: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, pair_u: &mut Vec<Option<usize>>, pair_v: &mut Vec<Option<usize>>, dist: &Vec<usize>) -> bool {
        for &v in &g[u] {
            if let Some(pu) = pair_v[v] {
                if dist[pu] != dist[u] + 1 { continue; }
                if dfs(pu, g, pair_u, pair_v, dist) {
                    pair_v[v] = Some(u);
                    pair_u[u] = Some(v);
                    return true;
                }
            } else {
                pair_v[v] = Some(u);
                pair_u[u] = Some(v);
                return true;
            }
        }
        false
    }
    while bfs(&g, &pair_u, &pair_v, &mut dist) {
        for u in 0..g.len() {
            if pair_u[u].is_none() {
                if dfs(u, &g, &mut pair_u, &mut pair_v, &dist) {}
            }
        }
    }
    // Map pair_v into original right vertices
    // Build result match for original vertices: return vector match_of_vertex where Some(partner)
    let mut match_orig = vec![None; n];
    for u in 0..n {
        if color[u] == 0 {
            let lu = left_ids[u] as usize;
            if lu < pair_u.len() {
                if let Some(rv) = pair_u[lu] {
                    // find original right vertex with id rv
                    // search for index where right_ids == rv
                    // build mapping arrays
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Build reverse mapping arrays to convert left/right ids to original indices
    let mut left_to_orig = vec![usize::MAX; lcount];
    let mut right_to_orig = vec![usize::MAX; rcount];
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_to_orig[left_ids[i] as usize] = i; }
        else { right_to_orig[right_ids[i] as usize] = i; }
    }
    // Use pair_u and pair_v to fill match_orig
    for lu in 0..lcount {
        if let Some(rv) = pair_u[lu] {
            let uorig = left_to_orig[lu];
            let vorig = right_to_orig[rv];
            match_orig[uorig] = Some(vorig);
            match_orig[vorig] = Some(uorig);
        }
    }
    match_orig
}

// From bipartite matching result (match_orig: For each vertex, Option(partner) ), derive min vertex cover using Kőnig's theorem
fn konig_min_vertex_cover(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, color: &Vec<i8>, match_orig: &Vec<Option<usize>>) -> Vec<usize> {
    // Build alternation starting from unmatched left vertices, follow alternating paths (edges not in matching from left->right, edges in matching from right->left).
    let mut left_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut right_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut lcount = 0usize;
    let mut rcount = 0usize;
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_ids[i] = lcount as isize; lcount += 1; }
        else { right_ids[i] = rcount as isize; rcount += 1; }
    }
    // build matching arrays pair_u (left->right) and pair_v (right->left)
    let mut pair_u = vec![None; lcount];
    let mut pair_v = vec![None; rcount];
    for i in 0..n {
        if let Some(j) = match_orig[i] {
            if color[i] == 0 {
                pair_u[left_ids[i] as usize] = Some(right_ids[j] as usize);
            } else {
                pair_v[right_ids[i] as usize] = Some(left_ids[j] as usize);
            }
        }
    }
    // BFS/DFS from unmatched left vertices along alternating edges
    let mut vis_left = vec![false; lcount];
    let mut vis_right = vec![false; rcount];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] != 0 { continue; }
        let li = left_ids[i] as usize;
        if pair_u[li].is_none() {
            vis_left[li] = true;
            q.push_back((0usize, li)); // 0: left
        }
    }
    while let Some((side, idx)) = q.pop_front() {
        if side == 0 {
            // from left idx, follow non-matching edges to right
            let u = (0..n).find(|&x| left_ids[x] as usize == idx).unwrap_or(usize::MAX);
            if u == usize::MAX { continue; }
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                let rv = right_ids[v] as usize;
                if !vis_right[rv] {
                    // Only follow edges not in matching? Standard: from left follow all edges to right that are NOT the matched edge.
                    if pair_u[idx] == Some(rv) { continue; }
                    vis_right[rv] = true;
                    q.push_back((1usize, rv));
                }
            }
        } else {
            // from right idx, follow matching edge to left
            if let Some(lj) = pair_v[idx] {
                if !vis_left[lj] {
                    vis_left[lj] = true;
                    q.push_back((0usize, lj));
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Minimum vertex cover = (all left not visited) union (all right visited)
    let mut cover = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 {
            let li = left_ids[i] as usize;
            if !vis_left[li] { cover.push(i); }
        } else {
            let ri = right_ids[i] as usize;
            if vis_right[ri] { cover.push(i); }
        }
    }
    cover
}

// 2-approx greedy: pick an uncovered edge, add both endpoints
fn approx_2(n: usize, edges: &Vec<(usize, usize)>, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>) -> Vec<bool> {
    let mut covered_e = vec![false; edges.len()];
    let mut cover = vec![false; n];
    // map vertex -> incident edges indices
    let mut v_to_edges: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for (i, &(u, v)) in edges.iter().enumerate() {
        v_to_edges[u].push(i);
        v_to_edges[v].push(i);
    }
    for (i, &(u, v)) in edges.iter().enumerate() {
        if covered_e[i] { continue; }
        // pick this edge
        cover[u] = true;
        cover[v] = true;
        // mark incident edges
        for &ei in &v_to_edges[u] { covered_e[ei] = true; }
        for &ei in &v_to_edges[v] { covered_e[ei] = true; }
    }
    cover
}

// Remove redundant vertices (if all neighbors are in cover, vertex can be removed)
fn remove_redundant(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, cover: &mut Vec<bool>) -> bool {
    let mut changed = false;
    let mut to_remove = Vec::new();
    for v in 0..n {
        if !cover[v] { continue; }
        let mut can_remove = true;
        for &u in &adj[v] {
            if !cover[u] { can_remove = false; break; }
        }
        if can_remove {
            to_remove.push(v);
        }
    }
    if !to_remove.is_empty() {
        changed = true;
        for v in to_remove { cover[v] = false; }
    }
    changed
}

// Attempt beneficial swaps: add a non-cover vertex v and remove neighbors fully dominated -> net decrease
fn local_swaps(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, cover: &mut Vec<bool>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> bool {
    // returns true if changed
    let mut changed = false;
    for v in 0..n {
        if let Some(tl) = time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { break; } }
        if cover[v] { continue; }
        // If we add v, which cover vertices would become removable?
        // A cover vertex u can be removed if after adding v all neighbors of u are in cover U {v}
        let mut removable = Vec::new();
        for &u in &adj[v] {
            if !cover[u] { continue; }
            // check if u would be redundant after adding v
            let mut ok = true;
            for &w in &adj[u] {
                if w == v { continue; }
                if !cover[w] { ok = false; break; }
            }
            if ok { removable.push(u); }
        }
        // net change = +1 (adding v) - removable.len()
        if removable.len() >= 2 {
            // perform swap
            cover[v] = true;
            for u in removable { cover[u] = false; }
            changed = true;
            // try more improvements after this
            return true;
        }
    }
    changed
}

// Quick validator
fn validate_cover(n: usize, edges: &Vec<(usize, usize)>, cover: &Vec<bool>) -> bool {
    for &(u, v) in edges {
        if !cover[u] && !cover[v] { return false; }
    }
    true
}

// Kernelization: degree-0 and degree-1 rules. Returns forced cover and remaining subgraph mapping
fn kernelize_deg0_deg1(n: usize, mut adj: Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> (Vec<bool>, Vec<usize>, Vec<Vec<usize>>) {
    let mut forced = vec![false; n];
    let mut active = vec![true; n];
    let mut deg = vec![0usize; n];
    for i in 0..n { deg[i] = adj[i].len(); }
    let mut changed = true;
    while changed {
        if let Some(tl) = time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { break; } }
        changed = false;
        // degree-0: remove
        for v in 0..n {
            if !active[v] { continue; }
            if deg[v] == 0 {
                active[v] = false; changed = true;
            }
        }
        // degree-1: include its neighbor
        for v in 0..n {
            if !active[v] { continue; }
            if deg[v] == 1 {
                // find neighbor
                let mut nei = usize::MAX;
                for &u in &adj[v] { if active[u] { nei = u; break; } }
                if nei == usize::MAX { continue; }
                // include neighbor nei in forced cover
                if !forced[nei] {
                    forced[nei] = true;
                }
                // remove nei and its incident edges
                if active[nei] {
                    active[nei] = false;
                    changed = true;
                    for &w in &adj[nei] {
                        if active[w] {
                            deg[w] = deg[w].saturating_sub(1);
                        }
                    }
                    deg[nei] = 0;
                }
                // v might become isolated; remove it too
                if active[v] {
                    active[v] = false;
                    changed = true;
                    for &w in &adj[v] {
                        if active[w] {
                            deg[w] = deg[w].saturating_sub(1);
                        }
                    }
                    deg[v] = 0;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Build mapping old->new for active vertices
    let mut old_to_new = vec![usize::MAX; n];
    let mut new_to_old = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if active[i] {
            old_to_new[i] = new_to_old.len();
            new_to_old.push(i);
        }
    }
    let newn = new_to_old.len();
    let mut new_adj = vec![Vec::new(); newn];
    for (new_idx, &orig) in new_to_old.iter().enumerate() {
        for &w in &adj[orig] {
            if old_to_new[w] != usize::MAX {
                new_adj[new_idx].push(old_to_new[w]);
            }
        }
    }
    for i in 0..newn { new_adj[i].sort_unstable(); new_adj[i].dedup(); }
    (forced, new_to_old, new_adj)
}

// Exact branch-and-bound for n <= 50 using u64 masks
struct ExactSmall {
    n: usize,
    adj_mask: Vec<u64>,
    best: usize,
    best_mask: u64,
    start: Instant,
    time_limit: Option<Instant>,
}

impl ExactSmall {
    fn new(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> Self {
        let mut adj_mask = vec![0u64; n];
        for i in 0..n {
            let mut m = 0u64;
            for &j in &adj[i] { m |= 1u64 << j; }
            adj_mask[i] = m;
        }
        ExactSmall { n, adj_mask, best: n+1, best_mask: 0u64, start: Instant::now(), time_limit }
    }

    fn popcount(x: u64) -> usize { x.count_ones() as usize }

    fn maximal_matching_lb(&self, active: u64) -> usize {
        let mut used = 0u64;
        let mut match_count = 0usize;
        let mut v = 0usize;
        while v < self.n {
            if (active & (1u64<<v)) == 0 || (used & (1u64<<v)) != 0 { v += 1; continue; }
            let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & active;
            let mut found = None;
            let mut u = neighs;
            while u != 0 {
                let l = u.trailing_zeros() as usize;
                if (used & (1u64<<l)) == 0 {
                    found = Some(l); break;
                }
                u &= u - 1;
            }
            if let Some(l) = found {
                used |= (1u64<<v) | (1u64<<l);
                match_count += 1;
            }
            v += 1;
        }
        match_count
    }

    fn reduce(&self, mut active: u64) -> (u64, usize) {
        // apply deg-0 and deg-1 reductions greedily
        let mut added = 0usize;
        loop {
            let mut changed = false;
            for v in 0..self.n {
                if (active & (1u64<<v)) == 0 { continue; }
                let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & active;
                let deg = ExactSmall::popcount(neighs);
                if deg == 0 {
                    active &= !(1u64<<v); changed = true;
                } else if deg == 1 {
                    // include its neighbor
                    let u = neighs.trailing_zeros() as usize;
                    // include u => remove u and v
                    if (active & (1u64<<u)) != 0 {
                        active &= !(1u64<<u);
                        added += 1;
                    }
                    if (active & (1u64<<v)) != 0 {
                        active &= !(1u64<<v);
                    }
                    changed = true;
                }
            }
            if !changed { break; }
        }
        (active, added)
    }

    fn dfs(&mut self, active: u64, current: usize, curr_mask: u64) {
        if let Some(tl) = self.time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { return; } }
        if current >= self.best { return; }
        let (mut a, added) = self.reduce(active);
        let current = current + added;
        if a == 0 {
            // no more vertices
            if current < self.best {
                self.best = current; self.best_mask = curr_mask;
            }
            return;
        }
        // lower bound from maximal matching
        let lb = self.maximal_matching_lb(a);
        if current + lb >= self.best { return; }
        // choose vertex with max degree
        let mut best_v = None;
        let mut best_deg = 0usize;
        let mut tmp = a;
        while tmp != 0 {
            let v = tmp.trailing_zeros() as usize;
            tmp &= tmp - 1;
            let deg = ExactSmall::popcount(self.adj_mask[v] & a);
            if deg > best_deg { best_deg = deg; best_v = Some(v); }
        }
        if best_v.is_none() {
            if current < self.best { self.best = current; self.best_mask = curr_mask; }
            return;
        }
        let v = best_v.unwrap();
        // Branch 1: include v
        self.dfs(a & !(1u64<<v), current + 1, curr_mask | (1u64<<v));
        // Branch 2: exclude v -> include all neighbors
        let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & a;
        let mut to_remove = neighs | (1u64<<v);
        let addcount = ExactSmall::popcount(neighs);
        if current + addcount < self.best {
            self.dfs(a & !to_remove, current + addcount, curr_mask | neighs);
        }
    }
}

fn exact_small_solver(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> Vec<usize> {
    // n <= 50
    let mut solver = ExactSmall::new(n, adj, time_limit);
    let full = if n == 64 { !0u64 } else { (1u64<<n) - 1 };
    solver.best = n + 1;
    solver.dfs(full, 0, 0u64);
    // decode best_mask
    let mut res = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if (solver.best_mask & (1u64<<i)) != 0 { res.push(i); }
    }
    // In case solver didn't find (time cut), fallback to greedy
    if solver.best > n {
        // fallback
        let mut cover_bool = vec![false; n];
        let edges = (0..n).flat_map(|u| adj[u].iter().map(move |&v| (u,v))).collect::<Vec<_>>();
        let approx = approx_2(n, &edges, adj);
        for i in 0..n { if approx[i] { res.push(i); } }
    }
    res
}

fn main() {
    let global_start = Instant::now();
    let (n, _m, edges_raw) = read_input();
    let adj = build_adj(n, &edges_raw);
    let edges = {
        // build unique edge list with u<v
        let mut e2 = Vec::new();
        for (u, v) in edges_raw.iter() {
            let (a,b) = if u <= v { (*u,*v) } else { (*v,*u) };
            if a != b { e2.push((a,b)); }
        }
        e2.sort_unstable(); e2.dedup(); e2
    };

    if edges.is_empty() {
        println!("0\n"); return;
    }

    // global time limit
    let global_time_limit = Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(280); // a bit under 5 minutes

    // 1) if bipartite - solve exactly using matching
    if let Some(color) = bipartite_color(n, &adj) {
        let match_orig = hopcroft_karp(n, &adj, &color);
        let cover = konig_min_vertex_cover(n, &adj, &color, &match_orig);
        let mut cover_sorted = cover.clone(); cover_sorted.sort_unstable();
        println!("{}", cover_sorted.len());
        for (i, v) in cover_sorted.iter().enumerate() {
            if i == cover_sorted.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); }
            else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // adapt based on size
    if n <= 50 {
        let time_limit = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(30)));
        let res = exact_small_solver(n, &adj, time_limit);
        let mut cover = vec![false; n]; for v in res { cover[v] = true; }
        // ensure coverage
        if !validate_cover(n, &edges, &cover) {
            // fallback approx
            let approx = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
            let mut out = Vec::new();
            for i in 0..n { if approx[i] { out.push(i); } }
            println!("{}", out.len());
            for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
                if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
            }
            return;
        }
        let mut out = Vec::new(); for i in 0..n { if cover[i] { out.push(i); } }
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // Kernelize degree-0 and degree-1 for medium/large
    let tl_kernel = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(20)));
    let (mut forced_orig, new_to_old, new_adj) = kernelize_deg0_deg1(n, adj.clone(), tl_kernel);
    let kernel_n = new_adj.len();
    // initialize cover with forced vertices
    let mut cover_bool = vec![false; n];
    for i in 0..n { if forced_orig[i] { cover_bool[i] = true; } }

    if kernel_n == 0 {
        // forced cover already covers all edges
        let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // If kernel small -> exact
    if kernel_n <= 50 {
        // build small adj for kernel mapping
        let mut small_adj = vec![Vec::new(); kernel_n];
        for i in 0..kernel_n { small_adj[i] = new_adj[i].clone(); }
        // solve exact on kernel
        let time_limit = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(40)));
        let kernel_sol = exact_small_solver(kernel_n, &small_adj, time_limit);
        for v in kernel_sol { let orig = new_to_old[v]; cover_bool[orig] = true; }
        // Output
        let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // Otherwise use approximation + local search
    // Build edges for kernel mapping to original indices
    let mut kernel_edges = Vec::new();
    for u in 0..kernel_n {
        for &v in &new_adj[u] { if u < v { kernel_edges.push((new_to_old[u], new_to_old[v])); } }
    }

    // Initial approx on whole graph: better to run approx on original edges
    let approx_cover = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
    for i in 0..n { if approx_cover[i] { cover_bool[i] = true; } }

    // Remove redundants
    let mut changed = true;
    let mut iter = 0;
    while changed && Instant::now() < global_time_limit && iter < 2000 {
        changed = remove_redundant(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool);
        // try local swaps that reduce size
        let tl_swaps = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_millis(200)));
        if local_swaps(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool, tl_swaps) { changed = true; }
        iter += 1;
    }

    // Final fallback: if still not covering (should cover), fix by greedy
    if !validate_cover(n, &edges, &cover_bool) {
        let approx_cover2 = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
        cover_bool = approx_cover2;
        // cleanup
        remove_redundant(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool);
    }

    let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
    println!("{}", out.len());
    for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
        if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
    }
}
Subpass 1
Subpass configurationView exact prompt

View chain of thought
 0.5000
[50 vertices, 150 edges] Cover size 44 (greedy: 42), 0.01s

Minimum Vertex Cover - 50 vertices, 150 edges

Approach: The solver adapts to graph size and structure. It first handles trivial cases (no edges). It detects bipartite graphs and uses Hopcroft–Karp plus Kőnig's theorem to produce an exact minimum vertex cover for bipartite graphs (works for any size). For small general graphs (n ≤ 50) it runs an exact branch-and-bound using bitmasks, degree-0/1 reductions, and a maximal-matching lower bound to prune. Fo...

View Rust Code (24457 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read};
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
use std::collections::VecDeque;

fn read_input() -> (usize, usize, Vec<(usize, usize)>) {
    let mut s = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut s).unwrap();
    let mut it = s.split_whitespace();
    let n: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let m: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let mut edges = Vec::with_capacity(m);
    for _ in 0..m {
        let u: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let v: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        if u < n && v < n && u != v {
            edges.push((u, v));
        }
    }
    (n, edges.len(), edges)
}

// Build adjacency list
fn build_adj(n: usize, edges: &[(usize, usize)]) -> Vec<Vec<usize>> {
    let mut adj = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for &(u, v) in edges {
        adj[u].push(v);
        adj[v].push(u);
    }
    for i in 0..n {
        adj[i].sort_unstable();
        adj[i].dedup();
    }
    adj
}

// Check bipartite and return color vec if bipartite (0/1). Otherwise None.
fn bipartite_color(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>) -> Option<Vec<i8>> {
    let mut color = vec![-1i8; n];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    for s in 0..n {
        if color[s] != -1 { continue; }
        color[s] = 0;
        q.push_back(s);
        while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                if color[v] == -1 {
                    color[v] = 1 - color[u];
                    q.push_back(v);
                } else if color[v] == color[u] {
                    return None;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    Some(color)
}

// Hopcroft-Karp for bipartite matching. Left side = vertices with color 0.
fn hopcroft_karp(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, color: &Vec<i8>) -> Vec<Option<usize>> {
    // Map left indices to list of neighbors in right
    let mut left_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut right_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut lcount = 0usize;
    let mut rcount = 0usize;
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_ids[i] = lcount as isize; lcount += 1; }
        else { right_ids[i] = rcount as isize; rcount += 1; }
    }
    let mut g: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); lcount];
    for u in 0..n {
        if color[u] != 0 { continue; }
        let lu = left_ids[u] as usize;
        for &v in &adj[u] {
            // edge u-v where v is right
            let rv = right_ids[v] as usize; // valid because bipartite
            g[lu].push(rv);
        }
    }
    // implement classic HK
    let mut pair_u = vec![None; lcount];
    let mut pair_v = vec![None; rcount];
    let mut dist = vec![0usize; lcount];
    let mut inf = usize::MAX / 4;
    fn bfs(g: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, pair_u: &Vec<Option<usize>>, pair_v: &Vec<Option<usize>>, dist: &mut Vec<usize>) -> bool {
        let mut q = VecDeque::new();
        let mut found = false;
        for u in 0..g.len() {
            if pair_u[u].is_none() {
                dist[u] = 0;
                q.push_back(u);
            } else {
                dist[u] = usize::MAX/4;
            }
        }
        while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
            for &v in &g[u] {
                if let Some(pu) = pair_v[v] {
                    if dist[pu] == usize::MAX/4 {
                        dist[pu] = dist[u] + 1;
                        q.push_back(pu);
                    }
                } else {
                    found = true;
                }
            }
        }
        found
    }
    fn dfs(u: usize, g: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, pair_u: &mut Vec<Option<usize>>, pair_v: &mut Vec<Option<usize>>, dist: &Vec<usize>) -> bool {
        for &v in &g[u] {
            if let Some(pu) = pair_v[v] {
                if dist[pu] != dist[u] + 1 { continue; }
                if dfs(pu, g, pair_u, pair_v, dist) {
                    pair_v[v] = Some(u);
                    pair_u[u] = Some(v);
                    return true;
                }
            } else {
                pair_v[v] = Some(u);
                pair_u[u] = Some(v);
                return true;
            }
        }
        false
    }
    while bfs(&g, &pair_u, &pair_v, &mut dist) {
        for u in 0..g.len() {
            if pair_u[u].is_none() {
                if dfs(u, &g, &mut pair_u, &mut pair_v, &dist) {}
            }
        }
    }
    // Map pair_v into original right vertices
    // Build result match for original vertices: return vector match_of_vertex where Some(partner)
    let mut match_orig = vec![None; n];
    for u in 0..n {
        if color[u] == 0 {
            let lu = left_ids[u] as usize;
            if lu < pair_u.len() {
                if let Some(rv) = pair_u[lu] {
                    // find original right vertex with id rv
                    // search for index where right_ids == rv
                    // build mapping arrays
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Build reverse mapping arrays to convert left/right ids to original indices
    let mut left_to_orig = vec![usize::MAX; lcount];
    let mut right_to_orig = vec![usize::MAX; rcount];
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_to_orig[left_ids[i] as usize] = i; }
        else { right_to_orig[right_ids[i] as usize] = i; }
    }
    // Use pair_u and pair_v to fill match_orig
    for lu in 0..lcount {
        if let Some(rv) = pair_u[lu] {
            let uorig = left_to_orig[lu];
            let vorig = right_to_orig[rv];
            match_orig[uorig] = Some(vorig);
            match_orig[vorig] = Some(uorig);
        }
    }
    match_orig
}

// From bipartite matching result (match_orig: For each vertex, Option(partner) ), derive min vertex cover using Kőnig's theorem
fn konig_min_vertex_cover(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, color: &Vec<i8>, match_orig: &Vec<Option<usize>>) -> Vec<usize> {
    // Build alternation starting from unmatched left vertices, follow alternating paths (edges not in matching from left->right, edges in matching from right->left).
    let mut left_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut right_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut lcount = 0usize;
    let mut rcount = 0usize;
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_ids[i] = lcount as isize; lcount += 1; }
        else { right_ids[i] = rcount as isize; rcount += 1; }
    }
    // build matching arrays pair_u (left->right) and pair_v (right->left)
    let mut pair_u = vec![None; lcount];
    let mut pair_v = vec![None; rcount];
    for i in 0..n {
        if let Some(j) = match_orig[i] {
            if color[i] == 0 {
                pair_u[left_ids[i] as usize] = Some(right_ids[j] as usize);
            } else {
                pair_v[right_ids[i] as usize] = Some(left_ids[j] as usize);
            }
        }
    }
    // BFS/DFS from unmatched left vertices along alternating edges
    let mut vis_left = vec![false; lcount];
    let mut vis_right = vec![false; rcount];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] != 0 { continue; }
        let li = left_ids[i] as usize;
        if pair_u[li].is_none() {
            vis_left[li] = true;
            q.push_back((0usize, li)); // 0: left
        }
    }
    while let Some((side, idx)) = q.pop_front() {
        if side == 0 {
            // from left idx, follow non-matching edges to right
            let u = (0..n).find(|&x| left_ids[x] as usize == idx).unwrap_or(usize::MAX);
            if u == usize::MAX { continue; }
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                let rv = right_ids[v] as usize;
                if !vis_right[rv] {
                    // Only follow edges not in matching? Standard: from left follow all edges to right that are NOT the matched edge.
                    if pair_u[idx] == Some(rv) { continue; }
                    vis_right[rv] = true;
                    q.push_back((1usize, rv));
                }
            }
        } else {
            // from right idx, follow matching edge to left
            if let Some(lj) = pair_v[idx] {
                if !vis_left[lj] {
                    vis_left[lj] = true;
                    q.push_back((0usize, lj));
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Minimum vertex cover = (all left not visited) union (all right visited)
    let mut cover = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 {
            let li = left_ids[i] as usize;
            if !vis_left[li] { cover.push(i); }
        } else {
            let ri = right_ids[i] as usize;
            if vis_right[ri] { cover.push(i); }
        }
    }
    cover
}

// 2-approx greedy: pick an uncovered edge, add both endpoints
fn approx_2(n: usize, edges: &Vec<(usize, usize)>, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>) -> Vec<bool> {
    let mut covered_e = vec![false; edges.len()];
    let mut cover = vec![false; n];
    // map vertex -> incident edges indices
    let mut v_to_edges: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for (i, &(u, v)) in edges.iter().enumerate() {
        v_to_edges[u].push(i);
        v_to_edges[v].push(i);
    }
    for (i, &(u, v)) in edges.iter().enumerate() {
        if covered_e[i] { continue; }
        // pick this edge
        cover[u] = true;
        cover[v] = true;
        // mark incident edges
        for &ei in &v_to_edges[u] { covered_e[ei] = true; }
        for &ei in &v_to_edges[v] { covered_e[ei] = true; }
    }
    cover
}

// Remove redundant vertices (if all neighbors are in cover, vertex can be removed)
fn remove_redundant(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, cover: &mut Vec<bool>) -> bool {
    let mut changed = false;
    let mut to_remove = Vec::new();
    for v in 0..n {
        if !cover[v] { continue; }
        let mut can_remove = true;
        for &u in &adj[v] {
            if !cover[u] { can_remove = false; break; }
        }
        if can_remove {
            to_remove.push(v);
        }
    }
    if !to_remove.is_empty() {
        changed = true;
        for v in to_remove { cover[v] = false; }
    }
    changed
}

// Attempt beneficial swaps: add a non-cover vertex v and remove neighbors fully dominated -> net decrease
fn local_swaps(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, cover: &mut Vec<bool>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> bool {
    // returns true if changed
    let mut changed = false;
    for v in 0..n {
        if let Some(tl) = time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { break; } }
        if cover[v] { continue; }
        // If we add v, which cover vertices would become removable?
        // A cover vertex u can be removed if after adding v all neighbors of u are in cover U {v}
        let mut removable = Vec::new();
        for &u in &adj[v] {
            if !cover[u] { continue; }
            // check if u would be redundant after adding v
            let mut ok = true;
            for &w in &adj[u] {
                if w == v { continue; }
                if !cover[w] { ok = false; break; }
            }
            if ok { removable.push(u); }
        }
        // net change = +1 (adding v) - removable.len()
        if removable.len() >= 2 {
            // perform swap
            cover[v] = true;
            for u in removable { cover[u] = false; }
            changed = true;
            // try more improvements after this
            return true;
        }
    }
    changed
}

// Quick validator
fn validate_cover(n: usize, edges: &Vec<(usize, usize)>, cover: &Vec<bool>) -> bool {
    for &(u, v) in edges {
        if !cover[u] && !cover[v] { return false; }
    }
    true
}

// Kernelization: degree-0 and degree-1 rules. Returns forced cover and remaining subgraph mapping
fn kernelize_deg0_deg1(n: usize, mut adj: Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> (Vec<bool>, Vec<usize>, Vec<Vec<usize>>) {
    let mut forced = vec![false; n];
    let mut active = vec![true; n];
    let mut deg = vec![0usize; n];
    for i in 0..n { deg[i] = adj[i].len(); }
    let mut changed = true;
    while changed {
        if let Some(tl) = time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { break; } }
        changed = false;
        // degree-0: remove
        for v in 0..n {
            if !active[v] { continue; }
            if deg[v] == 0 {
                active[v] = false; changed = true;
            }
        }
        // degree-1: include its neighbor
        for v in 0..n {
            if !active[v] { continue; }
            if deg[v] == 1 {
                // find neighbor
                let mut nei = usize::MAX;
                for &u in &adj[v] { if active[u] { nei = u; break; } }
                if nei == usize::MAX { continue; }
                // include neighbor nei in forced cover
                if !forced[nei] {
                    forced[nei] = true;
                }
                // remove nei and its incident edges
                if active[nei] {
                    active[nei] = false;
                    changed = true;
                    for &w in &adj[nei] {
                        if active[w] {
                            deg[w] = deg[w].saturating_sub(1);
                        }
                    }
                    deg[nei] = 0;
                }
                // v might become isolated; remove it too
                if active[v] {
                    active[v] = false;
                    changed = true;
                    for &w in &adj[v] {
                        if active[w] {
                            deg[w] = deg[w].saturating_sub(1);
                        }
                    }
                    deg[v] = 0;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Build mapping old->new for active vertices
    let mut old_to_new = vec![usize::MAX; n];
    let mut new_to_old = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if active[i] {
            old_to_new[i] = new_to_old.len();
            new_to_old.push(i);
        }
    }
    let newn = new_to_old.len();
    let mut new_adj = vec![Vec::new(); newn];
    for (new_idx, &orig) in new_to_old.iter().enumerate() {
        for &w in &adj[orig] {
            if old_to_new[w] != usize::MAX {
                new_adj[new_idx].push(old_to_new[w]);
            }
        }
    }
    for i in 0..newn { new_adj[i].sort_unstable(); new_adj[i].dedup(); }
    (forced, new_to_old, new_adj)
}

// Exact branch-and-bound for n <= 50 using u64 masks
struct ExactSmall {
    n: usize,
    adj_mask: Vec<u64>,
    best: usize,
    best_mask: u64,
    start: Instant,
    time_limit: Option<Instant>,
}

impl ExactSmall {
    fn new(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> Self {
        let mut adj_mask = vec![0u64; n];
        for i in 0..n {
            let mut m = 0u64;
            for &j in &adj[i] { m |= 1u64 << j; }
            adj_mask[i] = m;
        }
        ExactSmall { n, adj_mask, best: n+1, best_mask: 0u64, start: Instant::now(), time_limit }
    }

    fn popcount(x: u64) -> usize { x.count_ones() as usize }

    fn maximal_matching_lb(&self, active: u64) -> usize {
        let mut used = 0u64;
        let mut match_count = 0usize;
        let mut v = 0usize;
        while v < self.n {
            if (active & (1u64<<v)) == 0 || (used & (1u64<<v)) != 0 { v += 1; continue; }
            let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & active;
            let mut found = None;
            let mut u = neighs;
            while u != 0 {
                let l = u.trailing_zeros() as usize;
                if (used & (1u64<<l)) == 0 {
                    found = Some(l); break;
                }
                u &= u - 1;
            }
            if let Some(l) = found {
                used |= (1u64<<v) | (1u64<<l);
                match_count += 1;
            }
            v += 1;
        }
        match_count
    }

    fn reduce(&self, mut active: u64) -> (u64, usize) {
        // apply deg-0 and deg-1 reductions greedily
        let mut added = 0usize;
        loop {
            let mut changed = false;
            for v in 0..self.n {
                if (active & (1u64<<v)) == 0 { continue; }
                let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & active;
                let deg = ExactSmall::popcount(neighs);
                if deg == 0 {
                    active &= !(1u64<<v); changed = true;
                } else if deg == 1 {
                    // include its neighbor
                    let u = neighs.trailing_zeros() as usize;
                    // include u => remove u and v
                    if (active & (1u64<<u)) != 0 {
                        active &= !(1u64<<u);
                        added += 1;
                    }
                    if (active & (1u64<<v)) != 0 {
                        active &= !(1u64<<v);
                    }
                    changed = true;
                }
            }
            if !changed { break; }
        }
        (active, added)
    }

    fn dfs(&mut self, active: u64, current: usize, curr_mask: u64) {
        if let Some(tl) = self.time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { return; } }
        if current >= self.best { return; }
        let (mut a, added) = self.reduce(active);
        let current = current + added;
        if a == 0 {
            // no more vertices
            if current < self.best {
                self.best = current; self.best_mask = curr_mask;
            }
            return;
        }
        // lower bound from maximal matching
        let lb = self.maximal_matching_lb(a);
        if current + lb >= self.best { return; }
        // choose vertex with max degree
        let mut best_v = None;
        let mut best_deg = 0usize;
        let mut tmp = a;
        while tmp != 0 {
            let v = tmp.trailing_zeros() as usize;
            tmp &= tmp - 1;
            let deg = ExactSmall::popcount(self.adj_mask[v] & a);
            if deg > best_deg { best_deg = deg; best_v = Some(v); }
        }
        if best_v.is_none() {
            if current < self.best { self.best = current; self.best_mask = curr_mask; }
            return;
        }
        let v = best_v.unwrap();
        // Branch 1: include v
        self.dfs(a & !(1u64<<v), current + 1, curr_mask | (1u64<<v));
        // Branch 2: exclude v -> include all neighbors
        let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & a;
        let mut to_remove = neighs | (1u64<<v);
        let addcount = ExactSmall::popcount(neighs);
        if current + addcount < self.best {
            self.dfs(a & !to_remove, current + addcount, curr_mask | neighs);
        }
    }
}

fn exact_small_solver(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> Vec<usize> {
    // n <= 50
    let mut solver = ExactSmall::new(n, adj, time_limit);
    let full = if n == 64 { !0u64 } else { (1u64<<n) - 1 };
    solver.best = n + 1;
    solver.dfs(full, 0, 0u64);
    // decode best_mask
    let mut res = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if (solver.best_mask & (1u64<<i)) != 0 { res.push(i); }
    }
    // In case solver didn't find (time cut), fallback to greedy
    if solver.best > n {
        // fallback
        let mut cover_bool = vec![false; n];
        let edges = (0..n).flat_map(|u| adj[u].iter().map(move |&v| (u,v))).collect::<Vec<_>>();
        let approx = approx_2(n, &edges, adj);
        for i in 0..n { if approx[i] { res.push(i); } }
    }
    res
}

fn main() {
    let global_start = Instant::now();
    let (n, _m, edges_raw) = read_input();
    let adj = build_adj(n, &edges_raw);
    let edges = {
        // build unique edge list with u<v
        let mut e2 = Vec::new();
        for (u, v) in edges_raw.iter() {
            let (a,b) = if u <= v { (*u,*v) } else { (*v,*u) };
            if a != b { e2.push((a,b)); }
        }
        e2.sort_unstable(); e2.dedup(); e2
    };

    if edges.is_empty() {
        println!("0\n"); return;
    }

    // global time limit
    let global_time_limit = Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(280); // a bit under 5 minutes

    // 1) if bipartite - solve exactly using matching
    if let Some(color) = bipartite_color(n, &adj) {
        let match_orig = hopcroft_karp(n, &adj, &color);
        let cover = konig_min_vertex_cover(n, &adj, &color, &match_orig);
        let mut cover_sorted = cover.clone(); cover_sorted.sort_unstable();
        println!("{}", cover_sorted.len());
        for (i, v) in cover_sorted.iter().enumerate() {
            if i == cover_sorted.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); }
            else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // adapt based on size
    if n <= 50 {
        let time_limit = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(30)));
        let res = exact_small_solver(n, &adj, time_limit);
        let mut cover = vec![false; n]; for v in res { cover[v] = true; }
        // ensure coverage
        if !validate_cover(n, &edges, &cover) {
            // fallback approx
            let approx = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
            let mut out = Vec::new();
            for i in 0..n { if approx[i] { out.push(i); } }
            println!("{}", out.len());
            for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
                if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
            }
            return;
        }
        let mut out = Vec::new(); for i in 0..n { if cover[i] { out.push(i); } }
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // Kernelize degree-0 and degree-1 for medium/large
    let tl_kernel = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(20)));
    let (mut forced_orig, new_to_old, new_adj) = kernelize_deg0_deg1(n, adj.clone(), tl_kernel);
    let kernel_n = new_adj.len();
    // initialize cover with forced vertices
    let mut cover_bool = vec![false; n];
    for i in 0..n { if forced_orig[i] { cover_bool[i] = true; } }

    if kernel_n == 0 {
        // forced cover already covers all edges
        let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // If kernel small -> exact
    if kernel_n <= 50 {
        // build small adj for kernel mapping
        let mut small_adj = vec![Vec::new(); kernel_n];
        for i in 0..kernel_n { small_adj[i] = new_adj[i].clone(); }
        // solve exact on kernel
        let time_limit = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(40)));
        let kernel_sol = exact_small_solver(kernel_n, &small_adj, time_limit);
        for v in kernel_sol { let orig = new_to_old[v]; cover_bool[orig] = true; }
        // Output
        let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // Otherwise use approximation + local search
    // Build edges for kernel mapping to original indices
    let mut kernel_edges = Vec::new();
    for u in 0..kernel_n {
        for &v in &new_adj[u] { if u < v { kernel_edges.push((new_to_old[u], new_to_old[v])); } }
    }

    // Initial approx on whole graph: better to run approx on original edges
    let approx_cover = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
    for i in 0..n { if approx_cover[i] { cover_bool[i] = true; } }

    // Remove redundants
    let mut changed = true;
    let mut iter = 0;
    while changed && Instant::now() < global_time_limit && iter < 2000 {
        changed = remove_redundant(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool);
        // try local swaps that reduce size
        let tl_swaps = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_millis(200)));
        if local_swaps(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool, tl_swaps) { changed = true; }
        iter += 1;
    }

    // Final fallback: if still not covering (should cover), fix by greedy
    if !validate_cover(n, &edges, &cover_bool) {
        let approx_cover2 = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
        cover_bool = approx_cover2;
        // cleanup
        remove_redundant(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool);
    }

    let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
    println!("{}", out.len());
    for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
        if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
    }
}
Subpass 2

View chain of thought
