[100 vertices, 400 edges] Edge (18,35) not covered

Minimum Vertex Cover - 100 vertices, 400 edges

Approach: The solver adapts to graph size and structure. It first handles trivial cases (no edges). It detects bipartite graphs and uses Hopcroft–Karp plus Kőnig's theorem to produce an exact minimum vertex cover for bipartite graphs (works for any size). For small general graphs (n ≤ 50) it runs an exact branch-and-bound using bitmasks, degree-0/1 reductions, and a maximal-matching lower bound to prune. Fo...

View Rust Code (24457 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read};
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
use std::collections::VecDeque;

fn read_input() -> (usize, usize, Vec<(usize, usize)>) {
    let mut s = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut s).unwrap();
    let mut it = s.split_whitespace();
    let n: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let m: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let mut edges = Vec::with_capacity(m);
    for _ in 0..m {
        let u: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let v: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        if u < n && v < n && u != v {
            edges.push((u, v));
        }
    }
    (n, edges.len(), edges)
}

// Build adjacency list
fn build_adj(n: usize, edges: &[(usize, usize)]) -> Vec<Vec<usize>> {
    let mut adj = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for &(u, v) in edges {
        adj[u].push(v);
        adj[v].push(u);
    }
    for i in 0..n {
        adj[i].sort_unstable();
        adj[i].dedup();
    }
    adj
}

// Check bipartite and return color vec if bipartite (0/1). Otherwise None.
fn bipartite_color(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>) -> Option<Vec<i8>> {
    let mut color = vec![-1i8; n];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    for s in 0..n {
        if color[s] != -1 { continue; }
        color[s] = 0;
        q.push_back(s);
        while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                if color[v] == -1 {
                    color[v] = 1 - color[u];
                    q.push_back(v);
                } else if color[v] == color[u] {
                    return None;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    Some(color)
}

// Hopcroft-Karp for bipartite matching. Left side = vertices with color 0.
fn hopcroft_karp(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, color: &Vec<i8>) -> Vec<Option<usize>> {
    // Map left indices to list of neighbors in right
    let mut left_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut right_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut lcount = 0usize;
    let mut rcount = 0usize;
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_ids[i] = lcount as isize; lcount += 1; }
        else { right_ids[i] = rcount as isize; rcount += 1; }
    }
    let mut g: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); lcount];
    for u in 0..n {
        if color[u] != 0 { continue; }
        let lu = left_ids[u] as usize;
        for &v in &adj[u] {
            // edge u-v where v is right
            let rv = right_ids[v] as usize; // valid because bipartite
            g[lu].push(rv);
        }
    }
    // implement classic HK
    let mut pair_u = vec![None; lcount];
    let mut pair_v = vec![None; rcount];
    let mut dist = vec![0usize; lcount];
    let mut inf = usize::MAX / 4;
    fn bfs(g: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, pair_u: &Vec<Option<usize>>, pair_v: &Vec<Option<usize>>, dist: &mut Vec<usize>) -> bool {
        let mut q = VecDeque::new();
        let mut found = false;
        for u in 0..g.len() {
            if pair_u[u].is_none() {
                dist[u] = 0;
                q.push_back(u);
            } else {
                dist[u] = usize::MAX/4;
            }
        }
        while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
            for &v in &g[u] {
                if let Some(pu) = pair_v[v] {
                    if dist[pu] == usize::MAX/4 {
                        dist[pu] = dist[u] + 1;
                        q.push_back(pu);
                    }
                } else {
                    found = true;
                }
            }
        }
        found
    }
    fn dfs(u: usize, g: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, pair_u: &mut Vec<Option<usize>>, pair_v: &mut Vec<Option<usize>>, dist: &Vec<usize>) -> bool {
        for &v in &g[u] {
            if let Some(pu) = pair_v[v] {
                if dist[pu] != dist[u] + 1 { continue; }
                if dfs(pu, g, pair_u, pair_v, dist) {
                    pair_v[v] = Some(u);
                    pair_u[u] = Some(v);
                    return true;
                }
            } else {
                pair_v[v] = Some(u);
                pair_u[u] = Some(v);
                return true;
            }
        }
        false
    }
    while bfs(&g, &pair_u, &pair_v, &mut dist) {
        for u in 0..g.len() {
            if pair_u[u].is_none() {
                if dfs(u, &g, &mut pair_u, &mut pair_v, &dist) {}
            }
        }
    }
    // Map pair_v into original right vertices
    // Build result match for original vertices: return vector match_of_vertex where Some(partner)
    let mut match_orig = vec![None; n];
    for u in 0..n {
        if color[u] == 0 {
            let lu = left_ids[u] as usize;
            if lu < pair_u.len() {
                if let Some(rv) = pair_u[lu] {
                    // find original right vertex with id rv
                    // search for index where right_ids == rv
                    // build mapping arrays
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Build reverse mapping arrays to convert left/right ids to original indices
    let mut left_to_orig = vec![usize::MAX; lcount];
    let mut right_to_orig = vec![usize::MAX; rcount];
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_to_orig[left_ids[i] as usize] = i; }
        else { right_to_orig[right_ids[i] as usize] = i; }
    }
    // Use pair_u and pair_v to fill match_orig
    for lu in 0..lcount {
        if let Some(rv) = pair_u[lu] {
            let uorig = left_to_orig[lu];
            let vorig = right_to_orig[rv];
            match_orig[uorig] = Some(vorig);
            match_orig[vorig] = Some(uorig);
        }
    }
    match_orig
}

// From bipartite matching result (match_orig: For each vertex, Option(partner) ), derive min vertex cover using Kőnig's theorem
fn konig_min_vertex_cover(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, color: &Vec<i8>, match_orig: &Vec<Option<usize>>) -> Vec<usize> {
    // Build alternation starting from unmatched left vertices, follow alternating paths (edges not in matching from left->right, edges in matching from right->left).
    let mut left_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut right_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut lcount = 0usize;
    let mut rcount = 0usize;
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_ids[i] = lcount as isize; lcount += 1; }
        else { right_ids[i] = rcount as isize; rcount += 1; }
    }
    // build matching arrays pair_u (left->right) and pair_v (right->left)
    let mut pair_u = vec![None; lcount];
    let mut pair_v = vec![None; rcount];
    for i in 0..n {
        if let Some(j) = match_orig[i] {
            if color[i] == 0 {
                pair_u[left_ids[i] as usize] = Some(right_ids[j] as usize);
            } else {
                pair_v[right_ids[i] as usize] = Some(left_ids[j] as usize);
            }
        }
    }
    // BFS/DFS from unmatched left vertices along alternating edges
    let mut vis_left = vec![false; lcount];
    let mut vis_right = vec![false; rcount];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] != 0 { continue; }
        let li = left_ids[i] as usize;
        if pair_u[li].is_none() {
            vis_left[li] = true;
            q.push_back((0usize, li)); // 0: left
        }
    }
    while let Some((side, idx)) = q.pop_front() {
        if side == 0 {
            // from left idx, follow non-matching edges to right
            let u = (0..n).find(|&x| left_ids[x] as usize == idx).unwrap_or(usize::MAX);
            if u == usize::MAX { continue; }
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                let rv = right_ids[v] as usize;
                if !vis_right[rv] {
                    // Only follow edges not in matching? Standard: from left follow all edges to right that are NOT the matched edge.
                    if pair_u[idx] == Some(rv) { continue; }
                    vis_right[rv] = true;
                    q.push_back((1usize, rv));
                }
            }
        } else {
            // from right idx, follow matching edge to left
            if let Some(lj) = pair_v[idx] {
                if !vis_left[lj] {
                    vis_left[lj] = true;
                    q.push_back((0usize, lj));
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Minimum vertex cover = (all left not visited) union (all right visited)
    let mut cover = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 {
            let li = left_ids[i] as usize;
            if !vis_left[li] { cover.push(i); }
        } else {
            let ri = right_ids[i] as usize;
            if vis_right[ri] { cover.push(i); }
        }
    }
    cover
}

// 2-approx greedy: pick an uncovered edge, add both endpoints
fn approx_2(n: usize, edges: &Vec<(usize, usize)>, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>) -> Vec<bool> {
    let mut covered_e = vec![false; edges.len()];
    let mut cover = vec![false; n];
    // map vertex -> incident edges indices
    let mut v_to_edges: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for (i, &(u, v)) in edges.iter().enumerate() {
        v_to_edges[u].push(i);
        v_to_edges[v].push(i);
    }
    for (i, &(u, v)) in edges.iter().enumerate() {
        if covered_e[i] { continue; }
        // pick this edge
        cover[u] = true;
        cover[v] = true;
        // mark incident edges
        for &ei in &v_to_edges[u] { covered_e[ei] = true; }
        for &ei in &v_to_edges[v] { covered_e[ei] = true; }
    }
    cover
}

// Remove redundant vertices (if all neighbors are in cover, vertex can be removed)
fn remove_redundant(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, cover: &mut Vec<bool>) -> bool {
    let mut changed = false;
    let mut to_remove = Vec::new();
    for v in 0..n {
        if !cover[v] { continue; }
        let mut can_remove = true;
        for &u in &adj[v] {
            if !cover[u] { can_remove = false; break; }
        }
        if can_remove {
            to_remove.push(v);
        }
    }
    if !to_remove.is_empty() {
        changed = true;
        for v in to_remove { cover[v] = false; }
    }
    changed
}

// Attempt beneficial swaps: add a non-cover vertex v and remove neighbors fully dominated -> net decrease
fn local_swaps(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, cover: &mut Vec<bool>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> bool {
    // returns true if changed
    let mut changed = false;
    for v in 0..n {
        if let Some(tl) = time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { break; } }
        if cover[v] { continue; }
        // If we add v, which cover vertices would become removable?
        // A cover vertex u can be removed if after adding v all neighbors of u are in cover U {v}
        let mut removable = Vec::new();
        for &u in &adj[v] {
            if !cover[u] { continue; }
            // check if u would be redundant after adding v
            let mut ok = true;
            for &w in &adj[u] {
                if w == v { continue; }
                if !cover[w] { ok = false; break; }
            }
            if ok { removable.push(u); }
        }
        // net change = +1 (adding v) - removable.len()
        if removable.len() >= 2 {
            // perform swap
            cover[v] = true;
            for u in removable { cover[u] = false; }
            changed = true;
            // try more improvements after this
            return true;
        }
    }
    changed
}

// Quick validator
fn validate_cover(n: usize, edges: &Vec<(usize, usize)>, cover: &Vec<bool>) -> bool {
    for &(u, v) in edges {
        if !cover[u] && !cover[v] { return false; }
    }
    true
}

// Kernelization: degree-0 and degree-1 rules. Returns forced cover and remaining subgraph mapping
fn kernelize_deg0_deg1(n: usize, mut adj: Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> (Vec<bool>, Vec<usize>, Vec<Vec<usize>>) {
    let mut forced = vec![false; n];
    let mut active = vec![true; n];
    let mut deg = vec![0usize; n];
    for i in 0..n { deg[i] = adj[i].len(); }
    let mut changed = true;
    while changed {
        if let Some(tl) = time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { break; } }
        changed = false;
        // degree-0: remove
        for v in 0..n {
            if !active[v] { continue; }
            if deg[v] == 0 {
                active[v] = false; changed = true;
            }
        }
        // degree-1: include its neighbor
        for v in 0..n {
            if !active[v] { continue; }
            if deg[v] == 1 {
                // find neighbor
                let mut nei = usize::MAX;
                for &u in &adj[v] { if active[u] { nei = u; break; } }
                if nei == usize::MAX { continue; }
                // include neighbor nei in forced cover
                if !forced[nei] {
                    forced[nei] = true;
                }
                // remove nei and its incident edges
                if active[nei] {
                    active[nei] = false;
                    changed = true;
                    for &w in &adj[nei] {
                        if active[w] {
                            deg[w] = deg[w].saturating_sub(1);
                        }
                    }
                    deg[nei] = 0;
                }
                // v might become isolated; remove it too
                if active[v] {
                    active[v] = false;
                    changed = true;
                    for &w in &adj[v] {
                        if active[w] {
                            deg[w] = deg[w].saturating_sub(1);
                        }
                    }
                    deg[v] = 0;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Build mapping old->new for active vertices
    let mut old_to_new = vec![usize::MAX; n];
    let mut new_to_old = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if active[i] {
            old_to_new[i] = new_to_old.len();
            new_to_old.push(i);
        }
    }
    let newn = new_to_old.len();
    let mut new_adj = vec![Vec::new(); newn];
    for (new_idx, &orig) in new_to_old.iter().enumerate() {
        for &w in &adj[orig] {
            if old_to_new[w] != usize::MAX {
                new_adj[new_idx].push(old_to_new[w]);
            }
        }
    }
    for i in 0..newn { new_adj[i].sort_unstable(); new_adj[i].dedup(); }
    (forced, new_to_old, new_adj)
}

// Exact branch-and-bound for n <= 50 using u64 masks
struct ExactSmall {
    n: usize,
    adj_mask: Vec<u64>,
    best: usize,
    best_mask: u64,
    start: Instant,
    time_limit: Option<Instant>,
}

impl ExactSmall {
    fn new(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> Self {
        let mut adj_mask = vec![0u64; n];
        for i in 0..n {
            let mut m = 0u64;
            for &j in &adj[i] { m |= 1u64 << j; }
            adj_mask[i] = m;
        }
        ExactSmall { n, adj_mask, best: n+1, best_mask: 0u64, start: Instant::now(), time_limit }
    }

    fn popcount(x: u64) -> usize { x.count_ones() as usize }

    fn maximal_matching_lb(&self, active: u64) -> usize {
        let mut used = 0u64;
        let mut match_count = 0usize;
        let mut v = 0usize;
        while v < self.n {
            if (active & (1u64<<v)) == 0 || (used & (1u64<<v)) != 0 { v += 1; continue; }
            let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & active;
            let mut found = None;
            let mut u = neighs;
            while u != 0 {
                let l = u.trailing_zeros() as usize;
                if (used & (1u64<<l)) == 0 {
                    found = Some(l); break;
                }
                u &= u - 1;
            }
            if let Some(l) = found {
                used |= (1u64<<v) | (1u64<<l);
                match_count += 1;
            }
            v += 1;
        }
        match_count
    }

    fn reduce(&self, mut active: u64) -> (u64, usize) {
        // apply deg-0 and deg-1 reductions greedily
        let mut added = 0usize;
        loop {
            let mut changed = false;
            for v in 0..self.n {
                if (active & (1u64<<v)) == 0 { continue; }
                let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & active;
                let deg = ExactSmall::popcount(neighs);
                if deg == 0 {
                    active &= !(1u64<<v); changed = true;
                } else if deg == 1 {
                    // include its neighbor
                    let u = neighs.trailing_zeros() as usize;
                    // include u => remove u and v
                    if (active & (1u64<<u)) != 0 {
                        active &= !(1u64<<u);
                        added += 1;
                    }
                    if (active & (1u64<<v)) != 0 {
                        active &= !(1u64<<v);
                    }
                    changed = true;
                }
            }
            if !changed { break; }
        }
        (active, added)
    }

    fn dfs(&mut self, active: u64, current: usize, curr_mask: u64) {
        if let Some(tl) = self.time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { return; } }
        if current >= self.best { return; }
        let (mut a, added) = self.reduce(active);
        let current = current + added;
        if a == 0 {
            // no more vertices
            if current < self.best {
                self.best = current; self.best_mask = curr_mask;
            }
            return;
        }
        // lower bound from maximal matching
        let lb = self.maximal_matching_lb(a);
        if current + lb >= self.best { return; }
        // choose vertex with max degree
        let mut best_v = None;
        let mut best_deg = 0usize;
        let mut tmp = a;
        while tmp != 0 {
            let v = tmp.trailing_zeros() as usize;
            tmp &= tmp - 1;
            let deg = ExactSmall::popcount(self.adj_mask[v] & a);
            if deg > best_deg { best_deg = deg; best_v = Some(v); }
        }
        if best_v.is_none() {
            if current < self.best { self.best = current; self.best_mask = curr_mask; }
            return;
        }
        let v = best_v.unwrap();
        // Branch 1: include v
        self.dfs(a & !(1u64<<v), current + 1, curr_mask | (1u64<<v));
        // Branch 2: exclude v -> include all neighbors
        let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & a;
        let mut to_remove = neighs | (1u64<<v);
        let addcount = ExactSmall::popcount(neighs);
        if current + addcount < self.best {
            self.dfs(a & !to_remove, current + addcount, curr_mask | neighs);
        }
    }
}

fn exact_small_solver(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> Vec<usize> {
    // n <= 50
    let mut solver = ExactSmall::new(n, adj, time_limit);
    let full = if n == 64 { !0u64 } else { (1u64<<n) - 1 };
    solver.best = n + 1;
    solver.dfs(full, 0, 0u64);
    // decode best_mask
    let mut res = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if (solver.best_mask & (1u64<<i)) != 0 { res.push(i); }
    }
    // In case solver didn't find (time cut), fallback to greedy
    if solver.best > n {
        // fallback
        let mut cover_bool = vec![false; n];
        let edges = (0..n).flat_map(|u| adj[u].iter().map(move |&v| (u,v))).collect::<Vec<_>>();
        let approx = approx_2(n, &edges, adj);
        for i in 0..n { if approx[i] { res.push(i); } }
    }
    res
}

fn main() {
    let global_start = Instant::now();
    let (n, _m, edges_raw) = read_input();
    let adj = build_adj(n, &edges_raw);
    let edges = {
        // build unique edge list with u<v
        let mut e2 = Vec::new();
        for (u, v) in edges_raw.iter() {
            let (a,b) = if u <= v { (*u,*v) } else { (*v,*u) };
            if a != b { e2.push((a,b)); }
        }
        e2.sort_unstable(); e2.dedup(); e2
    };

    if edges.is_empty() {
        println!("0\n"); return;
    }

    // global time limit
    let global_time_limit = Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(280); // a bit under 5 minutes

    // 1) if bipartite - solve exactly using matching
    if let Some(color) = bipartite_color(n, &adj) {
        let match_orig = hopcroft_karp(n, &adj, &color);
        let cover = konig_min_vertex_cover(n, &adj, &color, &match_orig);
        let mut cover_sorted = cover.clone(); cover_sorted.sort_unstable();
        println!("{}", cover_sorted.len());
        for (i, v) in cover_sorted.iter().enumerate() {
            if i == cover_sorted.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); }
            else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // adapt based on size
    if n <= 50 {
        let time_limit = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(30)));
        let res = exact_small_solver(n, &adj, time_limit);
        let mut cover = vec![false; n]; for v in res { cover[v] = true; }
        // ensure coverage
        if !validate_cover(n, &edges, &cover) {
            // fallback approx
            let approx = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
            let mut out = Vec::new();
            for i in 0..n { if approx[i] { out.push(i); } }
            println!("{}", out.len());
            for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
                if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
            }
            return;
        }
        let mut out = Vec::new(); for i in 0..n { if cover[i] { out.push(i); } }
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // Kernelize degree-0 and degree-1 for medium/large
    let tl_kernel = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(20)));
    let (mut forced_orig, new_to_old, new_adj) = kernelize_deg0_deg1(n, adj.clone(), tl_kernel);
    let kernel_n = new_adj.len();
    // initialize cover with forced vertices
    let mut cover_bool = vec![false; n];
    for i in 0..n { if forced_orig[i] { cover_bool[i] = true; } }

    if kernel_n == 0 {
        // forced cover already covers all edges
        let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // If kernel small -> exact
    if kernel_n <= 50 {
        // build small adj for kernel mapping
        let mut small_adj = vec![Vec::new(); kernel_n];
        for i in 0..kernel_n { small_adj[i] = new_adj[i].clone(); }
        // solve exact on kernel
        let time_limit = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(40)));
        let kernel_sol = exact_small_solver(kernel_n, &small_adj, time_limit);
        for v in kernel_sol { let orig = new_to_old[v]; cover_bool[orig] = true; }
        // Output
        let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // Otherwise use approximation + local search
    // Build edges for kernel mapping to original indices
    let mut kernel_edges = Vec::new();
    for u in 0..kernel_n {
        for &v in &new_adj[u] { if u < v { kernel_edges.push((new_to_old[u], new_to_old[v])); } }
    }

    // Initial approx on whole graph: better to run approx on original edges
    let approx_cover = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
    for i in 0..n { if approx_cover[i] { cover_bool[i] = true; } }

    // Remove redundants
    let mut changed = true;
    let mut iter = 0;
    while changed && Instant::now() < global_time_limit && iter < 2000 {
        changed = remove_redundant(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool);
        // try local swaps that reduce size
        let tl_swaps = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_millis(200)));
        if local_swaps(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool, tl_swaps) { changed = true; }
        iter += 1;
    }

    // Final fallback: if still not covering (should cover), fix by greedy
    if !validate_cover(n, &edges, &cover_bool) {
        let approx_cover2 = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
        cover_bool = approx_cover2;
        // cleanup
        remove_redundant(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool);
    }

    let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
    println!("{}", out.len());
    for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
        if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
    }
}
Subpass 3
Subpass configurationView exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

[200 vertices, 1K edges] Edge (114,143) not covered

Minimum Vertex Cover - 200 vertices, 1K edges

Approach: The solver adapts to graph size and structure. It first handles trivial cases (no edges). It detects bipartite graphs and uses Hopcroft–Karp plus Kőnig's theorem to produce an exact minimum vertex cover for bipartite graphs (works for any size). For small general graphs (n ≤ 50) it runs an exact branch-and-bound using bitmasks, degree-0/1 reductions, and a maximal-matching lower bound to prune. Fo...

View Rust Code (24457 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read};
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
use std::collections::VecDeque;

fn read_input() -> (usize, usize, Vec<(usize, usize)>) {
    let mut s = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut s).unwrap();
    let mut it = s.split_whitespace();
    let n: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let m: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let mut edges = Vec::with_capacity(m);
    for _ in 0..m {
        let u: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let v: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        if u < n && v < n && u != v {
            edges.push((u, v));
        }
    }
    (n, edges.len(), edges)
}

// Build adjacency list
fn build_adj(n: usize, edges: &[(usize, usize)]) -> Vec<Vec<usize>> {
    let mut adj = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for &(u, v) in edges {
        adj[u].push(v);
        adj[v].push(u);
    }
    for i in 0..n {
        adj[i].sort_unstable();
        adj[i].dedup();
    }
    adj
}

// Check bipartite and return color vec if bipartite (0/1). Otherwise None.
fn bipartite_color(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>) -> Option<Vec<i8>> {
    let mut color = vec![-1i8; n];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    for s in 0..n {
        if color[s] != -1 { continue; }
        color[s] = 0;
        q.push_back(s);
        while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                if color[v] == -1 {
                    color[v] = 1 - color[u];
                    q.push_back(v);
                } else if color[v] == color[u] {
                    return None;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    Some(color)
}

// Hopcroft-Karp for bipartite matching. Left side = vertices with color 0.
fn hopcroft_karp(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, color: &Vec<i8>) -> Vec<Option<usize>> {
    // Map left indices to list of neighbors in right
    let mut left_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut right_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut lcount = 0usize;
    let mut rcount = 0usize;
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_ids[i] = lcount as isize; lcount += 1; }
        else { right_ids[i] = rcount as isize; rcount += 1; }
    }
    let mut g: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); lcount];
    for u in 0..n {
        if color[u] != 0 { continue; }
        let lu = left_ids[u] as usize;
        for &v in &adj[u] {
            // edge u-v where v is right
            let rv = right_ids[v] as usize; // valid because bipartite
            g[lu].push(rv);
        }
    }
    // implement classic HK
    let mut pair_u = vec![None; lcount];
    let mut pair_v = vec![None; rcount];
    let mut dist = vec![0usize; lcount];
    let mut inf = usize::MAX / 4;
    fn bfs(g: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, pair_u: &Vec<Option<usize>>, pair_v: &Vec<Option<usize>>, dist: &mut Vec<usize>) -> bool {
        let mut q = VecDeque::new();
        let mut found = false;
        for u in 0..g.len() {
            if pair_u[u].is_none() {
                dist[u] = 0;
                q.push_back(u);
            } else {
                dist[u] = usize::MAX/4;
            }
        }
        while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
            for &v in &g[u] {
                if let Some(pu) = pair_v[v] {
                    if dist[pu] == usize::MAX/4 {
                        dist[pu] = dist[u] + 1;
                        q.push_back(pu);
                    }
                } else {
                    found = true;
                }
            }
        }
        found
    }
    fn dfs(u: usize, g: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, pair_u: &mut Vec<Option<usize>>, pair_v: &mut Vec<Option<usize>>, dist: &Vec<usize>) -> bool {
        for &v in &g[u] {
            if let Some(pu) = pair_v[v] {
                if dist[pu] != dist[u] + 1 { continue; }
                if dfs(pu, g, pair_u, pair_v, dist) {
                    pair_v[v] = Some(u);
                    pair_u[u] = Some(v);
                    return true;
                }
            } else {
                pair_v[v] = Some(u);
                pair_u[u] = Some(v);
                return true;
            }
        }
        false
    }
    while bfs(&g, &pair_u, &pair_v, &mut dist) {
        for u in 0..g.len() {
            if pair_u[u].is_none() {
                if dfs(u, &g, &mut pair_u, &mut pair_v, &dist) {}
            }
        }
    }
    // Map pair_v into original right vertices
    // Build result match for original vertices: return vector match_of_vertex where Some(partner)
    let mut match_orig = vec![None; n];
    for u in 0..n {
        if color[u] == 0 {
            let lu = left_ids[u] as usize;
            if lu < pair_u.len() {
                if let Some(rv) = pair_u[lu] {
                    // find original right vertex with id rv
                    // search for index where right_ids == rv
                    // build mapping arrays
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Build reverse mapping arrays to convert left/right ids to original indices
    let mut left_to_orig = vec![usize::MAX; lcount];
    let mut right_to_orig = vec![usize::MAX; rcount];
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_to_orig[left_ids[i] as usize] = i; }
        else { right_to_orig[right_ids[i] as usize] = i; }
    }
    // Use pair_u and pair_v to fill match_orig
    for lu in 0..lcount {
        if let Some(rv) = pair_u[lu] {
            let uorig = left_to_orig[lu];
            let vorig = right_to_orig[rv];
            match_orig[uorig] = Some(vorig);
            match_orig[vorig] = Some(uorig);
        }
    }
    match_orig
}

// From bipartite matching result (match_orig: For each vertex, Option(partner) ), derive min vertex cover using Kőnig's theorem
fn konig_min_vertex_cover(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, color: &Vec<i8>, match_orig: &Vec<Option<usize>>) -> Vec<usize> {
    // Build alternation starting from unmatched left vertices, follow alternating paths (edges not in matching from left->right, edges in matching from right->left).
    let mut left_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut right_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut lcount = 0usize;
    let mut rcount = 0usize;
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_ids[i] = lcount as isize; lcount += 1; }
        else { right_ids[i] = rcount as isize; rcount += 1; }
    }
    // build matching arrays pair_u (left->right) and pair_v (right->left)
    let mut pair_u = vec![None; lcount];
    let mut pair_v = vec![None; rcount];
    for i in 0..n {
        if let Some(j) = match_orig[i] {
            if color[i] == 0 {
                pair_u[left_ids[i] as usize] = Some(right_ids[j] as usize);
            } else {
                pair_v[right_ids[i] as usize] = Some(left_ids[j] as usize);
            }
        }
    }
    // BFS/DFS from unmatched left vertices along alternating edges
    let mut vis_left = vec![false; lcount];
    let mut vis_right = vec![false; rcount];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] != 0 { continue; }
        let li = left_ids[i] as usize;
        if pair_u[li].is_none() {
            vis_left[li] = true;
            q.push_back((0usize, li)); // 0: left
        }
    }
    while let Some((side, idx)) = q.pop_front() {
        if side == 0 {
            // from left idx, follow non-matching edges to right
            let u = (0..n).find(|&x| left_ids[x] as usize == idx).unwrap_or(usize::MAX);
            if u == usize::MAX { continue; }
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                let rv = right_ids[v] as usize;
                if !vis_right[rv] {
                    // Only follow edges not in matching? Standard: from left follow all edges to right that are NOT the matched edge.
                    if pair_u[idx] == Some(rv) { continue; }
                    vis_right[rv] = true;
                    q.push_back((1usize, rv));
                }
            }
        } else {
            // from right idx, follow matching edge to left
            if let Some(lj) = pair_v[idx] {
                if !vis_left[lj] {
                    vis_left[lj] = true;
                    q.push_back((0usize, lj));
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Minimum vertex cover = (all left not visited) union (all right visited)
    let mut cover = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 {
            let li = left_ids[i] as usize;
            if !vis_left[li] { cover.push(i); }
        } else {
            let ri = right_ids[i] as usize;
            if vis_right[ri] { cover.push(i); }
        }
    }
    cover
}

// 2-approx greedy: pick an uncovered edge, add both endpoints
fn approx_2(n: usize, edges: &Vec<(usize, usize)>, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>) -> Vec<bool> {
    let mut covered_e = vec![false; edges.len()];
    let mut cover = vec![false; n];
    // map vertex -> incident edges indices
    let mut v_to_edges: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for (i, &(u, v)) in edges.iter().enumerate() {
        v_to_edges[u].push(i);
        v_to_edges[v].push(i);
    }
    for (i, &(u, v)) in edges.iter().enumerate() {
        if covered_e[i] { continue; }
        // pick this edge
        cover[u] = true;
        cover[v] = true;
        // mark incident edges
        for &ei in &v_to_edges[u] { covered_e[ei] = true; }
        for &ei in &v_to_edges[v] { covered_e[ei] = true; }
    }
    cover
}

// Remove redundant vertices (if all neighbors are in cover, vertex can be removed)
fn remove_redundant(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, cover: &mut Vec<bool>) -> bool {
    let mut changed = false;
    let mut to_remove = Vec::new();
    for v in 0..n {
        if !cover[v] { continue; }
        let mut can_remove = true;
        for &u in &adj[v] {
            if !cover[u] { can_remove = false; break; }
        }
        if can_remove {
            to_remove.push(v);
        }
    }
    if !to_remove.is_empty() {
        changed = true;
        for v in to_remove { cover[v] = false; }
    }
    changed
}

// Attempt beneficial swaps: add a non-cover vertex v and remove neighbors fully dominated -> net decrease
fn local_swaps(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, cover: &mut Vec<bool>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> bool {
    // returns true if changed
    let mut changed = false;
    for v in 0..n {
        if let Some(tl) = time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { break; } }
        if cover[v] { continue; }
        // If we add v, which cover vertices would become removable?
        // A cover vertex u can be removed if after adding v all neighbors of u are in cover U {v}
        let mut removable = Vec::new();
        for &u in &adj[v] {
            if !cover[u] { continue; }
            // check if u would be redundant after adding v
            let mut ok = true;
            for &w in &adj[u] {
                if w == v { continue; }
                if !cover[w] { ok = false; break; }
            }
            if ok { removable.push(u); }
        }
        // net change = +1 (adding v) - removable.len()
        if removable.len() >= 2 {
            // perform swap
            cover[v] = true;
            for u in removable { cover[u] = false; }
            changed = true;
            // try more improvements after this
            return true;
        }
    }
    changed
}

// Quick validator
fn validate_cover(n: usize, edges: &Vec<(usize, usize)>, cover: &Vec<bool>) -> bool {
    for &(u, v) in edges {
        if !cover[u] && !cover[v] { return false; }
    }
    true
}

// Kernelization: degree-0 and degree-1 rules. Returns forced cover and remaining subgraph mapping
fn kernelize_deg0_deg1(n: usize, mut adj: Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> (Vec<bool>, Vec<usize>, Vec<Vec<usize>>) {
    let mut forced = vec![false; n];
    let mut active = vec![true; n];
    let mut deg = vec![0usize; n];
    for i in 0..n { deg[i] = adj[i].len(); }
    let mut changed = true;
    while changed {
        if let Some(tl) = time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { break; } }
        changed = false;
        // degree-0: remove
        for v in 0..n {
            if !active[v] { continue; }
            if deg[v] == 0 {
                active[v] = false; changed = true;
            }
        }
        // degree-1: include its neighbor
        for v in 0..n {
            if !active[v] { continue; }
            if deg[v] == 1 {
                // find neighbor
                let mut nei = usize::MAX;
                for &u in &adj[v] { if active[u] { nei = u; break; } }
                if nei == usize::MAX { continue; }
                // include neighbor nei in forced cover
                if !forced[nei] {
                    forced[nei] = true;
                }
                // remove nei and its incident edges
                if active[nei] {
                    active[nei] = false;
                    changed = true;
                    for &w in &adj[nei] {
                        if active[w] {
                            deg[w] = deg[w].saturating_sub(1);
                        }
                    }
                    deg[nei] = 0;
                }
                // v might become isolated; remove it too
                if active[v] {
                    active[v] = false;
                    changed = true;
                    for &w in &adj[v] {
                        if active[w] {
                            deg[w] = deg[w].saturating_sub(1);
                        }
                    }
                    deg[v] = 0;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Build mapping old->new for active vertices
    let mut old_to_new = vec![usize::MAX; n];
    let mut new_to_old = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if active[i] {
            old_to_new[i] = new_to_old.len();
            new_to_old.push(i);
        }
    }
    let newn = new_to_old.len();
    let mut new_adj = vec![Vec::new(); newn];
    for (new_idx, &orig) in new_to_old.iter().enumerate() {
        for &w in &adj[orig] {
            if old_to_new[w] != usize::MAX {
                new_adj[new_idx].push(old_to_new[w]);
            }
        }
    }
    for i in 0..newn { new_adj[i].sort_unstable(); new_adj[i].dedup(); }
    (forced, new_to_old, new_adj)
}

// Exact branch-and-bound for n <= 50 using u64 masks
struct ExactSmall {
    n: usize,
    adj_mask: Vec<u64>,
    best: usize,
    best_mask: u64,
    start: Instant,
    time_limit: Option<Instant>,
}

impl ExactSmall {
    fn new(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> Self {
        let mut adj_mask = vec![0u64; n];
        for i in 0..n {
            let mut m = 0u64;
            for &j in &adj[i] { m |= 1u64 << j; }
            adj_mask[i] = m;
        }
        ExactSmall { n, adj_mask, best: n+1, best_mask: 0u64, start: Instant::now(), time_limit }
    }

    fn popcount(x: u64) -> usize { x.count_ones() as usize }

    fn maximal_matching_lb(&self, active: u64) -> usize {
        let mut used = 0u64;
        let mut match_count = 0usize;
        let mut v = 0usize;
        while v < self.n {
            if (active & (1u64<<v)) == 0 || (used & (1u64<<v)) != 0 { v += 1; continue; }
            let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & active;
            let mut found = None;
            let mut u = neighs;
            while u != 0 {
                let l = u.trailing_zeros() as usize;
                if (used & (1u64<<l)) == 0 {
                    found = Some(l); break;
                }
                u &= u - 1;
            }
            if let Some(l) = found {
                used |= (1u64<<v) | (1u64<<l);
                match_count += 1;
            }
            v += 1;
        }
        match_count
    }

    fn reduce(&self, mut active: u64) -> (u64, usize) {
        // apply deg-0 and deg-1 reductions greedily
        let mut added = 0usize;
        loop {
            let mut changed = false;
            for v in 0..self.n {
                if (active & (1u64<<v)) == 0 { continue; }
                let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & active;
                let deg = ExactSmall::popcount(neighs);
                if deg == 0 {
                    active &= !(1u64<<v); changed = true;
                } else if deg == 1 {
                    // include its neighbor
                    let u = neighs.trailing_zeros() as usize;
                    // include u => remove u and v
                    if (active & (1u64<<u)) != 0 {
                        active &= !(1u64<<u);
                        added += 1;
                    }
                    if (active & (1u64<<v)) != 0 {
                        active &= !(1u64<<v);
                    }
                    changed = true;
                }
            }
            if !changed { break; }
        }
        (active, added)
    }

    fn dfs(&mut self, active: u64, current: usize, curr_mask: u64) {
        if let Some(tl) = self.time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { return; } }
        if current >= self.best { return; }
        let (mut a, added) = self.reduce(active);
        let current = current + added;
        if a == 0 {
            // no more vertices
            if current < self.best {
                self.best = current; self.best_mask = curr_mask;
            }
            return;
        }
        // lower bound from maximal matching
        let lb = self.maximal_matching_lb(a);
        if current + lb >= self.best { return; }
        // choose vertex with max degree
        let mut best_v = None;
        let mut best_deg = 0usize;
        let mut tmp = a;
        while tmp != 0 {
            let v = tmp.trailing_zeros() as usize;
            tmp &= tmp - 1;
            let deg = ExactSmall::popcount(self.adj_mask[v] & a);
            if deg > best_deg { best_deg = deg; best_v = Some(v); }
        }
        if best_v.is_none() {
            if current < self.best { self.best = current; self.best_mask = curr_mask; }
            return;
        }
        let v = best_v.unwrap();
        // Branch 1: include v
        self.dfs(a & !(1u64<<v), current + 1, curr_mask | (1u64<<v));
        // Branch 2: exclude v -> include all neighbors
        let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & a;
        let mut to_remove = neighs | (1u64<<v);
        let addcount = ExactSmall::popcount(neighs);
        if current + addcount < self.best {
            self.dfs(a & !to_remove, current + addcount, curr_mask | neighs);
        }
    }
}

fn exact_small_solver(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> Vec<usize> {
    // n <= 50
    let mut solver = ExactSmall::new(n, adj, time_limit);
    let full = if n == 64 { !0u64 } else { (1u64<<n) - 1 };
    solver.best = n + 1;
    solver.dfs(full, 0, 0u64);
    // decode best_mask
    let mut res = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if (solver.best_mask & (1u64<<i)) != 0 { res.push(i); }
    }
    // In case solver didn't find (time cut), fallback to greedy
    if solver.best > n {
        // fallback
        let mut cover_bool = vec![false; n];
        let edges = (0..n).flat_map(|u| adj[u].iter().map(move |&v| (u,v))).collect::<Vec<_>>();
        let approx = approx_2(n, &edges, adj);
        for i in 0..n { if approx[i] { res.push(i); } }
    }
    res
}

fn main() {
    let global_start = Instant::now();
    let (n, _m, edges_raw) = read_input();
    let adj = build_adj(n, &edges_raw);
    let edges = {
        // build unique edge list with u<v
        let mut e2 = Vec::new();
        for (u, v) in edges_raw.iter() {
            let (a,b) = if u <= v { (*u,*v) } else { (*v,*u) };
            if a != b { e2.push((a,b)); }
        }
        e2.sort_unstable(); e2.dedup(); e2
    };

    if edges.is_empty() {
        println!("0\n"); return;
    }

    // global time limit
    let global_time_limit = Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(280); // a bit under 5 minutes

    // 1) if bipartite - solve exactly using matching
    if let Some(color) = bipartite_color(n, &adj) {
        let match_orig = hopcroft_karp(n, &adj, &color);
        let cover = konig_min_vertex_cover(n, &adj, &color, &match_orig);
        let mut cover_sorted = cover.clone(); cover_sorted.sort_unstable();
        println!("{}", cover_sorted.len());
        for (i, v) in cover_sorted.iter().enumerate() {
            if i == cover_sorted.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); }
            else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // adapt based on size
    if n <= 50 {
        let time_limit = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(30)));
        let res = exact_small_solver(n, &adj, time_limit);
        let mut cover = vec![false; n]; for v in res { cover[v] = true; }
        // ensure coverage
        if !validate_cover(n, &edges, &cover) {
            // fallback approx
            let approx = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
            let mut out = Vec::new();
            for i in 0..n { if approx[i] { out.push(i); } }
            println!("{}", out.len());
            for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
                if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
            }
            return;
        }
        let mut out = Vec::new(); for i in 0..n { if cover[i] { out.push(i); } }
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // Kernelize degree-0 and degree-1 for medium/large
    let tl_kernel = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(20)));
    let (mut forced_orig, new_to_old, new_adj) = kernelize_deg0_deg1(n, adj.clone(), tl_kernel);
    let kernel_n = new_adj.len();
    // initialize cover with forced vertices
    let mut cover_bool = vec![false; n];
    for i in 0..n { if forced_orig[i] { cover_bool[i] = true; } }

    if kernel_n == 0 {
        // forced cover already covers all edges
        let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // If kernel small -> exact
    if kernel_n <= 50 {
        // build small adj for kernel mapping
        let mut small_adj = vec![Vec::new(); kernel_n];
        for i in 0..kernel_n { small_adj[i] = new_adj[i].clone(); }
        // solve exact on kernel
        let time_limit = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(40)));
        let kernel_sol = exact_small_solver(kernel_n, &small_adj, time_limit);
        for v in kernel_sol { let orig = new_to_old[v]; cover_bool[orig] = true; }
        // Output
        let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // Otherwise use approximation + local search
    // Build edges for kernel mapping to original indices
    let mut kernel_edges = Vec::new();
    for u in 0..kernel_n {
        for &v in &new_adj[u] { if u < v { kernel_edges.push((new_to_old[u], new_to_old[v])); } }
    }

    // Initial approx on whole graph: better to run approx on original edges
    let approx_cover = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
    for i in 0..n { if approx_cover[i] { cover_bool[i] = true; } }

    // Remove redundants
    let mut changed = true;
    let mut iter = 0;
    while changed && Instant::now() < global_time_limit && iter < 2000 {
        changed = remove_redundant(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool);
        // try local swaps that reduce size
        let tl_swaps = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_millis(200)));
        if local_swaps(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool, tl_swaps) { changed = true; }
        iter += 1;
    }

    // Final fallback: if still not covering (should cover), fix by greedy
    if !validate_cover(n, &edges, &cover_bool) {
        let approx_cover2 = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
        cover_bool = approx_cover2;
        // cleanup
        remove_redundant(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool);
    }

    let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
    println!("{}", out.len());
    for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
        if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
    }
}
Subpass 4
Subpass configurationView exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

[500 vertices, 3K edges] Edge (156,396) not covered

Minimum Vertex Cover - 500 vertices, 3K edges

Approach: The solver adapts to graph size and structure. It first handles trivial cases (no edges). It detects bipartite graphs and uses Hopcroft–Karp plus Kőnig's theorem to produce an exact minimum vertex cover for bipartite graphs (works for any size). For small general graphs (n ≤ 50) it runs an exact branch-and-bound using bitmasks, degree-0/1 reductions, and a maximal-matching lower bound to prune. Fo...

View Rust Code (24457 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read};
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
use std::collections::VecDeque;

fn read_input() -> (usize, usize, Vec<(usize, usize)>) {
    let mut s = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut s).unwrap();
    let mut it = s.split_whitespace();
    let n: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let m: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let mut edges = Vec::with_capacity(m);
    for _ in 0..m {
        let u: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let v: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        if u < n && v < n && u != v {
            edges.push((u, v));
        }
    }
    (n, edges.len(), edges)
}

// Build adjacency list
fn build_adj(n: usize, edges: &[(usize, usize)]) -> Vec<Vec<usize>> {
    let mut adj = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for &(u, v) in edges {
        adj[u].push(v);
        adj[v].push(u);
    }
    for i in 0..n {
        adj[i].sort_unstable();
        adj[i].dedup();
    }
    adj
}

// Check bipartite and return color vec if bipartite (0/1). Otherwise None.
fn bipartite_color(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>) -> Option<Vec<i8>> {
    let mut color = vec![-1i8; n];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    for s in 0..n {
        if color[s] != -1 { continue; }
        color[s] = 0;
        q.push_back(s);
        while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                if color[v] == -1 {
                    color[v] = 1 - color[u];
                    q.push_back(v);
                } else if color[v] == color[u] {
                    return None;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    Some(color)
}

// Hopcroft-Karp for bipartite matching. Left side = vertices with color 0.
fn hopcroft_karp(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, color: &Vec<i8>) -> Vec<Option<usize>> {
    // Map left indices to list of neighbors in right
    let mut left_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut right_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut lcount = 0usize;
    let mut rcount = 0usize;
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_ids[i] = lcount as isize; lcount += 1; }
        else { right_ids[i] = rcount as isize; rcount += 1; }
    }
    let mut g: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); lcount];
    for u in 0..n {
        if color[u] != 0 { continue; }
        let lu = left_ids[u] as usize;
        for &v in &adj[u] {
            // edge u-v where v is right
            let rv = right_ids[v] as usize; // valid because bipartite
            g[lu].push(rv);
        }
    }
    // implement classic HK
    let mut pair_u = vec![None; lcount];
    let mut pair_v = vec![None; rcount];
    let mut dist = vec![0usize; lcount];
    let mut inf = usize::MAX / 4;
    fn bfs(g: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, pair_u: &Vec<Option<usize>>, pair_v: &Vec<Option<usize>>, dist: &mut Vec<usize>) -> bool {
        let mut q = VecDeque::new();
        let mut found = false;
        for u in 0..g.len() {
            if pair_u[u].is_none() {
                dist[u] = 0;
                q.push_back(u);
            } else {
                dist[u] = usize::MAX/4;
            }
        }
        while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
            for &v in &g[u] {
                if let Some(pu) = pair_v[v] {
                    if dist[pu] == usize::MAX/4 {
                        dist[pu] = dist[u] + 1;
                        q.push_back(pu);
                    }
                } else {
                    found = true;
                }
            }
        }
        found
    }
    fn dfs(u: usize, g: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, pair_u: &mut Vec<Option<usize>>, pair_v: &mut Vec<Option<usize>>, dist: &Vec<usize>) -> bool {
        for &v in &g[u] {
            if let Some(pu) = pair_v[v] {
                if dist[pu] != dist[u] + 1 { continue; }
                if dfs(pu, g, pair_u, pair_v, dist) {
                    pair_v[v] = Some(u);
                    pair_u[u] = Some(v);
                    return true;
                }
            } else {
                pair_v[v] = Some(u);
                pair_u[u] = Some(v);
                return true;
            }
        }
        false
    }
    while bfs(&g, &pair_u, &pair_v, &mut dist) {
        for u in 0..g.len() {
            if pair_u[u].is_none() {
                if dfs(u, &g, &mut pair_u, &mut pair_v, &dist) {}
            }
        }
    }
    // Map pair_v into original right vertices
    // Build result match for original vertices: return vector match_of_vertex where Some(partner)
    let mut match_orig = vec![None; n];
    for u in 0..n {
        if color[u] == 0 {
            let lu = left_ids[u] as usize;
            if lu < pair_u.len() {
                if let Some(rv) = pair_u[lu] {
                    // find original right vertex with id rv
                    // search for index where right_ids == rv
                    // build mapping arrays
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Build reverse mapping arrays to convert left/right ids to original indices
    let mut left_to_orig = vec![usize::MAX; lcount];
    let mut right_to_orig = vec![usize::MAX; rcount];
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_to_orig[left_ids[i] as usize] = i; }
        else { right_to_orig[right_ids[i] as usize] = i; }
    }
    // Use pair_u and pair_v to fill match_orig
    for lu in 0..lcount {
        if let Some(rv) = pair_u[lu] {
            let uorig = left_to_orig[lu];
            let vorig = right_to_orig[rv];
            match_orig[uorig] = Some(vorig);
            match_orig[vorig] = Some(uorig);
        }
    }
    match_orig
}

// From bipartite matching result (match_orig: For each vertex, Option(partner) ), derive min vertex cover using Kőnig's theorem
fn konig_min_vertex_cover(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, color: &Vec<i8>, match_orig: &Vec<Option<usize>>) -> Vec<usize> {
    // Build alternation starting from unmatched left vertices, follow alternating paths (edges not in matching from left->right, edges in matching from right->left).
    let mut left_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut right_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut lcount = 0usize;
    let mut rcount = 0usize;
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_ids[i] = lcount as isize; lcount += 1; }
        else { right_ids[i] = rcount as isize; rcount += 1; }
    }
    // build matching arrays pair_u (left->right) and pair_v (right->left)
    let mut pair_u = vec![None; lcount];
    let mut pair_v = vec![None; rcount];
    for i in 0..n {
        if let Some(j) = match_orig[i] {
            if color[i] == 0 {
                pair_u[left_ids[i] as usize] = Some(right_ids[j] as usize);
            } else {
                pair_v[right_ids[i] as usize] = Some(left_ids[j] as usize);
            }
        }
    }
    // BFS/DFS from unmatched left vertices along alternating edges
    let mut vis_left = vec![false; lcount];
    let mut vis_right = vec![false; rcount];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] != 0 { continue; }
        let li = left_ids[i] as usize;
        if pair_u[li].is_none() {
            vis_left[li] = true;
            q.push_back((0usize, li)); // 0: left
        }
    }
    while let Some((side, idx)) = q.pop_front() {
        if side == 0 {
            // from left idx, follow non-matching edges to right
            let u = (0..n).find(|&x| left_ids[x] as usize == idx).unwrap_or(usize::MAX);
            if u == usize::MAX { continue; }
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                let rv = right_ids[v] as usize;
                if !vis_right[rv] {
                    // Only follow edges not in matching? Standard: from left follow all edges to right that are NOT the matched edge.
                    if pair_u[idx] == Some(rv) { continue; }
                    vis_right[rv] = true;
                    q.push_back((1usize, rv));
                }
            }
        } else {
            // from right idx, follow matching edge to left
            if let Some(lj) = pair_v[idx] {
                if !vis_left[lj] {
                    vis_left[lj] = true;
                    q.push_back((0usize, lj));
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Minimum vertex cover = (all left not visited) union (all right visited)
    let mut cover = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 {
            let li = left_ids[i] as usize;
            if !vis_left[li] { cover.push(i); }
        } else {
            let ri = right_ids[i] as usize;
            if vis_right[ri] { cover.push(i); }
        }
    }
    cover
}

// 2-approx greedy: pick an uncovered edge, add both endpoints
fn approx_2(n: usize, edges: &Vec<(usize, usize)>, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>) -> Vec<bool> {
    let mut covered_e = vec![false; edges.len()];
    let mut cover = vec![false; n];
    // map vertex -> incident edges indices
    let mut v_to_edges: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for (i, &(u, v)) in edges.iter().enumerate() {
        v_to_edges[u].push(i);
        v_to_edges[v].push(i);
    }
    for (i, &(u, v)) in edges.iter().enumerate() {
        if covered_e[i] { continue; }
        // pick this edge
        cover[u] = true;
        cover[v] = true;
        // mark incident edges
        for &ei in &v_to_edges[u] { covered_e[ei] = true; }
        for &ei in &v_to_edges[v] { covered_e[ei] = true; }
    }
    cover
}

// Remove redundant vertices (if all neighbors are in cover, vertex can be removed)
fn remove_redundant(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, cover: &mut Vec<bool>) -> bool {
    let mut changed = false;
    let mut to_remove = Vec::new();
    for v in 0..n {
        if !cover[v] { continue; }
        let mut can_remove = true;
        for &u in &adj[v] {
            if !cover[u] { can_remove = false; break; }
        }
        if can_remove {
            to_remove.push(v);
        }
    }
    if !to_remove.is_empty() {
        changed = true;
        for v in to_remove { cover[v] = false; }
    }
    changed
}

// Attempt beneficial swaps: add a non-cover vertex v and remove neighbors fully dominated -> net decrease
fn local_swaps(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, cover: &mut Vec<bool>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> bool {
    // returns true if changed
    let mut changed = false;
    for v in 0..n {
        if let Some(tl) = time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { break; } }
        if cover[v] { continue; }
        // If we add v, which cover vertices would become removable?
        // A cover vertex u can be removed if after adding v all neighbors of u are in cover U {v}
        let mut removable = Vec::new();
        for &u in &adj[v] {
            if !cover[u] { continue; }
            // check if u would be redundant after adding v
            let mut ok = true;
            for &w in &adj[u] {
                if w == v { continue; }
                if !cover[w] { ok = false; break; }
            }
            if ok { removable.push(u); }
        }
        // net change = +1 (adding v) - removable.len()
        if removable.len() >= 2 {
            // perform swap
            cover[v] = true;
            for u in removable { cover[u] = false; }
            changed = true;
            // try more improvements after this
            return true;
        }
    }
    changed
}

// Quick validator
fn validate_cover(n: usize, edges: &Vec<(usize, usize)>, cover: &Vec<bool>) -> bool {
    for &(u, v) in edges {
        if !cover[u] && !cover[v] { return false; }
    }
    true
}

// Kernelization: degree-0 and degree-1 rules. Returns forced cover and remaining subgraph mapping
fn kernelize_deg0_deg1(n: usize, mut adj: Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> (Vec<bool>, Vec<usize>, Vec<Vec<usize>>) {
    let mut forced = vec![false; n];
    let mut active = vec![true; n];
    let mut deg = vec![0usize; n];
    for i in 0..n { deg[i] = adj[i].len(); }
    let mut changed = true;
    while changed {
        if let Some(tl) = time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { break; } }
        changed = false;
        // degree-0: remove
        for v in 0..n {
            if !active[v] { continue; }
            if deg[v] == 0 {
                active[v] = false; changed = true;
            }
        }
        // degree-1: include its neighbor
        for v in 0..n {
            if !active[v] { continue; }
            if deg[v] == 1 {
                // find neighbor
                let mut nei = usize::MAX;
                for &u in &adj[v] { if active[u] { nei = u; break; } }
                if nei == usize::MAX { continue; }
                // include neighbor nei in forced cover
                if !forced[nei] {
                    forced[nei] = true;
                }
                // remove nei and its incident edges
                if active[nei] {
                    active[nei] = false;
                    changed = true;
                    for &w in &adj[nei] {
                        if active[w] {
                            deg[w] = deg[w].saturating_sub(1);
                        }
                    }
                    deg[nei] = 0;
                }
                // v might become isolated; remove it too
                if active[v] {
                    active[v] = false;
                    changed = true;
                    for &w in &adj[v] {
                        if active[w] {
                            deg[w] = deg[w].saturating_sub(1);
                        }
                    }
                    deg[v] = 0;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Build mapping old->new for active vertices
    let mut old_to_new = vec![usize::MAX; n];
    let mut new_to_old = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if active[i] {
            old_to_new[i] = new_to_old.len();
            new_to_old.push(i);
        }
    }
    let newn = new_to_old.len();
    let mut new_adj = vec![Vec::new(); newn];
    for (new_idx, &orig) in new_to_old.iter().enumerate() {
        for &w in &adj[orig] {
            if old_to_new[w] != usize::MAX {
                new_adj[new_idx].push(old_to_new[w]);
            }
        }
    }
    for i in 0..newn { new_adj[i].sort_unstable(); new_adj[i].dedup(); }
    (forced, new_to_old, new_adj)
}

// Exact branch-and-bound for n <= 50 using u64 masks
struct ExactSmall {
    n: usize,
    adj_mask: Vec<u64>,
    best: usize,
    best_mask: u64,
    start: Instant,
    time_limit: Option<Instant>,
}

impl ExactSmall {
    fn new(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> Self {
        let mut adj_mask = vec![0u64; n];
        for i in 0..n {
            let mut m = 0u64;
            for &j in &adj[i] { m |= 1u64 << j; }
            adj_mask[i] = m;
        }
        ExactSmall { n, adj_mask, best: n+1, best_mask: 0u64, start: Instant::now(), time_limit }
    }

    fn popcount(x: u64) -> usize { x.count_ones() as usize }

    fn maximal_matching_lb(&self, active: u64) -> usize {
        let mut used = 0u64;
        let mut match_count = 0usize;
        let mut v = 0usize;
        while v < self.n {
            if (active & (1u64<<v)) == 0 || (used & (1u64<<v)) != 0 { v += 1; continue; }
            let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & active;
            let mut found = None;
            let mut u = neighs;
            while u != 0 {
                let l = u.trailing_zeros() as usize;
                if (used & (1u64<<l)) == 0 {
                    found = Some(l); break;
                }
                u &= u - 1;
            }
            if let Some(l) = found {
                used |= (1u64<<v) | (1u64<<l);
                match_count += 1;
            }
            v += 1;
        }
        match_count
    }

    fn reduce(&self, mut active: u64) -> (u64, usize) {
        // apply deg-0 and deg-1 reductions greedily
        let mut added = 0usize;
        loop {
            let mut changed = false;
            for v in 0..self.n {
                if (active & (1u64<<v)) == 0 { continue; }
                let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & active;
                let deg = ExactSmall::popcount(neighs);
                if deg == 0 {
                    active &= !(1u64<<v); changed = true;
                } else if deg == 1 {
                    // include its neighbor
                    let u = neighs.trailing_zeros() as usize;
                    // include u => remove u and v
                    if (active & (1u64<<u)) != 0 {
                        active &= !(1u64<<u);
                        added += 1;
                    }
                    if (active & (1u64<<v)) != 0 {
                        active &= !(1u64<<v);
                    }
                    changed = true;
                }
            }
            if !changed { break; }
        }
        (active, added)
    }

    fn dfs(&mut self, active: u64, current: usize, curr_mask: u64) {
        if let Some(tl) = self.time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { return; } }
        if current >= self.best { return; }
        let (mut a, added) = self.reduce(active);
        let current = current + added;
        if a == 0 {
            // no more vertices
            if current < self.best {
                self.best = current; self.best_mask = curr_mask;
            }
            return;
        }
        // lower bound from maximal matching
        let lb = self.maximal_matching_lb(a);
        if current + lb >= self.best { return; }
        // choose vertex with max degree
        let mut best_v = None;
        let mut best_deg = 0usize;
        let mut tmp = a;
        while tmp != 0 {
            let v = tmp.trailing_zeros() as usize;
            tmp &= tmp - 1;
            let deg = ExactSmall::popcount(self.adj_mask[v] & a);
            if deg > best_deg { best_deg = deg; best_v = Some(v); }
        }
        if best_v.is_none() {
            if current < self.best { self.best = current; self.best_mask = curr_mask; }
            return;
        }
        let v = best_v.unwrap();
        // Branch 1: include v
        self.dfs(a & !(1u64<<v), current + 1, curr_mask | (1u64<<v));
        // Branch 2: exclude v -> include all neighbors
        let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & a;
        let mut to_remove = neighs | (1u64<<v);
        let addcount = ExactSmall::popcount(neighs);
        if current + addcount < self.best {
            self.dfs(a & !to_remove, current + addcount, curr_mask | neighs);
        }
    }
}

fn exact_small_solver(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> Vec<usize> {
    // n <= 50
    let mut solver = ExactSmall::new(n, adj, time_limit);
    let full = if n == 64 { !0u64 } else { (1u64<<n) - 1 };
    solver.best = n + 1;
    solver.dfs(full, 0, 0u64);
    // decode best_mask
    let mut res = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if (solver.best_mask & (1u64<<i)) != 0 { res.push(i); }
    }
    // In case solver didn't find (time cut), fallback to greedy
    if solver.best > n {
        // fallback
        let mut cover_bool = vec![false; n];
        let edges = (0..n).flat_map(|u| adj[u].iter().map(move |&v| (u,v))).collect::<Vec<_>>();
        let approx = approx_2(n, &edges, adj);
        for i in 0..n { if approx[i] { res.push(i); } }
    }
    res
}

fn main() {
    let global_start = Instant::now();
    let (n, _m, edges_raw) = read_input();
    let adj = build_adj(n, &edges_raw);
    let edges = {
        // build unique edge list with u<v
        let mut e2 = Vec::new();
        for (u, v) in edges_raw.iter() {
            let (a,b) = if u <= v { (*u,*v) } else { (*v,*u) };
            if a != b { e2.push((a,b)); }
        }
        e2.sort_unstable(); e2.dedup(); e2
    };

    if edges.is_empty() {
        println!("0\n"); return;
    }

    // global time limit
    let global_time_limit = Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(280); // a bit under 5 minutes

    // 1) if bipartite - solve exactly using matching
    if let Some(color) = bipartite_color(n, &adj) {
        let match_orig = hopcroft_karp(n, &adj, &color);
        let cover = konig_min_vertex_cover(n, &adj, &color, &match_orig);
        let mut cover_sorted = cover.clone(); cover_sorted.sort_unstable();
        println!("{}", cover_sorted.len());
        for (i, v) in cover_sorted.iter().enumerate() {
            if i == cover_sorted.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); }
            else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // adapt based on size
    if n <= 50 {
        let time_limit = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(30)));
        let res = exact_small_solver(n, &adj, time_limit);
        let mut cover = vec![false; n]; for v in res { cover[v] = true; }
        // ensure coverage
        if !validate_cover(n, &edges, &cover) {
            // fallback approx
            let approx = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
            let mut out = Vec::new();
            for i in 0..n { if approx[i] { out.push(i); } }
            println!("{}", out.len());
            for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
                if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
            }
            return;
        }
        let mut out = Vec::new(); for i in 0..n { if cover[i] { out.push(i); } }
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // Kernelize degree-0 and degree-1 for medium/large
    let tl_kernel = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(20)));
    let (mut forced_orig, new_to_old, new_adj) = kernelize_deg0_deg1(n, adj.clone(), tl_kernel);
    let kernel_n = new_adj.len();
    // initialize cover with forced vertices
    let mut cover_bool = vec![false; n];
    for i in 0..n { if forced_orig[i] { cover_bool[i] = true; } }

    if kernel_n == 0 {
        // forced cover already covers all edges
        let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // If kernel small -> exact
    if kernel_n <= 50 {
        // build small adj for kernel mapping
        let mut small_adj = vec![Vec::new(); kernel_n];
        for i in 0..kernel_n { small_adj[i] = new_adj[i].clone(); }
        // solve exact on kernel
        let time_limit = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(40)));
        let kernel_sol = exact_small_solver(kernel_n, &small_adj, time_limit);
        for v in kernel_sol { let orig = new_to_old[v]; cover_bool[orig] = true; }
        // Output
        let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // Otherwise use approximation + local search
    // Build edges for kernel mapping to original indices
    let mut kernel_edges = Vec::new();
    for u in 0..kernel_n {
        for &v in &new_adj[u] { if u < v { kernel_edges.push((new_to_old[u], new_to_old[v])); } }
    }

    // Initial approx on whole graph: better to run approx on original edges
    let approx_cover = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
    for i in 0..n { if approx_cover[i] { cover_bool[i] = true; } }

    // Remove redundants
    let mut changed = true;
    let mut iter = 0;
    while changed && Instant::now() < global_time_limit && iter < 2000 {
        changed = remove_redundant(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool);
        // try local swaps that reduce size
        let tl_swaps = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_millis(200)));
        if local_swaps(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool, tl_swaps) { changed = true; }
        iter += 1;
    }

    // Final fallback: if still not covering (should cover), fix by greedy
    if !validate_cover(n, &edges, &cover_bool) {
        let approx_cover2 = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
        cover_bool = approx_cover2;
        // cleanup
        remove_redundant(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool);
    }

    let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
    println!("{}", out.len());
    for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
        if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
    }
}
Subpass 5
Subpass configurationView exact prompt
