[1M vertices, 50M edges] Execution timed out after 30 seconds

Minimum Vertex Cover - 1M vertices, 50M edges

Approach: The solver adapts to graph size and structure. It first handles trivial cases (no edges). It detects bipartite graphs and uses Hopcroft–Karp plus Kőnig's theorem to produce an exact minimum vertex cover for bipartite graphs (works for any size). For small general graphs (n ≤ 50) it runs an exact branch-and-bound using bitmasks, degree-0/1 reductions, and a maximal-matching lower bound to prune. Fo...

View Rust Code (24457 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read};
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
use std::collections::VecDeque;

fn read_input() -> (usize, usize, Vec<(usize, usize)>) {
    let mut s = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut s).unwrap();
    let mut it = s.split_whitespace();
    let n: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let m: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let mut edges = Vec::with_capacity(m);
    for _ in 0..m {
        let u: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let v: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        if u < n && v < n && u != v {
            edges.push((u, v));
        }
    }
    (n, edges.len(), edges)
}

// Build adjacency list
fn build_adj(n: usize, edges: &[(usize, usize)]) -> Vec<Vec<usize>> {
    let mut adj = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for &(u, v) in edges {
        adj[u].push(v);
        adj[v].push(u);
    }
    for i in 0..n {
        adj[i].sort_unstable();
        adj[i].dedup();
    }
    adj
}

// Check bipartite and return color vec if bipartite (0/1). Otherwise None.
fn bipartite_color(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>) -> Option<Vec<i8>> {
    let mut color = vec![-1i8; n];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    for s in 0..n {
        if color[s] != -1 { continue; }
        color[s] = 0;
        q.push_back(s);
        while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                if color[v] == -1 {
                    color[v] = 1 - color[u];
                    q.push_back(v);
                } else if color[v] == color[u] {
                    return None;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    Some(color)
}

// Hopcroft-Karp for bipartite matching. Left side = vertices with color 0.
fn hopcroft_karp(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, color: &Vec<i8>) -> Vec<Option<usize>> {
    // Map left indices to list of neighbors in right
    let mut left_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut right_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut lcount = 0usize;
    let mut rcount = 0usize;
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_ids[i] = lcount as isize; lcount += 1; }
        else { right_ids[i] = rcount as isize; rcount += 1; }
    }
    let mut g: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); lcount];
    for u in 0..n {
        if color[u] != 0 { continue; }
        let lu = left_ids[u] as usize;
        for &v in &adj[u] {
            // edge u-v where v is right
            let rv = right_ids[v] as usize; // valid because bipartite
            g[lu].push(rv);
        }
    }
    // implement classic HK
    let mut pair_u = vec![None; lcount];
    let mut pair_v = vec![None; rcount];
    let mut dist = vec![0usize; lcount];
    let mut inf = usize::MAX / 4;
    fn bfs(g: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, pair_u: &Vec<Option<usize>>, pair_v: &Vec<Option<usize>>, dist: &mut Vec<usize>) -> bool {
        let mut q = VecDeque::new();
        let mut found = false;
        for u in 0..g.len() {
            if pair_u[u].is_none() {
                dist[u] = 0;
                q.push_back(u);
            } else {
                dist[u] = usize::MAX/4;
            }
        }
        while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
            for &v in &g[u] {
                if let Some(pu) = pair_v[v] {
                    if dist[pu] == usize::MAX/4 {
                        dist[pu] = dist[u] + 1;
                        q.push_back(pu);
                    }
                } else {
                    found = true;
                }
            }
        }
        found
    }
    fn dfs(u: usize, g: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, pair_u: &mut Vec<Option<usize>>, pair_v: &mut Vec<Option<usize>>, dist: &Vec<usize>) -> bool {
        for &v in &g[u] {
            if let Some(pu) = pair_v[v] {
                if dist[pu] != dist[u] + 1 { continue; }
                if dfs(pu, g, pair_u, pair_v, dist) {
                    pair_v[v] = Some(u);
                    pair_u[u] = Some(v);
                    return true;
                }
            } else {
                pair_v[v] = Some(u);
                pair_u[u] = Some(v);
                return true;
            }
        }
        false
    }
    while bfs(&g, &pair_u, &pair_v, &mut dist) {
        for u in 0..g.len() {
            if pair_u[u].is_none() {
                if dfs(u, &g, &mut pair_u, &mut pair_v, &dist) {}
            }
        }
    }
    // Map pair_v into original right vertices
    // Build result match for original vertices: return vector match_of_vertex where Some(partner)
    let mut match_orig = vec![None; n];
    for u in 0..n {
        if color[u] == 0 {
            let lu = left_ids[u] as usize;
            if lu < pair_u.len() {
                if let Some(rv) = pair_u[lu] {
                    // find original right vertex with id rv
                    // search for index where right_ids == rv
                    // build mapping arrays
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Build reverse mapping arrays to convert left/right ids to original indices
    let mut left_to_orig = vec![usize::MAX; lcount];
    let mut right_to_orig = vec![usize::MAX; rcount];
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_to_orig[left_ids[i] as usize] = i; }
        else { right_to_orig[right_ids[i] as usize] = i; }
    }
    // Use pair_u and pair_v to fill match_orig
    for lu in 0..lcount {
        if let Some(rv) = pair_u[lu] {
            let uorig = left_to_orig[lu];
            let vorig = right_to_orig[rv];
            match_orig[uorig] = Some(vorig);
            match_orig[vorig] = Some(uorig);
        }
    }
    match_orig
}

// From bipartite matching result (match_orig: For each vertex, Option(partner) ), derive min vertex cover using Kőnig's theorem
fn konig_min_vertex_cover(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, color: &Vec<i8>, match_orig: &Vec<Option<usize>>) -> Vec<usize> {
    // Build alternation starting from unmatched left vertices, follow alternating paths (edges not in matching from left->right, edges in matching from right->left).
    let mut left_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut right_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut lcount = 0usize;
    let mut rcount = 0usize;
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_ids[i] = lcount as isize; lcount += 1; }
        else { right_ids[i] = rcount as isize; rcount += 1; }
    }
    // build matching arrays pair_u (left->right) and pair_v (right->left)
    let mut pair_u = vec![None; lcount];
    let mut pair_v = vec![None; rcount];
    for i in 0..n {
        if let Some(j) = match_orig[i] {
            if color[i] == 0 {
                pair_u[left_ids[i] as usize] = Some(right_ids[j] as usize);
            } else {
                pair_v[right_ids[i] as usize] = Some(left_ids[j] as usize);
            }
        }
    }
    // BFS/DFS from unmatched left vertices along alternating edges
    let mut vis_left = vec![false; lcount];
    let mut vis_right = vec![false; rcount];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] != 0 { continue; }
        let li = left_ids[i] as usize;
        if pair_u[li].is_none() {
            vis_left[li] = true;
            q.push_back((0usize, li)); // 0: left
        }
    }
    while let Some((side, idx)) = q.pop_front() {
        if side == 0 {
            // from left idx, follow non-matching edges to right
            let u = (0..n).find(|&x| left_ids[x] as usize == idx).unwrap_or(usize::MAX);
            if u == usize::MAX { continue; }
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                let rv = right_ids[v] as usize;
                if !vis_right[rv] {
                    // Only follow edges not in matching? Standard: from left follow all edges to right that are NOT the matched edge.
                    if pair_u[idx] == Some(rv) { continue; }
                    vis_right[rv] = true;
                    q.push_back((1usize, rv));
                }
            }
        } else {
            // from right idx, follow matching edge to left
            if let Some(lj) = pair_v[idx] {
                if !vis_left[lj] {
                    vis_left[lj] = true;
                    q.push_back((0usize, lj));
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Minimum vertex cover = (all left not visited) union (all right visited)
    let mut cover = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 {
            let li = left_ids[i] as usize;
            if !vis_left[li] { cover.push(i); }
        } else {
            let ri = right_ids[i] as usize;
            if vis_right[ri] { cover.push(i); }
        }
    }
    cover
}

// 2-approx greedy: pick an uncovered edge, add both endpoints
fn approx_2(n: usize, edges: &Vec<(usize, usize)>, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>) -> Vec<bool> {
    let mut covered_e = vec![false; edges.len()];
    let mut cover = vec![false; n];
    // map vertex -> incident edges indices
    let mut v_to_edges: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for (i, &(u, v)) in edges.iter().enumerate() {
        v_to_edges[u].push(i);
        v_to_edges[v].push(i);
    }
    for (i, &(u, v)) in edges.iter().enumerate() {
        if covered_e[i] { continue; }
        // pick this edge
        cover[u] = true;
        cover[v] = true;
        // mark incident edges
        for &ei in &v_to_edges[u] { covered_e[ei] = true; }
        for &ei in &v_to_edges[v] { covered_e[ei] = true; }
    }
    cover
}

// Remove redundant vertices (if all neighbors are in cover, vertex can be removed)
fn remove_redundant(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, cover: &mut Vec<bool>) -> bool {
    let mut changed = false;
    let mut to_remove = Vec::new();
    for v in 0..n {
        if !cover[v] { continue; }
        let mut can_remove = true;
        for &u in &adj[v] {
            if !cover[u] { can_remove = false; break; }
        }
        if can_remove {
            to_remove.push(v);
        }
    }
    if !to_remove.is_empty() {
        changed = true;
        for v in to_remove { cover[v] = false; }
    }
    changed
}

// Attempt beneficial swaps: add a non-cover vertex v and remove neighbors fully dominated -> net decrease
fn local_swaps(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, cover: &mut Vec<bool>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> bool {
    // returns true if changed
    let mut changed = false;
    for v in 0..n {
        if let Some(tl) = time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { break; } }
        if cover[v] { continue; }
        // If we add v, which cover vertices would become removable?
        // A cover vertex u can be removed if after adding v all neighbors of u are in cover U {v}
        let mut removable = Vec::new();
        for &u in &adj[v] {
            if !cover[u] { continue; }
            // check if u would be redundant after adding v
            let mut ok = true;
            for &w in &adj[u] {
                if w == v { continue; }
                if !cover[w] { ok = false; break; }
            }
            if ok { removable.push(u); }
        }
        // net change = +1 (adding v) - removable.len()
        if removable.len() >= 2 {
            // perform swap
            cover[v] = true;
            for u in removable { cover[u] = false; }
            changed = true;
            // try more improvements after this
            return true;
        }
    }
    changed
}

// Quick validator
fn validate_cover(n: usize, edges: &Vec<(usize, usize)>, cover: &Vec<bool>) -> bool {
    for &(u, v) in edges {
        if !cover[u] && !cover[v] { return false; }
    }
    true
}

// Kernelization: degree-0 and degree-1 rules. Returns forced cover and remaining subgraph mapping
fn kernelize_deg0_deg1(n: usize, mut adj: Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> (Vec<bool>, Vec<usize>, Vec<Vec<usize>>) {
    let mut forced = vec![false; n];
    let mut active = vec![true; n];
    let mut deg = vec![0usize; n];
    for i in 0..n { deg[i] = adj[i].len(); }
    let mut changed = true;
    while changed {
        if let Some(tl) = time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { break; } }
        changed = false;
        // degree-0: remove
        for v in 0..n {
            if !active[v] { continue; }
            if deg[v] == 0 {
                active[v] = false; changed = true;
            }
        }
        // degree-1: include its neighbor
        for v in 0..n {
            if !active[v] { continue; }
            if deg[v] == 1 {
                // find neighbor
                let mut nei = usize::MAX;
                for &u in &adj[v] { if active[u] { nei = u; break; } }
                if nei == usize::MAX { continue; }
                // include neighbor nei in forced cover
                if !forced[nei] {
                    forced[nei] = true;
                }
                // remove nei and its incident edges
                if active[nei] {
                    active[nei] = false;
                    changed = true;
                    for &w in &adj[nei] {
                        if active[w] {
                            deg[w] = deg[w].saturating_sub(1);
                        }
                    }
                    deg[nei] = 0;
                }
                // v might become isolated; remove it too
                if active[v] {
                    active[v] = false;
                    changed = true;
                    for &w in &adj[v] {
                        if active[w] {
                            deg[w] = deg[w].saturating_sub(1);
                        }
                    }
                    deg[v] = 0;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Build mapping old->new for active vertices
    let mut old_to_new = vec![usize::MAX; n];
    let mut new_to_old = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if active[i] {
            old_to_new[i] = new_to_old.len();
            new_to_old.push(i);
        }
    }
    let newn = new_to_old.len();
    let mut new_adj = vec![Vec::new(); newn];
    for (new_idx, &orig) in new_to_old.iter().enumerate() {
        for &w in &adj[orig] {
            if old_to_new[w] != usize::MAX {
                new_adj[new_idx].push(old_to_new[w]);
            }
        }
    }
    for i in 0..newn { new_adj[i].sort_unstable(); new_adj[i].dedup(); }
    (forced, new_to_old, new_adj)
}

// Exact branch-and-bound for n <= 50 using u64 masks
struct ExactSmall {
    n: usize,
    adj_mask: Vec<u64>,
    best: usize,
    best_mask: u64,
    start: Instant,
    time_limit: Option<Instant>,
}

impl ExactSmall {
    fn new(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> Self {
        let mut adj_mask = vec![0u64; n];
        for i in 0..n {
            let mut m = 0u64;
            for &j in &adj[i] { m |= 1u64 << j; }
            adj_mask[i] = m;
        }
        ExactSmall { n, adj_mask, best: n+1, best_mask: 0u64, start: Instant::now(), time_limit }
    }

    fn popcount(x: u64) -> usize { x.count_ones() as usize }

    fn maximal_matching_lb(&self, active: u64) -> usize {
        let mut used = 0u64;
        let mut match_count = 0usize;
        let mut v = 0usize;
        while v < self.n {
            if (active & (1u64<<v)) == 0 || (used & (1u64<<v)) != 0 { v += 1; continue; }
            let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & active;
            let mut found = None;
            let mut u = neighs;
            while u != 0 {
                let l = u.trailing_zeros() as usize;
                if (used & (1u64<<l)) == 0 {
                    found = Some(l); break;
                }
                u &= u - 1;
            }
            if let Some(l) = found {
                used |= (1u64<<v) | (1u64<<l);
                match_count += 1;
            }
            v += 1;
        }
        match_count
    }

    fn reduce(&self, mut active: u64) -> (u64, usize) {
        // apply deg-0 and deg-1 reductions greedily
        let mut added = 0usize;
        loop {
            let mut changed = false;
            for v in 0..self.n {
                if (active & (1u64<<v)) == 0 { continue; }
                let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & active;
                let deg = ExactSmall::popcount(neighs);
                if deg == 0 {
                    active &= !(1u64<<v); changed = true;
                } else if deg == 1 {
                    // include its neighbor
                    let u = neighs.trailing_zeros() as usize;
                    // include u => remove u and v
                    if (active & (1u64<<u)) != 0 {
                        active &= !(1u64<<u);
                        added += 1;
                    }
                    if (active & (1u64<<v)) != 0 {
                        active &= !(1u64<<v);
                    }
                    changed = true;
                }
            }
            if !changed { break; }
        }
        (active, added)
    }

    fn dfs(&mut self, active: u64, current: usize, curr_mask: u64) {
        if let Some(tl) = self.time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { return; } }
        if current >= self.best { return; }
        let (mut a, added) = self.reduce(active);
        let current = current + added;
        if a == 0 {
            // no more vertices
            if current < self.best {
                self.best = current; self.best_mask = curr_mask;
            }
            return;
        }
        // lower bound from maximal matching
        let lb = self.maximal_matching_lb(a);
        if current + lb >= self.best { return; }
        // choose vertex with max degree
        let mut best_v = None;
        let mut best_deg = 0usize;
        let mut tmp = a;
        while tmp != 0 {
            let v = tmp.trailing_zeros() as usize;
            tmp &= tmp - 1;
            let deg = ExactSmall::popcount(self.adj_mask[v] & a);
            if deg > best_deg { best_deg = deg; best_v = Some(v); }
        }
        if best_v.is_none() {
            if current < self.best { self.best = current; self.best_mask = curr_mask; }
            return;
        }
        let v = best_v.unwrap();
        // Branch 1: include v
        self.dfs(a & !(1u64<<v), current + 1, curr_mask | (1u64<<v));
        // Branch 2: exclude v -> include all neighbors
        let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & a;
        let mut to_remove = neighs | (1u64<<v);
        let addcount = ExactSmall::popcount(neighs);
        if current + addcount < self.best {
            self.dfs(a & !to_remove, current + addcount, curr_mask | neighs);
        }
    }
}

fn exact_small_solver(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> Vec<usize> {
    // n <= 50
    let mut solver = ExactSmall::new(n, adj, time_limit);
    let full = if n == 64 { !0u64 } else { (1u64<<n) - 1 };
    solver.best = n + 1;
    solver.dfs(full, 0, 0u64);
    // decode best_mask
    let mut res = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if (solver.best_mask & (1u64<<i)) != 0 { res.push(i); }
    }
    // In case solver didn't find (time cut), fallback to greedy
    if solver.best > n {
        // fallback
        let mut cover_bool = vec![false; n];
        let edges = (0..n).flat_map(|u| adj[u].iter().map(move |&v| (u,v))).collect::<Vec<_>>();
        let approx = approx_2(n, &edges, adj);
        for i in 0..n { if approx[i] { res.push(i); } }
    }
    res
}

fn main() {
    let global_start = Instant::now();
    let (n, _m, edges_raw) = read_input();
    let adj = build_adj(n, &edges_raw);
    let edges = {
        // build unique edge list with u<v
        let mut e2 = Vec::new();
        for (u, v) in edges_raw.iter() {
            let (a,b) = if u <= v { (*u,*v) } else { (*v,*u) };
            if a != b { e2.push((a,b)); }
        }
        e2.sort_unstable(); e2.dedup(); e2
    };

    if edges.is_empty() {
        println!("0\n"); return;
    }

    // global time limit
    let global_time_limit = Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(280); // a bit under 5 minutes

    // 1) if bipartite - solve exactly using matching
    if let Some(color) = bipartite_color(n, &adj) {
        let match_orig = hopcroft_karp(n, &adj, &color);
        let cover = konig_min_vertex_cover(n, &adj, &color, &match_orig);
        let mut cover_sorted = cover.clone(); cover_sorted.sort_unstable();
        println!("{}", cover_sorted.len());
        for (i, v) in cover_sorted.iter().enumerate() {
            if i == cover_sorted.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); }
            else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // adapt based on size
    if n <= 50 {
        let time_limit = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(30)));
        let res = exact_small_solver(n, &adj, time_limit);
        let mut cover = vec![false; n]; for v in res { cover[v] = true; }
        // ensure coverage
        if !validate_cover(n, &edges, &cover) {
            // fallback approx
            let approx = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
            let mut out = Vec::new();
            for i in 0..n { if approx[i] { out.push(i); } }
            println!("{}", out.len());
            for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
                if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
            }
            return;
        }
        let mut out = Vec::new(); for i in 0..n { if cover[i] { out.push(i); } }
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // Kernelize degree-0 and degree-1 for medium/large
    let tl_kernel = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(20)));
    let (mut forced_orig, new_to_old, new_adj) = kernelize_deg0_deg1(n, adj.clone(), tl_kernel);
    let kernel_n = new_adj.len();
    // initialize cover with forced vertices
    let mut cover_bool = vec![false; n];
    for i in 0..n { if forced_orig[i] { cover_bool[i] = true; } }

    if kernel_n == 0 {
        // forced cover already covers all edges
        let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // If kernel small -> exact
    if kernel_n <= 50 {
        // build small adj for kernel mapping
        let mut small_adj = vec![Vec::new(); kernel_n];
        for i in 0..kernel_n { small_adj[i] = new_adj[i].clone(); }
        // solve exact on kernel
        let time_limit = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(40)));
        let kernel_sol = exact_small_solver(kernel_n, &small_adj, time_limit);
        for v in kernel_sol { let orig = new_to_old[v]; cover_bool[orig] = true; }
        // Output
        let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // Otherwise use approximation + local search
    // Build edges for kernel mapping to original indices
    let mut kernel_edges = Vec::new();
    for u in 0..kernel_n {
        for &v in &new_adj[u] { if u < v { kernel_edges.push((new_to_old[u], new_to_old[v])); } }
    }

    // Initial approx on whole graph: better to run approx on original edges
    let approx_cover = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
    for i in 0..n { if approx_cover[i] { cover_bool[i] = true; } }

    // Remove redundants
    let mut changed = true;
    let mut iter = 0;
    while changed && Instant::now() < global_time_limit && iter < 2000 {
        changed = remove_redundant(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool);
        // try local swaps that reduce size
        let tl_swaps = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_millis(200)));
        if local_swaps(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool, tl_swaps) { changed = true; }
        iter += 1;
    }

    // Final fallback: if still not covering (should cover), fix by greedy
    if !validate_cover(n, &edges, &cover_bool) {
        let approx_cover2 = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
        cover_bool = approx_cover2;
        // cleanup
        remove_redundant(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool);
    }

    let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
    println!("{}", out.len());
    for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
        if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
    }
}
Subpass 14
Subpass configurationView exact prompt
View raw AI output

[2M vertices, 150M edges (>1GB)] Execution timed out after 30 seconds

Minimum Vertex Cover - 2M vertices, 150M edges (>1GB)

Approach: The solver adapts to graph size and structure. It first handles trivial cases (no edges). It detects bipartite graphs and uses Hopcroft–Karp plus Kőnig's theorem to produce an exact minimum vertex cover for bipartite graphs (works for any size). For small general graphs (n ≤ 50) it runs an exact branch-and-bound using bitmasks, degree-0/1 reductions, and a maximal-matching lower bound to prune. Fo...

View Rust Code (24457 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read};
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
use std::collections::VecDeque;

fn read_input() -> (usize, usize, Vec<(usize, usize)>) {
    let mut s = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut s).unwrap();
    let mut it = s.split_whitespace();
    let n: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let m: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let mut edges = Vec::with_capacity(m);
    for _ in 0..m {
        let u: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let v: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        if u < n && v < n && u != v {
            edges.push((u, v));
        }
    }
    (n, edges.len(), edges)
}

// Build adjacency list
fn build_adj(n: usize, edges: &[(usize, usize)]) -> Vec<Vec<usize>> {
    let mut adj = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for &(u, v) in edges {
        adj[u].push(v);
        adj[v].push(u);
    }
    for i in 0..n {
        adj[i].sort_unstable();
        adj[i].dedup();
    }
    adj
}

// Check bipartite and return color vec if bipartite (0/1). Otherwise None.
fn bipartite_color(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>) -> Option<Vec<i8>> {
    let mut color = vec![-1i8; n];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    for s in 0..n {
        if color[s] != -1 { continue; }
        color[s] = 0;
        q.push_back(s);
        while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                if color[v] == -1 {
                    color[v] = 1 - color[u];
                    q.push_back(v);
                } else if color[v] == color[u] {
                    return None;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    Some(color)
}

// Hopcroft-Karp for bipartite matching. Left side = vertices with color 0.
fn hopcroft_karp(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, color: &Vec<i8>) -> Vec<Option<usize>> {
    // Map left indices to list of neighbors in right
    let mut left_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut right_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut lcount = 0usize;
    let mut rcount = 0usize;
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_ids[i] = lcount as isize; lcount += 1; }
        else { right_ids[i] = rcount as isize; rcount += 1; }
    }
    let mut g: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); lcount];
    for u in 0..n {
        if color[u] != 0 { continue; }
        let lu = left_ids[u] as usize;
        for &v in &adj[u] {
            // edge u-v where v is right
            let rv = right_ids[v] as usize; // valid because bipartite
            g[lu].push(rv);
        }
    }
    // implement classic HK
    let mut pair_u = vec![None; lcount];
    let mut pair_v = vec![None; rcount];
    let mut dist = vec![0usize; lcount];
    let mut inf = usize::MAX / 4;
    fn bfs(g: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, pair_u: &Vec<Option<usize>>, pair_v: &Vec<Option<usize>>, dist: &mut Vec<usize>) -> bool {
        let mut q = VecDeque::new();
        let mut found = false;
        for u in 0..g.len() {
            if pair_u[u].is_none() {
                dist[u] = 0;
                q.push_back(u);
            } else {
                dist[u] = usize::MAX/4;
            }
        }
        while let Some(u) = q.pop_front() {
            for &v in &g[u] {
                if let Some(pu) = pair_v[v] {
                    if dist[pu] == usize::MAX/4 {
                        dist[pu] = dist[u] + 1;
                        q.push_back(pu);
                    }
                } else {
                    found = true;
                }
            }
        }
        found
    }
    fn dfs(u: usize, g: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, pair_u: &mut Vec<Option<usize>>, pair_v: &mut Vec<Option<usize>>, dist: &Vec<usize>) -> bool {
        for &v in &g[u] {
            if let Some(pu) = pair_v[v] {
                if dist[pu] != dist[u] + 1 { continue; }
                if dfs(pu, g, pair_u, pair_v, dist) {
                    pair_v[v] = Some(u);
                    pair_u[u] = Some(v);
                    return true;
                }
            } else {
                pair_v[v] = Some(u);
                pair_u[u] = Some(v);
                return true;
            }
        }
        false
    }
    while bfs(&g, &pair_u, &pair_v, &mut dist) {
        for u in 0..g.len() {
            if pair_u[u].is_none() {
                if dfs(u, &g, &mut pair_u, &mut pair_v, &dist) {}
            }
        }
    }
    // Map pair_v into original right vertices
    // Build result match for original vertices: return vector match_of_vertex where Some(partner)
    let mut match_orig = vec![None; n];
    for u in 0..n {
        if color[u] == 0 {
            let lu = left_ids[u] as usize;
            if lu < pair_u.len() {
                if let Some(rv) = pair_u[lu] {
                    // find original right vertex with id rv
                    // search for index where right_ids == rv
                    // build mapping arrays
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Build reverse mapping arrays to convert left/right ids to original indices
    let mut left_to_orig = vec![usize::MAX; lcount];
    let mut right_to_orig = vec![usize::MAX; rcount];
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_to_orig[left_ids[i] as usize] = i; }
        else { right_to_orig[right_ids[i] as usize] = i; }
    }
    // Use pair_u and pair_v to fill match_orig
    for lu in 0..lcount {
        if let Some(rv) = pair_u[lu] {
            let uorig = left_to_orig[lu];
            let vorig = right_to_orig[rv];
            match_orig[uorig] = Some(vorig);
            match_orig[vorig] = Some(uorig);
        }
    }
    match_orig
}

// From bipartite matching result (match_orig: For each vertex, Option(partner) ), derive min vertex cover using Kőnig's theorem
fn konig_min_vertex_cover(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, color: &Vec<i8>, match_orig: &Vec<Option<usize>>) -> Vec<usize> {
    // Build alternation starting from unmatched left vertices, follow alternating paths (edges not in matching from left->right, edges in matching from right->left).
    let mut left_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut right_ids = vec![-1isize; n];
    let mut lcount = 0usize;
    let mut rcount = 0usize;
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 { left_ids[i] = lcount as isize; lcount += 1; }
        else { right_ids[i] = rcount as isize; rcount += 1; }
    }
    // build matching arrays pair_u (left->right) and pair_v (right->left)
    let mut pair_u = vec![None; lcount];
    let mut pair_v = vec![None; rcount];
    for i in 0..n {
        if let Some(j) = match_orig[i] {
            if color[i] == 0 {
                pair_u[left_ids[i] as usize] = Some(right_ids[j] as usize);
            } else {
                pair_v[right_ids[i] as usize] = Some(left_ids[j] as usize);
            }
        }
    }
    // BFS/DFS from unmatched left vertices along alternating edges
    let mut vis_left = vec![false; lcount];
    let mut vis_right = vec![false; rcount];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] != 0 { continue; }
        let li = left_ids[i] as usize;
        if pair_u[li].is_none() {
            vis_left[li] = true;
            q.push_back((0usize, li)); // 0: left
        }
    }
    while let Some((side, idx)) = q.pop_front() {
        if side == 0 {
            // from left idx, follow non-matching edges to right
            let u = (0..n).find(|&x| left_ids[x] as usize == idx).unwrap_or(usize::MAX);
            if u == usize::MAX { continue; }
            for &v in &adj[u] {
                let rv = right_ids[v] as usize;
                if !vis_right[rv] {
                    // Only follow edges not in matching? Standard: from left follow all edges to right that are NOT the matched edge.
                    if pair_u[idx] == Some(rv) { continue; }
                    vis_right[rv] = true;
                    q.push_back((1usize, rv));
                }
            }
        } else {
            // from right idx, follow matching edge to left
            if let Some(lj) = pair_v[idx] {
                if !vis_left[lj] {
                    vis_left[lj] = true;
                    q.push_back((0usize, lj));
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Minimum vertex cover = (all left not visited) union (all right visited)
    let mut cover = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if color[i] == 0 {
            let li = left_ids[i] as usize;
            if !vis_left[li] { cover.push(i); }
        } else {
            let ri = right_ids[i] as usize;
            if vis_right[ri] { cover.push(i); }
        }
    }
    cover
}

// 2-approx greedy: pick an uncovered edge, add both endpoints
fn approx_2(n: usize, edges: &Vec<(usize, usize)>, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>) -> Vec<bool> {
    let mut covered_e = vec![false; edges.len()];
    let mut cover = vec![false; n];
    // map vertex -> incident edges indices
    let mut v_to_edges: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); n];
    for (i, &(u, v)) in edges.iter().enumerate() {
        v_to_edges[u].push(i);
        v_to_edges[v].push(i);
    }
    for (i, &(u, v)) in edges.iter().enumerate() {
        if covered_e[i] { continue; }
        // pick this edge
        cover[u] = true;
        cover[v] = true;
        // mark incident edges
        for &ei in &v_to_edges[u] { covered_e[ei] = true; }
        for &ei in &v_to_edges[v] { covered_e[ei] = true; }
    }
    cover
}

// Remove redundant vertices (if all neighbors are in cover, vertex can be removed)
fn remove_redundant(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, cover: &mut Vec<bool>) -> bool {
    let mut changed = false;
    let mut to_remove = Vec::new();
    for v in 0..n {
        if !cover[v] { continue; }
        let mut can_remove = true;
        for &u in &adj[v] {
            if !cover[u] { can_remove = false; break; }
        }
        if can_remove {
            to_remove.push(v);
        }
    }
    if !to_remove.is_empty() {
        changed = true;
        for v in to_remove { cover[v] = false; }
    }
    changed
}

// Attempt beneficial swaps: add a non-cover vertex v and remove neighbors fully dominated -> net decrease
fn local_swaps(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, cover: &mut Vec<bool>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> bool {
    // returns true if changed
    let mut changed = false;
    for v in 0..n {
        if let Some(tl) = time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { break; } }
        if cover[v] { continue; }
        // If we add v, which cover vertices would become removable?
        // A cover vertex u can be removed if after adding v all neighbors of u are in cover U {v}
        let mut removable = Vec::new();
        for &u in &adj[v] {
            if !cover[u] { continue; }
            // check if u would be redundant after adding v
            let mut ok = true;
            for &w in &adj[u] {
                if w == v { continue; }
                if !cover[w] { ok = false; break; }
            }
            if ok { removable.push(u); }
        }
        // net change = +1 (adding v) - removable.len()
        if removable.len() >= 2 {
            // perform swap
            cover[v] = true;
            for u in removable { cover[u] = false; }
            changed = true;
            // try more improvements after this
            return true;
        }
    }
    changed
}

// Quick validator
fn validate_cover(n: usize, edges: &Vec<(usize, usize)>, cover: &Vec<bool>) -> bool {
    for &(u, v) in edges {
        if !cover[u] && !cover[v] { return false; }
    }
    true
}

// Kernelization: degree-0 and degree-1 rules. Returns forced cover and remaining subgraph mapping
fn kernelize_deg0_deg1(n: usize, mut adj: Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> (Vec<bool>, Vec<usize>, Vec<Vec<usize>>) {
    let mut forced = vec![false; n];
    let mut active = vec![true; n];
    let mut deg = vec![0usize; n];
    for i in 0..n { deg[i] = adj[i].len(); }
    let mut changed = true;
    while changed {
        if let Some(tl) = time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { break; } }
        changed = false;
        // degree-0: remove
        for v in 0..n {
            if !active[v] { continue; }
            if deg[v] == 0 {
                active[v] = false; changed = true;
            }
        }
        // degree-1: include its neighbor
        for v in 0..n {
            if !active[v] { continue; }
            if deg[v] == 1 {
                // find neighbor
                let mut nei = usize::MAX;
                for &u in &adj[v] { if active[u] { nei = u; break; } }
                if nei == usize::MAX { continue; }
                // include neighbor nei in forced cover
                if !forced[nei] {
                    forced[nei] = true;
                }
                // remove nei and its incident edges
                if active[nei] {
                    active[nei] = false;
                    changed = true;
                    for &w in &adj[nei] {
                        if active[w] {
                            deg[w] = deg[w].saturating_sub(1);
                        }
                    }
                    deg[nei] = 0;
                }
                // v might become isolated; remove it too
                if active[v] {
                    active[v] = false;
                    changed = true;
                    for &w in &adj[v] {
                        if active[w] {
                            deg[w] = deg[w].saturating_sub(1);
                        }
                    }
                    deg[v] = 0;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    // Build mapping old->new for active vertices
    let mut old_to_new = vec![usize::MAX; n];
    let mut new_to_old = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if active[i] {
            old_to_new[i] = new_to_old.len();
            new_to_old.push(i);
        }
    }
    let newn = new_to_old.len();
    let mut new_adj = vec![Vec::new(); newn];
    for (new_idx, &orig) in new_to_old.iter().enumerate() {
        for &w in &adj[orig] {
            if old_to_new[w] != usize::MAX {
                new_adj[new_idx].push(old_to_new[w]);
            }
        }
    }
    for i in 0..newn { new_adj[i].sort_unstable(); new_adj[i].dedup(); }
    (forced, new_to_old, new_adj)
}

// Exact branch-and-bound for n <= 50 using u64 masks
struct ExactSmall {
    n: usize,
    adj_mask: Vec<u64>,
    best: usize,
    best_mask: u64,
    start: Instant,
    time_limit: Option<Instant>,
}

impl ExactSmall {
    fn new(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> Self {
        let mut adj_mask = vec![0u64; n];
        for i in 0..n {
            let mut m = 0u64;
            for &j in &adj[i] { m |= 1u64 << j; }
            adj_mask[i] = m;
        }
        ExactSmall { n, adj_mask, best: n+1, best_mask: 0u64, start: Instant::now(), time_limit }
    }

    fn popcount(x: u64) -> usize { x.count_ones() as usize }

    fn maximal_matching_lb(&self, active: u64) -> usize {
        let mut used = 0u64;
        let mut match_count = 0usize;
        let mut v = 0usize;
        while v < self.n {
            if (active & (1u64<<v)) == 0 || (used & (1u64<<v)) != 0 { v += 1; continue; }
            let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & active;
            let mut found = None;
            let mut u = neighs;
            while u != 0 {
                let l = u.trailing_zeros() as usize;
                if (used & (1u64<<l)) == 0 {
                    found = Some(l); break;
                }
                u &= u - 1;
            }
            if let Some(l) = found {
                used |= (1u64<<v) | (1u64<<l);
                match_count += 1;
            }
            v += 1;
        }
        match_count
    }

    fn reduce(&self, mut active: u64) -> (u64, usize) {
        // apply deg-0 and deg-1 reductions greedily
        let mut added = 0usize;
        loop {
            let mut changed = false;
            for v in 0..self.n {
                if (active & (1u64<<v)) == 0 { continue; }
                let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & active;
                let deg = ExactSmall::popcount(neighs);
                if deg == 0 {
                    active &= !(1u64<<v); changed = true;
                } else if deg == 1 {
                    // include its neighbor
                    let u = neighs.trailing_zeros() as usize;
                    // include u => remove u and v
                    if (active & (1u64<<u)) != 0 {
                        active &= !(1u64<<u);
                        added += 1;
                    }
                    if (active & (1u64<<v)) != 0 {
                        active &= !(1u64<<v);
                    }
                    changed = true;
                }
            }
            if !changed { break; }
        }
        (active, added)
    }

    fn dfs(&mut self, active: u64, current: usize, curr_mask: u64) {
        if let Some(tl) = self.time_limit { if Instant::now() > tl { return; } }
        if current >= self.best { return; }
        let (mut a, added) = self.reduce(active);
        let current = current + added;
        if a == 0 {
            // no more vertices
            if current < self.best {
                self.best = current; self.best_mask = curr_mask;
            }
            return;
        }
        // lower bound from maximal matching
        let lb = self.maximal_matching_lb(a);
        if current + lb >= self.best { return; }
        // choose vertex with max degree
        let mut best_v = None;
        let mut best_deg = 0usize;
        let mut tmp = a;
        while tmp != 0 {
            let v = tmp.trailing_zeros() as usize;
            tmp &= tmp - 1;
            let deg = ExactSmall::popcount(self.adj_mask[v] & a);
            if deg > best_deg { best_deg = deg; best_v = Some(v); }
        }
        if best_v.is_none() {
            if current < self.best { self.best = current; self.best_mask = curr_mask; }
            return;
        }
        let v = best_v.unwrap();
        // Branch 1: include v
        self.dfs(a & !(1u64<<v), current + 1, curr_mask | (1u64<<v));
        // Branch 2: exclude v -> include all neighbors
        let neighs = self.adj_mask[v] & a;
        let mut to_remove = neighs | (1u64<<v);
        let addcount = ExactSmall::popcount(neighs);
        if current + addcount < self.best {
            self.dfs(a & !to_remove, current + addcount, curr_mask | neighs);
        }
    }
}

fn exact_small_solver(n: usize, adj: &Vec<Vec<usize>>, time_limit: Option<Instant>) -> Vec<usize> {
    // n <= 50
    let mut solver = ExactSmall::new(n, adj, time_limit);
    let full = if n == 64 { !0u64 } else { (1u64<<n) - 1 };
    solver.best = n + 1;
    solver.dfs(full, 0, 0u64);
    // decode best_mask
    let mut res = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..n {
        if (solver.best_mask & (1u64<<i)) != 0 { res.push(i); }
    }
    // In case solver didn't find (time cut), fallback to greedy
    if solver.best > n {
        // fallback
        let mut cover_bool = vec![false; n];
        let edges = (0..n).flat_map(|u| adj[u].iter().map(move |&v| (u,v))).collect::<Vec<_>>();
        let approx = approx_2(n, &edges, adj);
        for i in 0..n { if approx[i] { res.push(i); } }
    }
    res
}

fn main() {
    let global_start = Instant::now();
    let (n, _m, edges_raw) = read_input();
    let adj = build_adj(n, &edges_raw);
    let edges = {
        // build unique edge list with u<v
        let mut e2 = Vec::new();
        for (u, v) in edges_raw.iter() {
            let (a,b) = if u <= v { (*u,*v) } else { (*v,*u) };
            if a != b { e2.push((a,b)); }
        }
        e2.sort_unstable(); e2.dedup(); e2
    };

    if edges.is_empty() {
        println!("0\n"); return;
    }

    // global time limit
    let global_time_limit = Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(280); // a bit under 5 minutes

    // 1) if bipartite - solve exactly using matching
    if let Some(color) = bipartite_color(n, &adj) {
        let match_orig = hopcroft_karp(n, &adj, &color);
        let cover = konig_min_vertex_cover(n, &adj, &color, &match_orig);
        let mut cover_sorted = cover.clone(); cover_sorted.sort_unstable();
        println!("{}", cover_sorted.len());
        for (i, v) in cover_sorted.iter().enumerate() {
            if i == cover_sorted.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); }
            else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // adapt based on size
    if n <= 50 {
        let time_limit = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(30)));
        let res = exact_small_solver(n, &adj, time_limit);
        let mut cover = vec![false; n]; for v in res { cover[v] = true; }
        // ensure coverage
        if !validate_cover(n, &edges, &cover) {
            // fallback approx
            let approx = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
            let mut out = Vec::new();
            for i in 0..n { if approx[i] { out.push(i); } }
            println!("{}", out.len());
            for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
                if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
            }
            return;
        }
        let mut out = Vec::new(); for i in 0..n { if cover[i] { out.push(i); } }
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // Kernelize degree-0 and degree-1 for medium/large
    let tl_kernel = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(20)));
    let (mut forced_orig, new_to_old, new_adj) = kernelize_deg0_deg1(n, adj.clone(), tl_kernel);
    let kernel_n = new_adj.len();
    // initialize cover with forced vertices
    let mut cover_bool = vec![false; n];
    for i in 0..n { if forced_orig[i] { cover_bool[i] = true; } }

    if kernel_n == 0 {
        // forced cover already covers all edges
        let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // If kernel small -> exact
    if kernel_n <= 50 {
        // build small adj for kernel mapping
        let mut small_adj = vec![Vec::new(); kernel_n];
        for i in 0..kernel_n { small_adj[i] = new_adj[i].clone(); }
        // solve exact on kernel
        let time_limit = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(40)));
        let kernel_sol = exact_small_solver(kernel_n, &small_adj, time_limit);
        for v in kernel_sol { let orig = new_to_old[v]; cover_bool[orig] = true; }
        // Output
        let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
        println!("{}", out.len());
        for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
            if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
        }
        return;
    }

    // Otherwise use approximation + local search
    // Build edges for kernel mapping to original indices
    let mut kernel_edges = Vec::new();
    for u in 0..kernel_n {
        for &v in &new_adj[u] { if u < v { kernel_edges.push((new_to_old[u], new_to_old[v])); } }
    }

    // Initial approx on whole graph: better to run approx on original edges
    let approx_cover = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
    for i in 0..n { if approx_cover[i] { cover_bool[i] = true; } }

    // Remove redundants
    let mut changed = true;
    let mut iter = 0;
    while changed && Instant::now() < global_time_limit && iter < 2000 {
        changed = remove_redundant(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool);
        // try local swaps that reduce size
        let tl_swaps = Some(std::cmp::min(global_time_limit, Instant::now() + Duration::from_millis(200)));
        if local_swaps(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool, tl_swaps) { changed = true; }
        iter += 1;
    }

    // Final fallback: if still not covering (should cover), fix by greedy
    if !validate_cover(n, &edges, &cover_bool) {
        let approx_cover2 = approx_2(n, &edges, &adj);
        cover_bool = approx_cover2;
        // cleanup
        remove_redundant(n, &adj, &mut cover_bool);
    }

    let out: Vec<usize> = (0..n).filter(|&v| cover_bool[v]).collect();
    println!("{}", out.len());
    for (i, v) in out.iter().enumerate() {
        if i==out.len()-1 { print!("{}\n", v); } else { print!("{} ", v); }
    }
}
Test 29: Maximum Clique (C#) Score: 0.00 / 18
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to solve the Maximum Clique problem.

You must write a C# solver that can handle ANY graph complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: Small sparse graphs (10-20 vertices), basic backtracking
- **Medium**: Moderate graphs (50-100 vertices), Bron-Kerbosch with pivoting
- **Large**: Dense graphs (200-500 vertices), advanced branch-and-bound
- **Extreme**: Very dense graphs (1000+ vertices), heuristics and approximation

**The Challenge:**
Your C# maximum clique finder will be tested with graphs ranging from sparse to very dense instances. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL graph complexities.

**Problem:**
Find the largest complete subgraph (clique) in an undirected graph. A clique is a set of vertices where every pair of vertices is connected by an edge. This is NP-Hard.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges
u v (for each edge, 0-indexed)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
clique_size
vertex_1 vertex_2 ... vertex_k (vertices in the maximum clique, space-separated)
```

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on graph size and density
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for very dense graphs
3. **Quality**: Find maximum or near-maximum cliques

**Algorithm Strategy Recommendations:**
- **Small sparse graphs (≤50 vertices)**: Can use basic backtracking algorithms
- **Medium graphs (50-200 vertices)**: Bron-Kerbosch algorithm with pivoting
- **Large dense graphs (200-1000 vertices)**: Advanced branch-and-bound with pruning
- **Very dense graphs (>1000 vertices)**: Fast heuristics, approximation algorithms

**Key Techniques:**
- **Bron-Kerbosch**: Recursive backtracking with pivot selection
- **Pivoting**: Choose pivot vertex to reduce recursive calls
- **Branch-and-bound**: Prune search space using upper bounds
- **Degeneracy ordering**: Process vertices in order of increasing degree
- **Heuristics**: Greedy algorithms for initial bounds

**Implementation Hints:**
- Detect graph complexity and choose appropriate algorithm
- Use efficient adjacency matrix for dense graphs, adjacency lists for sparse
- Implement adaptive quality vs speed tradeoffs
- For very dense graphs, focus on fast heuristics
- Handle edge cases: empty graphs, complete graphs, disconnected graphs
- Use fast I/O for large inputs

**Success Criteria:**
- All vertices in output form a complete subgraph
- No larger clique exists in the graph
- Output format is correct

**Failure Criteria:**
- Output vertices don't form a clique
- Larger clique exists
- Timeout without solution
- Invalid output format

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable graph sizes and densities
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying graph complexities efficiently

Write complete, compilable C# code with a Main method.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on graph complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

View chain of thought
Maximum Clique finds the largest complete subgraph in a graph. **Algorithms:** - **Bron-Kerbosch**: Classic backtracking with pivoting - **Branch and Bound**: Pruning with upper bounds - **Greedy**: Fast approximation, not optimal
AI Reasoning: This solver adapts to graph size and density by selecting different strategies: for very small graphs (<=60) it runs an exact Bron–Kerbosch style search using 64-bit bitmasks for maximal speed and exactness; for medium/large graphs (61..1000) it runs an advanced branch-and-bound (Tomita-style / coloring bound) with adjacency in compact bitsets; for very large graphs (>1000) it runs fast greedy and randomized local-search heuristics with multiple restarts and a short time budget. The code uses efficient bit operations (ulong words), greedy coloring for upper bounds, pivoting and pruning to reduce recursion, and time checks so it will return the best found solution within time limits. It handles edge cases like empty graphs and isolated vertices and prints a valid clique (exact when possible, near-optimal for huge instances).

View C# Code (14897 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Diagnostics;
using System.IO;
using System.Text;

class MaxCliqueSolver {
    static void Main(string[] args) {
        var sc = new FastScanner();
        int n = sc.NextInt();
        int m = sc.NextInt();
        if (n <= 0) {
            Console.WriteLine("0");
            Console.WriteLine();
            return;
        }

        // Build adjacency structure
        if (n <= 64) {
            // Use single-ulong adjacency
            ulong[] adj = new ulong[n];
            for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) {
                int u = sc.NextInt();
                int v = sc.NextInt();
                if (u == v) continue;
                adj[u] |= (1UL << v);
                adj[v] |= (1UL << u);
            }
            SolveSmall(n, adj);
        } else {
            int words = (n + 63) / 64;
            ulong[][] adj = new ulong[n][];
            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                adj[i] = new ulong[words];
            }
            for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) {
                int u = sc.NextInt();
                int v = sc.NextInt();
                if (u == v) continue;
                adj[u][v >> 6] |= 1UL << (v & 63);
                adj[v][u >> 6] |= 1UL << (u & 63);
            }
            if (n <= 1000) {
                SolveMediumLarge(n, adj);
            } else {
                SolveHuge(n, adj);
            }
        }
    }

    // -------------------- SMALL (n<=64) exact solver --------------------
    static void SolveSmall(int n, ulong[] adj) {
        int bestSize = 0;
        ulong bestMask = 0UL;
        Stopwatch sw = Stopwatch.StartNew();
        // degeneracy ordering might help but for n<=64 simple Tomita-like recursion

        void Expand(ulong R, ulong P) {
            if (sw.Elapsed.TotalSeconds > 280) return; // safety
            if (P == 0) {
                int rcount = PopCount(R);
                if (rcount > bestSize) {
                    bestSize = rcount; bestMask = R;
                }
                return;
            }
            // bound
            int pcount = PopCount(P);
            if (PopCount(R) + pcount <= bestSize) return;

            // choose pivot u from P union X (here just pick highest-degree in P)
            int pivot = -1; int maxDeg = -1;
            ulong tmp = P;
            while (tmp != 0) {
                int v = BitScanForward(tmp);
                tmp &= tmp - 1;
                int deg = PopCount(P & adj[v]);
                if (deg > maxDeg) { maxDeg = deg; pivot = v; }
            }
            // iterate vertices in P without neighbors of pivot
            ulong candidates = P & ~(pivot == -1 ? 0UL : adj[pivot]);
            while (candidates != 0) {
                int v = BitScanForward(candidates);
                candidates &= candidates - 1;
                ulong vbit = 1UL << v;
                Expand(R | vbit, P & adj[v]);
                P &= ~vbit;
                if (sw.Elapsed.TotalSeconds > 280) break;
            }
        }

        ulong all = (n == 64) ? ulong.MaxValue : ((1UL << n) - 1);
        Expand(0UL, all);

        // Output
        Console.WriteLine(bestSize);
        if (bestSize > 0) {
            StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) if (((bestMask >> i) & 1UL) != 0) {
                if (sb.Length > 0) sb.Append(' ');
                sb.Append(i);
            }
            Console.WriteLine(sb.ToString());
        } else Console.WriteLine();
    }

    // -------------------- MEDIUM/LARGE (n<=1000) Tomita-style with coloring --------------------
    static void SolveMediumLarge(int n, ulong[][] adj) {
        Stopwatch sw = Stopwatch.StartNew();
        int bestSize = 0;
        int[] bestClique = new int[0];

        // Initial heuristic: greedy to get good lower bound
        int[] order = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) order[i] = i;
        // sort by decreasing degree
        Array.Sort(order, (a, b) => -Degree(adj, a).CompareTo(Degree(adj, b)));
        var initial = GreedyCliqueFromOrder(n, adj, order);
        bestSize = initial.Count; bestClique = initial.ToArray();

        // Candidate list as int[] initially all vertices sorted by degeneracy (small to large) may help
        int[] vertices = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) vertices[i] = i;

        // We'll implement recursive expand using int[] candidate list and greedy coloring for bound
        List<int> R = new List<int>();

        void Expand(List<int> cand) {
            if (sw.Elapsed.TotalSeconds > 280) return;
            int candCount = cand.Count;
            if (candCount == 0) {
                if (R.Count > bestSize) {
                    bestSize = R.Count; bestClique = R.ToArray();
                }
                return;
            }
            // color greedy to get order and colors
            int[] colors = new int[candCount];
            int[] ordered = new int[candCount];
            GreedyColoring(cand, adj, ordered, colors);

            for (int i = candCount - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
                if (sw.Elapsed.TotalSeconds > 280) return;
                int v = ordered[i];
                int color = colors[i];
                if (R.Count + color <= bestSize) return; // prune
                R.Add(v);
                // build new candidate list = cand ∩ N(v)
                var newCand = new List<int>();
                var vAdj = adj[v];
                foreach (int u in cand) {
                    if (u == v) continue;
                    if (IsAdjacentArray(vAdj, u)) newCand.Add(u);
                }
                Expand(newCand);
                R.RemoveAt(R.Count - 1);
                // remove v from cand
                cand.Remove(v);
            }
        }

        var initialCand = new List<int>(n);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) initialCand.Add(i);

        Expand(initialCand);

        // Output best
        Console.WriteLine(bestSize);
        if (bestSize > 0) Console.WriteLine(string.Join(' ', bestClique)); else Console.WriteLine();
    }

    // -------------------- HUGE (>1000) heuristic solver --------------------
    static void SolveHuge(int n, ulong[][] adj) {
        Stopwatch sw = Stopwatch.StartNew();
        int bestSize = 0;
        List<int> bestClique = new List<int>();

        // Precompute degrees
        int[] deg = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) deg[i] = Degree(adj, i);

        // Several randomized greedy restarts within time budget
        double timeBudget = Math.Min(20.0, Math.Max(3.0, n / 100.0)); // seconds: scale with n
        long seed = DateTime.Now.Ticks;
        Random rng = new Random((int)(seed & 0x7fffffff));

        // base order: descending degree
        int[] baseOrder = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) baseOrder[i] = i;
        Array.Sort(baseOrder, (a, b) => deg[b].CompareTo(deg[a]));

        int iterations = 0;
        while (sw.Elapsed.TotalSeconds < timeBudget) {
            iterations++;
            // perturb order slightly
            int[] order = (int[])baseOrder.Clone();
            // perform a few random swaps
            int swaps = Math.Min(20, n/100 + 1);
            for (int s = 0; s < swaps; s++) {
                int i = rng.Next(n);
                int j = rng.Next(n);
                int tmp = order[i]; order[i] = order[j]; order[j] = tmp;
            }
            var clique = GreedyCliqueFromOrder(n, adj, order);
            if (clique.Count > bestSize) { bestSize = clique.Count; bestClique = clique; }

            // local improvement: try single-vertex swaps
            bool improved = true;
            while (improved && sw.Elapsed.TotalSeconds < timeBudget) {
                improved = false;
                // try to add vertices that are common neighbors
                var common = CommonNeighborsList(adj, clique);
                foreach (int v in common) {
                    if (IsCliqueExtendable(clique, v, adj)) {
                        clique.Add(v); improved = true;
                        if (clique.Count > bestSize) { bestSize = clique.Count; bestClique = new List<int>(clique); }
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (improved) continue;
                // try swap: replace one vertex with another to allow expansion
                bool swapped = false;
                for (int outside = 0; outside < n && !swapped && sw.Elapsed.TotalSeconds < timeBudget; outside++) {
                    int v = outside;
                    if (clique.Contains(v)) continue;
                    // count neighbors within clique
                    int cnt = 0; int nonNeighborIndex = -1;
                    foreach (int u in clique) {
                        if (IsAdjacentArray(adj[v], u)) cnt++; else nonNeighborIndex = u;
                    }
                    if (cnt == clique.Count - 1 && nonNeighborIndex != -1) {
                        // swap: remove nonNeighborIndex, add v
                        clique.Remove(nonNeighborIndex);
                        clique.Add(v);
                        swapped = true; improved = true;
                        if (clique.Count > bestSize) { bestSize = clique.Count; bestClique = new List<int>(clique); }
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (swapped) continue;
            }
            // quick time-out break
            if (iterations > 100000) break;
        }

        // Fallback: if nothing found => single vertex
        if (bestSize == 0) { bestSize = 1; bestClique = new List<int>() { 0 }; }

        Console.WriteLine(bestSize);
        Console.WriteLine(string.Join(' ', bestClique));
    }

    // -------------------- Utilities --------------------
    static int PopCount(ulong x) {
        return System.Numerics.BitOperations.PopCount(x);
    }
    static int BitScanForward(ulong x) {
        return BitOperationsTrailingZeroes(x);
    }
    static int BitOperationsTrailingZeroes(ulong x) {
        if (x == 0) return -1;
        return System.Numerics.BitOperations.TrailingZeroCount(x);
    }

    static int Degree(ulong[][] adj, int v) {
        int deg = 0;
        foreach (ulong w in adj[v]) deg += System.Numerics.BitOperations.PopCount(w);
        return deg;
    }
    static int Degree(ulong[] adjRow, int v) {
        // not used
        int deg = 0;
        foreach (ulong w in adjRow) deg += System.Numerics.BitOperations.PopCount(w);
        return deg;
    }
    static bool IsAdjacentArray(ulong[] row, int to) {
        return ((row[to >> 6] >> (to & 63)) & 1UL) != 0UL;
    }

    // Greedy coloring on candidate list (for Tomita bound)
    static void GreedyColoring(List<int> cand, ulong[][] adj, int[] ordered, int[] colors) {
        int m = cand.Count;
        // We'll assign colors in a simple greedy way and produce an ordering
        // We process vertices in decreasing degree within cand
        int[] deg = new int[m];
        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) deg[i] = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) {
            int v = cand[i];
            int d = 0;
            var row = adj[v];
            for (int j = 0; j < m; j++) if (i != j) {
                if (IsAdjacentArray(row, cand[j])) d++;
            }
            deg[i] = d;
        }
        int[] idx = new int[m];
        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) idx[i] = i;
        Array.Sort(idx, (a,b)=> deg[b].CompareTo(deg[a]));

        // color classes as temporary lists
        List<List<int>> colorClasses = new List<List<int>>();
        int[] colorOf = new int[m];
        for (int k = 0; k < m; k++) colorOf[k] = -1;

        for (int ii = 0; ii < m; ii++) {
            int i = idx[ii];
            int v = cand[i];
            bool placed = false;
            for (int c = 0; c < colorClasses.Count; c++) {
                bool ok = true;
                foreach (int jIndex in colorClasses[c]) {
                    int u = cand[jIndex];
                    if (!IsAdjacentArray(adj[v], u)) { ok = false; break; }
                }
                if (ok) { colorClasses[c].Add(i); colorOf[i] = c; placed = true; break; }
            }
            if (!placed) { var list = new List<int>(); list.Add(i); colorClasses.Add(list); colorOf[i] = colorClasses.Count - 1; }
        }
        // Now build ordered and colors array: order vertices by color then some order
        int pos = 0;
        for (int c = 0; c < colorClasses.Count; c++) {
            foreach (int idxInCand in colorClasses[c]) {
                ordered[pos] = cand[idxInCand];
                colors[pos] = colorClasses.Count - c; // use reverse color as upper bound heuristic
                pos++;
            }
        }
        // If some leftover not filled (shouldn't happen), fill
        for (int i = pos; i < m; i++) { ordered[i] = cand[i]; colors[i] = 1; }
    }

    static List<int> GreedyCliqueFromOrder(int n, ulong[][] adj, int[] order) {
        List<int> clique = new List<int>();
        bool[] inClique = new bool[n];
        foreach (int v in order) {
            bool ok = true;
            foreach (int u in clique) if (!IsAdjacentArray(adj[v], u)) { ok = false; break; }
            if (ok) { clique.Add(v); inClique[v] = true; }
        }
        return clique;
    }

    static List<int> CommonNeighborsList(ulong[][] adj, List<int> clique) {
        int n = adj.Length;
        bool[] mark = new bool[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) mark[i] = true;
        foreach (int v in clique) {
            var row = adj[v];
            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                if (mark[i]) {
                    if (!IsAdjacentArray(row, i)) mark[i] = false;
                }
            }
        }
        var list = new List<int>();
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) if (mark[i]) list.Add(i);
        // remove clique vertices
        foreach (int v in clique) list.Remove(v);
        return list;
    }

    static bool IsCliqueExtendable(List<int> clique, int v, ulong[][] adj) {
        foreach (int u in clique) if (!IsAdjacentArray(adj[v], u)) return false;
        return true;
    }
}

// Fast scanner for large input
class FastScanner {
    private readonly Stream stream;
    private readonly byte[] buffer = new byte[1 << 16];
    private int ptr = 0, len = 0;
    public FastScanner() { stream = Console.OpenStandardInput(); }
    private byte Read() {
        if (ptr >= len) { len = stream.Read(buffer, 0, buffer.Length); ptr = 0; if (len == 0) return 0; }
        return buffer[ptr++];
    }
    public int NextInt() {
        int ret = 0; byte b; do { b = Read(); if (b == 0) return 0; } while (b <= 32);
        bool neg = false; if (b == '-') { neg = true; b = Read(); }
        while (b > 32) { ret = ret * 10 + b - '0'; b = Read(); }
        return neg ? -ret : ret;
    }
}
